previous_plan.title: MindBridge Resource Hub: Static Site Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static site with Contentful CMS, Pagefind search, and CloudFront CDN for a national mental health non-profit resource hub.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static generation at build time from Contentful CMS; deployed to S3 + CloudFront. No server-side runtime.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS via CloudFront ACM. CSP headers mentioned but directives not specified. GA4 with IP anonymization.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions deploys to S3 and CloudFront. Cache invalidation strategy not defined. Contentful webhook triggers rebuild.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve two medium-severity gaps: the CloudFront cache TTL and invalidation strategy (ISS-001), and the CSP directive specification for Astro React island hydration (ISS-002). The overall static architecture is correct and does not require structural changes. The invalidation fix is operationally critical given the crisis hotline use case where stale phone numbers could cause real harm.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined two-tier cache strategy in deployment section: HTML pages served with Cache-Control max-age=0, s-maxage=86400 allowing CloudFront to cache for 24 hours while browsers revalidate immediately. Immutable static assets (JS/CSS/images with content-hash filenames) served with max-age=31536000, immutable. On each deployment, GitHub Actions runs CloudFront create-invalidation --paths '/*' to flush HTML pages immediately. Crisis hotline pages additionally get surrogate-key-based invalidation via Contentful webhook path targeting.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Content editors see new crisis hotline numbers and articles live on CloudFront within 60 seconds of CI deployment completion; long-lived assets cached at edges for up to 1 year

fix_report.action_taken: Specified CloudFront response headers policy with explicit CSP directives: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' (Astro hydration bundles use content-hash filenames eliminating need for inline scripts or nonces); style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (Astro CSS-in-JS requires inline styles); img-src 'self' data: https://images.ctfassets.net; connect-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com; frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Frame-Options DENY. X-Content-Type-Options nosniff. Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP blocks XSS injection while allowing Astro hydration bundles and Contentful image CDN; inline script injection from third-party content is blocked

target_output.title: MindBridge Resource Hub: Static Site Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-server-runtime static website for a national mental health non-profit, built with Astro 4 and React islands, deployed to AWS S3 and CloudFront. Content editors publish articles, crisis hotline listings, and CBT worksheets via Contentful CMS; a GitHub Actions pipeline rebuilds and deploys the full site in under 10 minutes and invalidates CloudFront edge caches on every publish. Pagefind provides client-side full-text search served entirely from CDN. CloudFront handles awareness campaign traffic spikes with no origin scaling required. GA4 with IP anonymization provides analytics with GDPR-compliant cookie consent.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generator pulls all content from Contentful Content Delivery API at build time: articles, resource_categories, crisis_hotlines, worksheets, and awareness_campaigns collections. The build output is a directory of pre-rendered HTML files, Pagefind search index shards, and content-hashed static assets. GitHub Actions deploys the output to an S3 bucket and runs a CloudFront /* invalidation to flush HTML page caches within 60 seconds of deployment. CloudFront serves all traffic from 400+ edge locations; the S3 origin receives requests only on cache misses. Two-tier cache TTL: HTML pages at s-maxage=86400 with browser revalidation; JS/CSS/image assets with content-hash filenames at max-age=31536000 immutable. React islands hydrate client-side for interactive search filtering and hotline directory filtering; remaining pages are zero-JavaScript static HTML. No server-side runtime exists in any environment.
target_output.technology_stack: Static generator: Astro 4 with @astrojs/react and @astrojs/contentful integrations, TypeScript. UI components: React 18 islands for Pagefind search widget and hotline directory filter. Styling: Tailwind CSS. Search: Pagefind built against Astro output directory. CMS: Contentful headless CMS (Content Delivery API at build time, Contentful webhook for rebuild triggers). CDN: AWS CloudFront with custom response headers policy. Origin storage: AWS S3 with versioned deployment prefixes. DNS + TLS: Route53 alias, ACM certificate. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Bun as package manager. Analytics: GA4 with anonymize_ip, Cookiebot for GDPR cookie consent.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Article Library: Astro static pages per article slug with category and tag filtering via React island; articles fetched from Contentful at build time. Pagefind Search: client-side full-text search across all articles and worksheets with result snippets; index served as static files from CloudFront. Crisis Hotline Directory: filterable by geographic_coverage and language; hotline data from Contentful crisis_hotlines content type; phone and text numbers displayed as tel: links. Worksheet Downloads: static pages per worksheet with PDF download link pointing to Contentful-hosted asset CDN URL. Awareness Campaign Sections: hero sections injected per page via awareness_campaigns Contentful entries during build; styled campaign banners link to featured article collections.
target_output.system_components: Contentful CMS: editorial workflow for articles, crisis_hotlines, worksheets, awareness_campaigns; webhook fires on entry publish. GitHub Actions CI: Bun install + Astro build + Pagefind index generation + S3 sync + CloudFront invalidation. S3 bucket: versioned deployment prefixes (deploy-{git-sha}/); prior prefix retained for one-command rollback. CloudFront distribution: S3 origin with custom response headers policy (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options), 400+ edge locations. Route53 + ACM: DNS and TLS termination. GA4 + Cookiebot: analytics with consent management.
target_output.workflows: Content Publish Workflow: Editor updates crisis hotline phone number in Contentful and clicks Publish -> Contentful webhook fires HTTP POST to GitHub Actions repository_dispatch endpoint -> GitHub Actions workflow starts: bun install, astro build (Contentful API pulls updated crisis_hotlines), pagefind --source dist, aws s3 sync dist/ s3://bucket/deploy-{sha}/ -> CloudFront create-invalidation --paths '/*' -> edge caches flushed within 60 seconds -> users worldwide see updated hotline number. Total time from editor publish to live: under 10 minutes. Campaign Traffic Spike Workflow: World Mental Health Day triggers 50x normal traffic -> all requests hit CloudFront edge locations; article HTML and Pagefind index shards served from edge cache (cache hit rate target 95%) -> S3 origin receives only cache misses for newly published content -> no origin auto-scaling required; CloudFront absorbs spike at CDN layer.
target_output.data_model: All data managed in Contentful at editorial time; no runtime database. articles (Contentful content type: title TEXT, slug TEXT unique, body_richtext RichText, category_ref Reference->resource_categories, tags TEXT[], published_date DATE, seo_meta JSON, featured_image AssetRef). resource_categories (name TEXT, slug TEXT, icon_svg AssetRef, parent_category_ref Reference->resource_categories nullable). crisis_hotlines (organization_name TEXT, phone_number TEXT, text_number TEXT nullable, hours TEXT, geographic_coverage TEXT[], languages TEXT[], last_verified_date DATE). worksheets (title TEXT, description TEXT, pdf_asset AssetRef, cbt_technique_tag TEXT CHECK IN ('cognitive_restructuring','behavioral_activation','exposure','mindfulness','safety_planning'), difficulty_level TEXT CHECK IN ('introductory','intermediate','advanced')). awareness_campaigns (campaign_name TEXT, hero_headline TEXT, hero_subtext TEXT, featured_article_refs Reference[], campaign_start DATE, campaign_end DATE). Pagefind indexes article titles, body text excerpts, and worksheet titles at build time.
target_output.api_design: No runtime API endpoints. Build-time data access: Contentful Content Delivery API queried via @astrojs/contentful during astro build. Contentful webhook payload (entry.publish event) delivered via HTTPS POST to GitHub Actions repository_dispatch; payload includes content_type and entry_id for targeted invalidation logging. GA4 Measurement Protocol used for server-side event backup on worksheet downloads (PDF served from Contentful CDN bypasses gtag.js). No client-facing API routes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced: CloudFront with ACM TLS certificate, HSTS header (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload) via response headers policy. Content Security Policy directives: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' (content-hash filenames eliminate inline script need); style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (Astro CSS-in-JS); img-src 'self' data: https://images.ctfassets.net; connect-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com https://region1.google-analytics.com; frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Frame-Options DENY. X-Content-Type-Options nosniff. S3 bucket policy: public read restricted to /public/* paths; Contentful API key never embedded in static output (build-time only via GitHub Actions secret). GDPR: GA4 configured with anonymize_ip: true, no User-ID; Cookiebot consent banner blocks GA4 until user accepts analytics cookies. Cookie consent banner meets ePrivacy Directive requirements for EU visitors.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI workflow (triggered by push to main or Contentful repository_dispatch): Step 1: bun install with frozen lockfile. Step 2: astro build with CONTENTFUL_ACCESS_TOKEN from repository secret. Step 3: pagefind --source dist to generate search index. Step 4: aws s3 sync dist/ s3://{bucket}/deploy-{GITHUB_SHA}/ --delete --cache-control for HTML pages (max-age=0, s-maxage=86400) and immutable assets (max-age=31536000). Step 5: CloudFront create-invalidation --distribution-id {id} --paths '/*' to flush HTML edge caches. Step 6: update S3 bucket website configuration origin prefix to new deploy-{GITHUB_SHA}. Rollback: update S3 origin prefix to prior deploy-{sha} and run invalidation. Build time target: under 10 minutes. CloudFront cache behavior: HTML pages cache 24 hours at edge with immediate browser revalidation; content-hashed assets cached 1 year immutable.
target_output.observability: CloudFront access logs delivered to S3 bucket with daily partitioning; Athena table for ad-hoc query (page path, status code, cache hit/miss, bytes transferred). CloudWatch alarms: 5xxErrorRate >0.5% for 5 minutes (SNS -> engineering Slack), CacheHitRate <85% sustained 15 minutes (indicates invalidation issue or cold CDN). GA4 real-time dashboard monitored during awareness campaigns for concurrent active users, article engagement rate, hotline click events, and worksheet download events. Build pipeline failure notifications via GitHub Actions Slack integration.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: S3 storage: ~$2/month for 5GB static assets with versioned prefixes. CloudFront data transfer: ~$15/month at 100GB/month normal traffic; awareness campaign spike to 1TB estimated ~$85 one-time. CloudFront requests: ~$1/million. GitHub Actions: free tier covers ~30 minutes/day of CI. Contentful: free tier supports up to 25,000 API calls/month (adequate for build frequency). Total estimated: under $25/month normal operations; under $120/month during major campaign month. Linear scaling with no origin compute costs.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Astro 4 project scaffold with Contentful integration, article library static pages, resource_categories taxonomy pages, crisis_hotlines directory with React island filter, Pagefind search integration, GA4 + Cookiebot, GitHub Actions CI/CD with S3 sync and CloudFront invalidation, CSP response headers policy, HTTPS + HSTS. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Worksheet download pages with CBT technique tagging, awareness_campaign hero sections, worksheet download GA4 event tracking, CloudWatch alarms for 5xx rate and cache hit rate. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multilingual content via Contentful locale support, crisis chat widget integration, resource recommendation cross-links between articles and worksheets.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Astro config. React islands must be explicitly marked with client:load or client:visible directives; no accidental full-page hydration. Contentful content types defined as TypeScript interfaces in /src/types/contentful.ts generated via contentful-typescript-codegen. All build-time Contentful API calls wrapped in error handling that fails the build loudly on missing required content fields. Pagefind index size monitored in CI; alert if index exceeds 2MB (impacts search load time on mobile). CSP directives validated in staging deployment before production release via web security scanner.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful API outage during CI build blocks content updates. Mitigation: prior CloudFront deployment remains live; engineers can manually trigger rebuild when Contentful recovers. Risk: Pagefind index size grows beyond 2MB as article library expands to 1000+ entries, impacting mobile search load time. Mitigation: Pagefind supports chunked index loading; configure max-index-chunk-size. Tradeoff: /* CloudFront invalidation flushes all edges on every deployment rather than path-targeted invalidation; simpler to operate but burns all edge cache warming. For content volume under 500 pages this is acceptable; at 1000+ pages consider surrogate-key invalidation via Contentful webhook entry IDs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Pagefind chosen over Algolia DocSearch for zero-cost client-side search with no external API dependency. CloudFront /* invalidation chosen over path-targeted for operational simplicity at current content volume. Cookiebot chosen for GDPR consent management over custom implementation. S3 versioned deployment prefixes enable one-command rollback without re-running CI.

metadata.notes: Mental health non-profit static site; round 2 defines CloudFront cache TTL/invalidation strategy and CSP directives for Astro React island hydration.

profile.domain: HIPAA-compliant desktop application for private-practice therapists to manage session notes, treatment plans, and progress documentation with encrypted cloud backup

project_goal.value: HIPAA-compliant desktop application for private-practice therapists to manage session notes, treatment plans, and progress documentation, replacing paper-based workflows, with encrypted cloud backup syncing to AWS S3 under therapist credentials
project_goal.rationale: Therapists need structured digital documentation with cloud backup for disaster recovery while maintaining HIPAA compliance

target_users.value: Licensed private-practice therapists (solo and small group practices) managing client caseloads
target_users.rationale: Primary users are individual therapists; multi-therapist group practice support is a future scope item

project_class.rationale: Tauri desktop application with local-first storage and optional cloud backup; no hosted multi-tenant backend

capabilities.rationale: Svelte UI, Rust embedded backend, SQLCipher local DB, JWT biometric auth, and Tauri bundler CI/CD

complexity_level.rationale: Local-first with cloud sync adds complexity over pure local storage; Rust Axum embedded server for structured business logic

risk_level.rationale: Stores highly sensitive psychiatric session notes and treatment plans; HIPAA violation risk if PHI escapes unencrypted

data_sensitivity.rationale: Session notes and treatment plans are PHI under HIPAA; client diagnoses and medication references are sensitive health data

external_exposure.rationale: Cloud backup to S3 requires therapist authentication; access restricted to the individual therapist's credentials

access_model.value: Single-therapist local authentication via custom JWT with biometric unlock via OS keychain (TouchID/Windows Hello); cloud backup authenticated via AWS IAM user credentials stored in OS keychain
access_model.rationale: Biometric unlock provides strong local access control; dedicated IAM credentials for S3 backup scoped to single bucket

feature_scope.value: Client intake forms, SOAP session notes with structured sections, treatment plan authoring with goals and progress tracking, appointment scheduling, encrypted cloud backup and restore, de-identified compliance audit report generation
feature_scope.rationale: Core documentation workflow replacing paper charts with digital structured notes

mvp_scope.value: Client intake forms, SOAP session notes, treatment plan creation, local SQLCipher storage, biometric authentication, encrypted S3 backup
mvp_scope.rationale: Core documentation and backup features needed before adding appointment scheduling and compliance reporting

security_baseline.value: SQLCipher AES-256 encryption for local database, TLS 1.3 for S3 backup transfers, biometric authentication, Sentry crash reports must contain no PHI (no session_notes content or client identifiers)
security_baseline.rationale: Local encryption protects device theft; TLS protects backup in transit; Sentry PHI scrubbing prevents cloud leakage via crash telemetry

frontend_stack.value: Tauri + Svelte with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides smaller bundle than Electron with Rust backend integration; Svelte for reactive UI without virtual DOM overhead

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum (embedded local server accessible via Tauri IPC)
backend_stack.rationale: Rust for memory safety in security-sensitive local server; Axum for typed async HTTP handlers bridged via Tauri commands

data_platform.value: SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher; encryption key derived from biometric-unlocked OS keychain secret
data_platform.rationale: SQLCipher provides transparent AES-256 page-level encryption without application-level field encryption overhead

hosting_target.value: Desktop-packaged via Tauri bundler for macOS and Windows; cloud backup to AWS S3 with server-side encryption (SSE-KMS)
hosting_target.rationale: Tauri produces native installers; S3 with SSE-KMS provides HIPAA-eligible cloud backup storage

privacy_retention_policy.value: Client session notes retained 7 years per state licensing board requirements; therapist controls deletion; cloud backup snapshots retained 90 days with automatic cleanup; Sentry crash data retained 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State licensing requirements mandate 7-year retention of therapy records; 90-day backup window for point-in-time recovery

future_scope.value: Multi-therapist group practice support, insurance billing integration, telehealth session linking
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP expansion into practice management features

constraints.value: App must function fully offline; cloud backup is optional and async; Sentry must not include session_note content or client name/DOB in crash payloads; BAA required with AWS for S3 backup
constraints.rationale: Offline-first ensures therapists in low-connectivity offices can document sessions; BAA with AWS covers HIPAA S3 usage

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting with PHI scrubbing; local audit log of all data access and backup operations; no remote APM
observability_baseline.rationale: Desktop app observability limited to crash reporting and local audit; remote APM would require outbound connections

execution_preference.value: Iterative releases with semantic versioning; Tauri updater for auto-updates from GitHub Releases
execution_preference.rationale: Auto-update via Tauri updater ensures therapists receive security patches without manual download

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH compliance; BAA required with AWS for S3 cloud backup; data at rest encrypted AES-256 via SQLCipher; data in transit encrypted TLS 1.3; no PHI in Sentry payloads; 7-year record retention per state licensing
compliance_context.rationale: Private-practice therapist storing PHI requires HIPAA compliance; cloud storage requires BAA with AWS

project.goal: HIPAA-compliant desktop therapy documentation app replacing paper charts with encrypted cloud backup
project.target_users: Licensed private-practice therapists
project.timeline: Q3 MVP; Q4 for audit reporting and appointment scheduling

frontend.state: Svelte stores for session state and form drafts

backend.api: Tauri IPC commands; no network server

security.auth: Biometric unlock via OS keychain; JWT session token in memory
security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 for local DB; TLS 1.3 for S3 backup
security.compliance: HIPAA BAA with AWS; Sentry PHI scrubbing

devops.packaging: Tauri bundler for macOS .dmg and Windows .msi

open_questions.backup_conflict: How to handle backup restore conflicts if therapist uses multiple devices

reasoner_reviews.product: The local-first offline-capable design is correct for the solo private-practice therapist context. SOAP note structured sections (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan) are the standard clinical documentation format and should be explicitly modeled, not treated as free-text blobs. Round 2 improved the backup conflict handling but the de-identified audit report generation for compliance reviews is still thin in the implementation detail.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SQLCipher page-level encryption is the right choice over application-level field encryption for a local DB - it eliminates per-field encryption overhead while covering all data. Rounds 1 and 2 fixed the SQLCipher key derivation and backup encryption details. Round 3 should focus on the audit report generation workflow which has remained underspecified across two rounds.
reasoner_reviews.security: Sentry PHI scrubbing has been defined in round 2. The cloud backup IAM credential scoping (single-bucket read/write, no list-buckets) was added in round 2. The remaining gap is the de-identified compliance audit report: the report must use de-identification before any export to ensure no client PII appears in compliance documents shared with licensing boards.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The backup restore conflict resolution for multi-device use is an open question but explicitly deferred to future scope. The 90-day S3 backup snapshot retention with lifecycle cleanup should be implemented as an S3 Lifecycle policy, not application logic, to ensure retention compliance even if the app is not running.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri app with Svelte SPA in webview; Rust backend accessible via Tauri invoke() commands. Svelte stores for active client context, session note draft, and authentication state.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Unlock screen (biometric or PIN fallback), Client List with search, Client Profile (intake form, session history timeline, treatment plan), SOAP Note editor (four structured text areas: Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan with auto-save), Treatment Plan editor (goals with target_date and status), Cloud Backup status panel
frontend.state_management: Svelte writable stores: authStore (session JWT, biometric state), activeClientStore (current client context), noteDraftStore (auto-save draft for active SOAP note). All stores cleared on app lock.
frontend.frontend_risks: Auto-save must not write plaintext drafts to OS temp files; note drafts must be held in memory only or written via Rust backend through SQLCipher

backend.service_design: Rust Axum handlers exposed as Tauri commands: create_client, get_client, create_soap_note, update_soap_note, create_treatment_plan, update_goal_progress, trigger_backup, restore_from_backup
backend.business_modules: SoapNoteService validates SOAP structure (all four sections non-empty before finalization), TreatmentPlanService tracks goal progress with status enum (not_started/in_progress/achieved/discontinued), BackupService serializes SQLCipher DB file to encrypted tar archive and uploads to S3 via AWS SDK for Rust
backend.failure_handling: Backup failures logged to local audit_operations log and surfaced in UI status panel; app functions normally offline if backup fails. SQLCipher write errors surface as typed error responses to Svelte frontend.

security.auth_design: Biometric authentication via OS keychain (LocalAuthentication on macOS, Windows Hello on Windows) unlocks the SQLCipher database key stored as a keychain item. JWT session token generated in Rust on successful biometric auth; held in memory, cleared on app lock or 30-minute inactivity timeout.
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher DB key stored in OS keychain under app-specific service name; never written to disk outside keychain. AWS IAM access key for S3 backup stored in OS keychain; scoped to single S3 bucket with PutObject and GetObject permissions only.
security.privacy_controls: Sentry beforeSend hook in Tauri webview scrubs: removes any breadcrumb with message matching /session|note|client|diagnosis|treatment/, strips event.contexts.session, removes exception parameters matching /soap|subjective|objective|assessment|plan|client_id/. Only Rust panic messages and Tauri framework errors transmitted.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Local audit_operations table in SQLCipher DB: records every Tauri command invocation with command_name, client_id (hashed), timestamp, success/failure. Backup and restore operations logged with S3 object key (not content). Audit table never included in Sentry payloads.

data.entities: clients (id UUID PK, first_name_encrypted TEXT, last_name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, intake_date DATE, diagnosis_codes TEXT[], assigned_therapist_id TEXT, created_at). session_notes (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, note_date DATE, subjective_encrypted TEXT, objective_encrypted TEXT, assessment_encrypted TEXT, plan_encrypted TEXT, status ENUM draft/finalized, finalized_at DATETIME nullable). treatment_plans (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, created_at DATETIME, status ENUM active/completed/discontinued). progress_goals (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, goal_text_encrypted TEXT, target_date DATE, status ENUM not_started/in_progress/achieved/discontinued, last_updated DATETIME). appointment_slots (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, slot_datetime DATETIME, duration_minutes INTEGER, notes_encrypted TEXT). backup_snapshots (id UUID PK, snapshot_s3_key TEXT, snapshot_size_bytes INTEGER, created_at DATETIME, restored_at DATETIME nullable).
data.storage_design: Single SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite file in OS app data directory. Page-level AES-256 encryption transparent to application layer. WAL journal mode for crash resilience. All PHI columns in plaintext within SQLCipher (encryption is at storage layer, not column layer).
data.retention_and_deletion: Client records retained 7 years from last session date. App provides Delete Client workflow that removes all related session_notes, treatment_plans, progress_goals from SQLCipher and triggers S3 backup to propagate deletion. S3 Lifecycle policy: delete backup_snapshots older than 90 days automatically.

devops.deployment_topology: Tauri bundler produces: macOS .dmg (signed with Apple Developer ID, notarized via notarytool), Windows .msi (signed with EV code signing certificate). GitHub Releases hosts installers. Tauri updater checks GitHub Releases endpoint on app launch.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix build: macOS runner for .dmg (code sign + notarize), Windows runner for .msi (signtool). Steps: cargo test, npm run build (Svelte), tauri build, sign, upload to GitHub Release. Sentry source maps uploaded in CI for Rust and Svelte symbol resolution.
devops.rollback_strategy: Prior installer version available on GitHub Releases. Tauri updater supports downgrade via direct version URL. SQLCipher DB schema migrations use versioned up/down SQL files applied on startup.
