target_output.title: TherapistVault: HIPAA Desktop Documentation App v3
target_output.executive_summary: A HIPAA-compliant Tauri + Svelte desktop application for private-practice therapists to document client sessions, track treatment plans, and store encrypted records locally. SQLCipher provides AES-256 page-level encryption for the local SQLite database; the encryption key is stored in the OS keychain and unlocked by biometric authentication (TouchID or Windows Hello). Encrypted backup snapshots sync asynchronously to AWS S3 with SSE-KMS under a scoped IAM credential. De-identified compliance audit reports replace client PII with case_reference_ids for licensing board submission. Sentry crash reporting is configured with a PHI scrubbing hook. The app functions fully offline; backup is optional and async.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri desktop application with a Svelte SPA in the WebView and a Rust Axum server in the Tauri core process, accessible via Tauri invoke() commands. SQLCipher encrypts the entire SQLite database file at the page level using a 256-bit key stored in the OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager). On app launch, biometric authentication (LocalAuthentication on macOS, Windows Hello on Windows) unlocks the keychain item to retrieve the SQLCipher key; a Rust JWT session token is held in memory and cleared on 30-minute inactivity or explicit lock. BackupService serializes the SQLCipher DB file to an encrypted archive and uploads to AWS S3 with SSE-KMS via the aws-sdk-rust S3 client; IAM credentials scoped to PutObject/GetObject on the single backup bucket. AuditReportService generates de-identified compliance reports as PDFs using printpdf, replacing client names and DOBs with BLAKE3-derived case_reference_ids. Sentry crash reporting operates with a beforeSend PHI scrubbing hook. No network server is exposed; all backend logic runs in-process.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop runtime: Tauri 2.0 with Rust 1.78 core. Frontend: Svelte 5 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Svelte stores. Backend (Rust): Axum, SQLx with SQLCipher feature flag, aws-sdk-rust S3 client, printpdf for audit report generation, BLAKE3 for case_reference_id hashing, jsonwebtoken for in-memory session JWTs. Database: SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256 page encryption. Cloud backup: AWS S3 with SSE-KMS. Auth: OS keychain biometric via Tauri plugin-keychain. Crash reporting: Sentry Rust SDK + Sentry JavaScript SDK with coordinated beforeSend hooks. Packaging: Tauri bundler (macOS .dmg with code signing + notarization, Windows .msi with EV code signing). CI: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Client Intake: structured intake form capturing presenting concerns, referral source, insurance info, and diagnosis_codes (ICD-10 multi-select); stored encrypted in clients table. SOAP Session Notes: four structured text areas (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan) with auto-save draft mode; finalization locks note and sets finalized_at timestamp. Treatment Plan Management: goal authoring with target_date and status tracking (not_started, in_progress, achieved, discontinued); goal_completion_rate computed on plan dashboard. Cloud Backup: background BackupService polls for unsent changes, serializes SQLCipher file, uploads to S3 with SSE-KMS; backup status indicator in sidebar with last_backup_at timestamp. De-identified Audit Report: AuditReportService generates PDF with case_reference_id (BLAKE3 hash), session count, date range, finalized note count, and goal completion rate for licensing board submission.
target_output.system_components: Tauri core (Rust): Axum command router, SoapNoteService, TreatmentPlanService, BackupService (aws-sdk-rust), AuditReportService (printpdf, BLAKE3), AuthService (OS keychain, JWT), SQLCipher connection pool (SQLx). Tauri WebView (Svelte): authStore, activeClientStore, noteDraftStore, backup status component, SOAP editor component, treatment plan dashboard. SQLCipher database file: stored in OS app data directory, page-encrypted with AES-256 key from keychain. AWS S3 backup bucket: SSE-KMS server-side encryption, S3 Lifecycle rule deleting snapshots older than 90 days. GitHub Releases: Tauri installer artifacts. Tauri updater: checks GitHub Releases endpoint on launch for available updates.
target_output.workflows: Session Documentation Workflow: Therapist unlocks app via biometric -> OS keychain releases SQLCipher key -> Rust AuthService generates in-memory JWT -> Svelte frontend loads Client List -> therapist selects client and creates new session note -> SOAP editor auto-saves draft via invoke('update_soap_note_draft', {note_id, section, content}) every 30 seconds to SQLCipher (never to temp files) -> therapist completes note and clicks Finalize -> SoapNoteService validates all four sections non-empty, sets status=finalized, records finalized_at -> note locked from editing. Cloud Backup Workflow: BackupService runs on 15-minute timer in Rust background thread -> checks backup_snapshots for last_successful_backup_at -> if >4 hours since last backup: serializes SQLCipher DB file to in-memory buffer, compresses with zstd, uploads to S3 key therapy-vault/{therapist_id}/{timestamp}.db.zst with SSE-KMS -> writes backup_snapshots record with s3_key and size -> Svelte sidebar updates last backup timestamp. Audit Report Generation Workflow: Therapist opens Audit Report panel, selects date range and client -> invoke('generate_audit_report', {client_id, start_date, end_date}) -> AuditReportService fetches session_notes and progress_goals for range -> replaces client first_name, last_name, DOB with case_reference_id (BLAKE3(client_id_bytes)[0..4] as hex) -> computes total_session_count, finalized_note_count, goal_completion_rate -> printpdf renders PDF with TherapistVault header, de-identified client reference, session metrics table -> PDF saved to OS Downloads folder -> audit_report_requests record written with client_id_hash, date_range, report_filename, generated_at.
target_output.data_model: clients (id UUID PK, first_name_encrypted TEXT, last_name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, intake_date DATE, diagnosis_codes TEXT[], presenting_concerns_encrypted TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). session_notes (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, note_date DATE, subjective_encrypted TEXT, objective_encrypted TEXT, assessment_encrypted TEXT, plan_encrypted TEXT, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('draft','finalized')), finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). treatment_plans (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, presenting_problem_encrypted TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('active','completed','discontinued'))). progress_goals (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, goal_text_encrypted TEXT, target_date DATE, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('not_started','in_progress','achieved','discontinued')), last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). appointment_slots (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, slot_datetime TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_minutes INTEGER, notes_encrypted TEXT). backup_snapshots (id UUID PK, s3_key TEXT, size_bytes INTEGER, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, restored_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). audit_report_requests (id UUID PK, client_id_hash TEXT, report_date_start DATE, report_date_end DATE, report_filename TEXT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Local audit_operations (id INTEGER PK, command_name TEXT, client_id_hash TEXT, success BOOLEAN, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Tauri IPC commands (Rust #[tauri::command] functions, no network server): invoke('auth:unlock') -> {success, session_token}. invoke('auth:lock') -> void. invoke('clients:create', {intake_data}) -> {client_id}. invoke('clients:list') -> [{id, display_name_encrypted, intake_date}]. invoke('soap_notes:create', {client_id, note_date}) -> {note_id}. invoke('soap_notes:update_draft', {note_id, section, content_encrypted}) -> void. invoke('soap_notes:finalize', {note_id}) -> {finalized_at}. invoke('treatment_plans:create', {client_id, presenting_problem}) -> {plan_id}. invoke('progress_goals:update_status', {goal_id, status}) -> {last_updated}. invoke('backup:trigger') -> {snapshot_id, s3_key}. invoke('backup:restore', {snapshot_id}) -> {restored_at}. invoke('audit_report:generate', {client_id, start_date, end_date}) -> {pdf_path, case_reference_id, session_count}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256 page-level encryption: 256-bit key generated on first launch, stored in OS keychain (macOS Keychain under com.therapistvault.dbkey, Windows Credential Manager equivalent). Biometric unlock (LocalAuthentication / Windows Hello) retrieves keychain item; fallback to 6-digit PIN after 3 biometric failures. JWT session token generated by Rust AuthService on successful unlock; held in Tauri State<Mutex<Option<SessionToken>>>; cleared after 30-minute inactivity timer or explicit lock. S3 backup: aws-sdk-rust uploads with ServerSideEncryption::AwsKms; IAM credentials stored in OS keychain, scoped to: s3:PutObject, s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::{backup-bucket}/{therapist-id}/*. Sentry PHI scrubbing: Rust SDK with before_send closure removing fields matching /client|session|note|soap|plan|goal|diagnosis/; Svelte Sentry SDK with beforeSend hook removing breadcrumbs with categories matching same pattern. BAA with AWS in place for HIPAA S3 usage. HIPAA: no PHI transmitted to any third-party service except encrypted S3 with BAA. De-identified audit reports use BLAKE3 hash as case_reference_id; original client identity never included in report output.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI matrix: macOS runner: cargo test, bun run build, tauri build --target aarch64-apple-darwin + x86_64-apple-darwin (universal binary), sign with Apple Developer ID via imported certificate, notarize with notarytool, staple ticket. Windows runner: cargo test, bun run build, tauri build, sign .msi with EV certificate via signtool. Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Release draft with semantic version tag. Tauri updater endpoint configured as GitHub Releases latest API URL; app checks on launch and prompts therapist to install available updates. SQLCipher schema migrations: versioned .sql files in /migrations; applied via SQLx migrate! macro on DB connection open. S3 Lifecycle policy: delete backup objects with prefix therapy-vault/{therapist_id}/ after 90 days.
target_output.observability: Sentry Rust SDK for Tauri core panics and unhandled errors; Sentry JavaScript SDK for Svelte frontend exceptions; both configured with coordinated PHI scrubbing hooks removing client and session identifiers before transmission. Local audit_operations table in SQLCipher records every Tauri command invocation (command name, client_id_hash, success/failure, timestamp) for internal forensic review. Local app.log file (10MB with rotation) records: biometric auth events (success/failure, no credential detail), backup operation status (s3_key and size, no data content), audit report generation events (case_reference_id and date range). No remote metrics or APM.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost for app delivery; GitHub Releases serves installers at no cost. AWS S3 backup: at 50MB SQLCipher DB per therapist, 90-day retention with daily snapshots: ~4.5GB storage per therapist at $0.023/GB = ~$0.10/month per therapist. S3 PUT requests: ~$0.005/month. KMS key: $1/month. Tauri updater: GitHub CDN serves update manifests and installer downloads at no cost. Total per-therapist cloud cost: ~$1.10/month, primarily KMS key amortized across user base.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): biometric authentication with OS keychain SQLCipher key management, client intake forms, SOAP session note editor with auto-save, SQLCipher local database, Sentry PHI scrubbing, encrypted S3 backup with scoped IAM credentials, Tauri bundler CI for macOS and Windows with code signing and notarization. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Treatment plan creation with progress_goals tracking, goal completion rate dashboard, appointment_slots scheduling module, backup restore workflow with conflict warning. Phase 3 (4 weeks): De-identified compliance audit report generation (AuditReportService with BLAKE3 case_reference_id, printpdf output, audit_report_requests logging), S3 Lifecycle policy for 90-day retention, Tauri auto-updater integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust 1.78 stable with clippy lints enforced in CI (clippy::all, clippy::pedantic subset). All Tauri commands defined in typed command modules; never inline closures. SQLx compile-time query verification with sqlx::query_as! macros against test database. Svelte stores must be cleared on auth:lock command; enforced by unit test simulating lock and asserting store values are null. No PHI written to OS temp directories; SOAP note auto-save draft must use invoke() to SQLCipher, not Svelte writableStore persisted to disk. AuditReportService unit tested with synthetic client UUID to verify BLAKE3 hash output is consistent and client name does not appear in PDF output. cargo audit run in CI to check Rust dependency vulnerabilities.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OS keychain unavailable (corrupted keychain) makes SQLCipher key unrecoverable; all local data inaccessible. Mitigation: one-time printed recovery key stored in keychain as backup export option during initial setup. Risk: AWS IAM credential rotation breaks S3 backup silently. Mitigation: backup status UI shows last_backup_at; alert after 24-hour backup gap. Risk: biometric API differences between macOS LocalAuthentication and Windows Hello may require platform-specific Tauri plugin code paths. Mitigation: Tauri plugin-keychain abstracts platform differences; tested on both platforms in CI. Tradeoff: SQLCipher page-level encryption over application-level field encryption simplifies implementation at cost of granular access control (any holder of the DB key reads all data); acceptable for single-therapist local-only model.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: SQLCipher chosen over application-level AES encryption for operational simplicity and full-coverage protection. BLAKE3 selected for case_reference_id hashing for speed and collision resistance. printpdf Rust crate chosen for audit report PDF generation over headless browser rendering. Multi-device backup conflict resolution deferred to future multi-therapist group practice feature.

metadata.notes: HIPAA therapist desktop app; round 3 closes final ISS-003 gap with de-identified audit report generation using BLAKE3 case_reference_id and printpdf.

profile.domain: Longitudinal mental wellness tracking platform for college counseling centers aggregating student mood logs, academic stressor surveys, and sleep data to identify at-risk students

project_goal.value: Longitudinal mental wellness tracking platform for college counseling centers that aggregates daily student mood logs, academic stressor surveys, and sleep quality data to identify at-risk students and surface population-level trends to counseling staff
project_goal.rationale: College counseling centers need data-driven early intervention tools; students are chronically underserved due to counselor bandwidth limitations

target_users.value: College students submitting daily wellness check-ins; counseling staff reviewing individual and population-level dashboards; university administrators reviewing aggregate trend reports
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user roles with different access levels: students submit and view own data, counselors review assigned caseloads, admins see de-identified population trends

project_class.rationale: Mobile-optimized web app accessible via university SSO; no native app required for MVP

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Next.js UI, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL data layer, Shibboleth SAML auth, Grafana analytics, admin dashboard, and nightly risk scoring batch jobs

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-role access control, longitudinal data aggregation, nightly risk scoring pipeline, FERPA and HIPAA compliance layers, GKE Autopilot orchestration

risk_level.rationale: Student mental health data is sensitive health information; risk flags for suicide or self-harm have life-safety implications; FERPA and HIPAA apply

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student mood logs, sleep data, and counselor notes constitute mental health records protected under FERPA and potentially HIPAA

external_exposure.rationale: Access restricted to authenticated university community members via Shibboleth SAML SSO; no public endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC via Shibboleth SAML SSO: student (submit check-ins, view own history), counselor (view assigned student caseload with PHI), admin (de-identified population reports only); MFA enforced at university IdP level
access_model.rationale: FERPA requires access controls ensuring students can only see their own records; counselors limited to assigned caseloads; admins see only de-identified aggregates

feature_scope.value: Daily wellness check-in forms (mood, sleep, academic stressors), automated nightly risk scoring against threshold rules, counselor dashboard with individual student drilldown, population-level trend analytics, counselor notes on at-risk students, admin aggregate reporting, FERPA access audit logging
feature_scope.rationale: Complete lifecycle from student self-reporting to counselor intervention to institutional trend analysis

mvp_scope.value: Student check-in forms (mood, sleep, stressor survey), nightly batch risk scoring, counselor dashboard with risk flags and individual drilldown, FERPA audit logging
mvp_scope.rationale: Core risk identification and counselor notification workflow before adding population analytics and admin reporting

security_baseline.value: PHI encrypted at rest with GCP CMEK, TLS 1.3 in transit, Shibboleth SAML MFA enforced, FERPA audit log for all student record access, row-level security restricting counselors to assigned student caseloads
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA mandates access audit logs; row-level security prevents cross-caseload PHI access; CMEK provides university control over encryption keys

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for fast initial load on mobile-optimized check-in forms; TypeScript for type safety across student and counselor flows

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async for concurrent check-in submissions; Python for batch job risk scoring logic and data science libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with CMEK; Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore for session caching and batch job coordination
data_platform.rationale: Cloud SQL for managed PostgreSQL with university-controlled CMEK; Redis for session tokens and batch job distributed lock

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16; Cloud Memorystore Redis 7
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot for managed Kubernetes without node management; us-central1 for data residency; Cloud SQL HA for counseling center availability

privacy_retention_policy.value: Student wellness records retained 5 years after graduation per university data governance policy; FERPA access logs retained 7 years; de-identified aggregate data retained indefinitely for research; student can request deletion of non-counseling records
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: University data governance policy governs student record retention; research exemption allows de-identified data to persist

future_scope.value: Native mobile app, peer support network features, integration with academic SIS for academic performance correlation, crisis intervention workflow with counselor alert routing
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP expansion into proactive intervention and institutional integration

constraints.value: Must integrate with university Shibboleth SAML IdP; counselors must not access students outside their assigned caseload; admin dashboard must show only de-identified aggregates; GCP data residency in us-central1
constraints.rationale: FERPA caseload restriction is legally mandated; data residency required by university security policy

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards on GKE metrics; Prometheus for API latency and batch job duration; GCP Cloud Logging for audit events; PagerDuty for critical alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: University IT requires Grafana dashboards; Prometheus metrics align with GKE Autopilot native monitoring; audit events in Cloud Logging for FERPA compliance

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI/CD with 2-week sprint cadence; GKE rolling deployments
execution_preference.rationale: University engineering team uses GitLab; rolling deployments minimize downtime during academic year

compliance_context.value: FERPA for student education records; HIPAA considerations for counseling session data and mental health records; institutional data governance policies; FERPA audit trail mandatory for all student record access events; university Legal must approve de-identification methodology for admin aggregates
compliance_context.rationale: FERPA is the primary regulatory framework for student data; mental health counseling records may also trigger HIPAA coverage depending on institutional setup

project.goal: Mental wellness longitudinal tracking for college counseling centers
project.target_users: Students, counselors, university administrators
project.timeline: Q3 MVP; Q4 population analytics and admin reporting

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for student check-in form state

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1; batch jobs as Cloud Run Jobs or GKE CronJobs

security.auth: Shibboleth SAML 2.0 SSO via NextAuth.js; RBAC enforced in FastAPI middleware
security.compliance: FERPA audit log; row-level security for caseload restriction

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA with CMEK
data.cache: Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 for sessions and batch coordination

open_questions.risk_threshold: What numeric threshold on composite risk score triggers a counselor notification
open_questions.deidentification: What de-identification methodology satisfies both FERPA and university Legal review for admin aggregates

reasoner_reviews.product: The three-role model (student, counselor, admin) is correct and aligns with FERPA access requirements. The nightly batch risk scoring pipeline is appropriate for the early-intervention use case where counselors review flags the following morning. The student check-in form must be mobile-optimized and take under 3 minutes to complete to achieve daily completion rates needed for meaningful longitudinal data.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GKE Autopilot with FastAPI and PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL is well-suited for this workload profile: moderate concurrent check-in submissions during evening hours and batch processing at midnight. The nightly CronJob risk scoring pipeline should use a distributed lock via Redis to prevent duplicate scoring if the job is restarted. Row-level security on the students table in PostgreSQL is the right enforcement point for FERPA caseload restrictions rather than application-layer filtering.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA audit logging requires recording every access to a student wellness record with accessor identity, record type, and timestamp. The audit log must be immutable and retained 7 years. Row-level security policies on PostgreSQL counselors-to-students mapping must be tested with integration tests that assert a counselor cannot query records of students outside their assigned caseload. Admin de-identification methodology must be approved by university Legal before the admin dashboard ships.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The nightly risk scoring CronJob runs at midnight local time; if it processes 10,000 students with 90 days of check-in history each, it must complete within the 6-hour window before morning clinic opens. Batch query optimization (indexed queries on student_id and check_in_date) and async processing are critical to meet this window.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with server components for counselor dashboard data fetching (SSR with SAML session context) and client components for student check-in form (React state with auto-save draft)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Student: Daily Check-in form (mood slider 1-10, sleep quality 1-10, academic stressor multi-select, optional free-text reflection); Wellness History page (personal mood trend chart, 30-day calendar heatmap). Counselor: Assigned Student List with risk_flag badge; Student Drilldown (longitudinal mood chart, recent check-in detail, counselor note form). Admin: Population Dashboard (aggregate mood trend by week, stressor frequency distribution, at-risk student count by cohort - all de-identified).
frontend.state_management: React Query for counselor dashboard (students list, drilldown data, 60-second stale time). Zustand for student check-in form draft (auto-saved to localStorage for session resilience, cleared on submission).
frontend.performance_notes: Check-in form must be functional within 1 second on 3G mobile connection via static form shell with client hydration. Counselor dashboard population chart uses React Query prefetch on hover to eliminate loading delay on drilldown navigation.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: /check-ins (student submission and history), /risk-flags (counselor review queue), /students (counselor caseload access with RLS enforcement), /counselor-notes (note creation and retrieval), /analytics (admin aggregate endpoints), /audit (FERPA log access for compliance officers)
backend.business_modules: CheckInService validates mood/sleep/stressor schema and persists to mood_logs, sleep_records, stressor_surveys. RiskFlagService (batch): computes composite_risk_score from rolling 7-day mood average, sleep deficit, and high-severity stressor frequency; writes risk_flags with severity ENUM. CounselorNoteService persists counselor_notes with encrypted content. AggregateAnalyticsService de-identifies student data before computing population-level metrics for admin dashboard.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly CronJob (GKE CronJob, 00:00 UTC): acquires Redis distributed lock (key: risk-scoring-lock, TTL 6 hours), iterates active students in batches of 500, computes composite_risk_score, writes or updates risk_flags, releases lock. Job duration monitoring via Prometheus batch_job_duration_seconds metric. Alert if duration exceeds 5 hours.

security.auth_design: NextAuth.js SAML provider configured against university Shibboleth IdP. SAML attributes mapped to local user roles: eduPersonAffiliation=student -> student role, eduPersonEntitlement=counselor -> counselor role. FastAPI validates session JWT issued by NextAuth and checks role from JWT claims.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security: counselors table has assigned_student_ids[] array; RLS policy on students table restricts counselor SELECT to rows where id = ANY(current_setting('app.counselor_assigned_ids')::uuid[]). Admin role bypasses RLS but all admin queries route through AggregateAnalyticsService which applies k-anonymity (minimum group size 10) before returning data.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable ferpa_audit_log table: accessor_id UUID, student_id UUID, resource_type TEXT, action TEXT, ip_address INET, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Populated on every GET to /students/:id, /check-ins/:student_id, /counselor-notes/:student_id. Audit records streamed to GCP Cloud Logging with 7-year retention via Log Sink to GCS.
security.privacy_controls: Admin aggregate endpoints apply k-anonymity: suppress cohort cells with count < 10 students. De-identification of admin reports uses cohort-level statistics only (no individual student data points). Student free-text reflection fields not included in aggregate analytics.

data.entities: students (id UUID PK, university_student_id_encrypted TEXT, name_encrypted TEXT, email_encrypted TEXT, cohort_year INTEGER, assigned_counselor_id UUID FK nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). mood_logs (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, check_in_date DATE, mood_score INTEGER CHECK 1-10, energy_score INTEGER CHECK 1-10, reflection_encrypted TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). sleep_records (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, check_in_date DATE, sleep_hours NUMERIC(3,1), sleep_quality INTEGER CHECK 1-10, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). stressor_surveys (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, check_in_date DATE, stressor_type TEXT CHECK IN ('academic_deadline','exam_period','financial','social','family','housing'), severity INTEGER CHECK 1-5, free_text_encrypted TEXT). risk_flags (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, scoring_date DATE, composite_risk_score NUMERIC(5,2), severity TEXT CHECK IN ('low','moderate','high','critical'), acknowledged_by UUID FK nullable, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). counselor_notes (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, counselor_id UUID FK, note_content_encrypted TEXT, note_date DATE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). aggregate_reports (id UUID PK, report_type TEXT, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, cohort_year INTEGER nullable, metrics_json JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA with CMEK. Row-level security policies on students, mood_logs, sleep_records, stressor_surveys, counselor_notes. Read replica for counselor dashboard and analytics queries. Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 for session JWT cache and batch job distributed lock.
data.analytics_events: check_in_submitted (student_id_hash, mood_score, sleep_hours, stressor_count), risk_flag_generated (severity, scoring_date, student_cohort_year), counselor_viewed_student (counselor_id_hash, student_cohort_year - no student_id), admin_report_generated (report_type, period) emitted to BigQuery via Pub/Sub for longitudinal platform analytics.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1: API deployment (FastAPI, 2-8 pods HPA on CPU 60%), Next.js deployment (2-4 pods), nightly risk scoring CronJob (GKE CronJob 00:00 UTC, single pod, requests 4 vCPU 8GB for batch). Cloud SQL HA PostgreSQL 16 (db.g1.medium with failover replica). Cloud Memorystore Redis 7. Cloud Load Balancing with IAP for authentication layer before Next.js.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff + mypy lint, pytest 85% coverage gate (including RLS integration tests), Docker build, GCR push, kubectl rolling deploy to GKE. Helm chart for all Kubernetes manifests. CronJob spec versioned in Helm values.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics scraped by GKE Autopilot managed collector. Grafana dashboards: API request rate by endpoint, check-in submission rate (daily active students), risk_flag count by severity, batch job duration. PagerDuty alerts: API 5xx rate >1%, batch job failure or duration >5 hours, Cloud SQL connection pool saturation >80%.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a multi-role web app for college counseling centers with strong FERPA and HIPAA compliance requirements. Key architectural decisions: GKE Autopilot with FastAPI for scalable async API, PostgreSQL row-level security for FERPA caseload enforcement, nightly CronJob risk scoring pipeline with Redis distributed lock, k-anonymity for admin aggregate reports. Next.js 14 with Shibboleth SAML SSO for three-role access model. Data model must support longitudinal aggregation across mood_logs, sleep_records, and stressor_surveys for risk scoring.
target_output.title: CampusWell: Longitudinal Student Mental Wellness Tracking Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A FERPA and HIPAA-aware web application for college counseling centers that aggregates daily student wellness check-ins (mood scores, sleep quality, academic stressor surveys) to identify at-risk students and surface population-level trends. Students submit mobile-optimized daily check-ins via Next.js 14; a nightly GKE CronJob scores all active students against composite risk thresholds and generates risk_flags for counselor review. Counselors access assigned caseloads via a FastAPI-backed dashboard with PostgreSQL row-level security enforcing FERPA caseload restrictions. Administrators see only de-identified k-anonymized cohort aggregates. University Shibboleth SAML SSO with RBAC. GCP GKE Autopilot in us-central1 with Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 and Cloud Memorystore Redis.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier web architecture on GKE Autopilot: Next.js 14 frontend deployment (server-side rendered counselor dashboards, client-hydrated student check-in forms) behind Cloud Load Balancing with Identity-Aware Proxy; FastAPI backend deployment for API requests; GKE CronJob for nightly risk scoring batch. Authentication flows through NextAuth.js SAML provider against the university Shibboleth IdP; SAML attributes (eduPersonAffiliation, eduPersonEntitlement) map to local RBAC roles (student, counselor, admin). FastAPI validates session JWTs and enforces row-level security on every database query via PostgreSQL RLS policies. Student wellness records (mood_logs, sleep_records, stressor_surveys) stored in PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA with CMEK. Redis on Cloud Memorystore caches session JWTs and coordinates the nightly batch job via distributed lock. FERPA audit events streamed to Cloud Logging via Log Sink with 7-year GCS retention. Admin aggregate endpoints apply k-anonymity (minimum group size 10) before returning population metrics.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query (60-second stale), Zustand for check-in form state, NextAuth.js with SAML provider. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2.0 with PostgreSQL RLS session variables, Pydantic v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA with CMEK, read replica. Cache: Cloud Memorystore Redis 7. Batch: GKE CronJob (Python script, pandas for risk score aggregation). Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot us-central1, Cloud Load Balancing, Cloud IAP, Cloud SQL, Cloud Memorystore, GCS for audit log archival, BigQuery for platform analytics via Pub/Sub. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Helm for Kubernetes manifests. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana (GKE managed). Alerting: PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Student Check-in: mobile-optimized form with mood slider (1-10), energy slider (1-10), sleep hours and quality sliders, academic stressor multi-select (academic_deadline, exam_period, financial, social, family, housing) with severity rating, optional free-text reflection; target completion time under 3 minutes. Student Wellness History: personal mood trend chart (30-day), sleep quality line chart, stressor frequency calendar heatmap. Counselor Dashboard: assigned student list with risk_flag severity badges; individual student drilldown with longitudinal mood/sleep/stressor charts and counselor note entry form. Risk Scoring Engine: nightly CronJob computes composite_risk_score from rolling 7-day mood average weighted average, sleep deficit (hours below 7), and high-severity (4-5) stressor frequency; flags students above low/moderate/high/critical thresholds. Admin Population Dashboard: cohort-level trend charts (mean mood by week, sleep hours distribution, stressor type frequency), at-risk student count by severity tier; all cells suppressed if cohort count < 10 (k-anonymity).
target_output.system_components: Cloud Load Balancing + Cloud IAP: authenticates all requests before reaching GKE. GKE Autopilot cluster: Next.js deployment (2-4 pods), FastAPI API deployment (2-8 pods HPA), nightly risk scoring CronJob. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL HA: primary instance with CMEK; read replica for counselor dashboard and analytics queries. Cloud Memorystore Redis 7: session JWT cache, distributed lock for risk scoring CronJob. GCS bucket: FERPA audit log archive (Cloud Logging Log Sink, 7-year retention). BigQuery dataset: longitudinal platform analytics from Pub/Sub events. Prometheus: GKE Autopilot managed metrics collection. Grafana: operator dashboards on API latency, check-in submission rate, batch job duration, risk flag counts.
target_output.workflows: Student Daily Check-in: Student opens check-in URL on mobile -> Shibboleth SAML redirects to university IdP -> NextAuth.js receives SAML assertion, maps eduPersonAffiliation=student to student role, issues JWT -> Next.js renders check-in form (SSR shell, client hydration) -> student completes mood, energy, sleep, stressor fields (Zustand draft auto-saves to localStorage every 10s) -> submits form -> FastAPI POST /check-ins validates schema, writes mood_logs, sleep_records, and stressor_surveys records in a single transaction, clears Redis draft key -> student sees confirmation with streak count. Nightly Risk Scoring: GKE CronJob starts at 00:00 UTC -> acquires Redis distributed lock (SETNX risk-scoring-{date}, TTL 21600) -> queries active students in batches of 500 -> for each student: computes rolling 7-day mood_score average, sleep deficit (7 - mean_sleep_hours if negative else 0), high_stressor_frequency (count stressor_surveys where severity >= 4 in last 7 days) -> composite_risk_score = (10 - mood_avg) * 0.5 + sleep_deficit * 1.0 + high_stressor_frequency * 0.8 -> writes or updates risk_flags with severity threshold (score >= 8 = critical, >= 5 = high, >= 3 = moderate, else low) -> releases lock -> sends Pub/Sub event per high/critical flag for future notification pipeline. Counselor Drilldown: Counselor authenticates via SAML -> selects student from assigned list -> FastAPI GET /students/{id}/drilldown sets app.counselor_assigned_ids PostgreSQL session variable -> RLS policy verifies student in counselor's assigned_student_ids[] -> returns 90-day mood_logs, sleep_records, stressor_surveys, counselor_notes, current risk_flag -> FERPA audit_log record written for this access event.
target_output.data_model: students (id UUID PK, university_student_id_encrypted TEXT, name_encrypted TEXT, email_encrypted TEXT, cohort_year INTEGER, assigned_counselor_id UUID FK nullable, enrollment_status TEXT CHECK IN ('active','on_leave','graduated'), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). mood_logs (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK REFERENCES students ON DELETE CASCADE, check_in_date DATE, mood_score INTEGER CHECK(mood_score BETWEEN 1 AND 10), energy_score INTEGER CHECK(energy_score BETWEEN 1 AND 10), reflection_encrypted TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(student_id, check_in_date)). sleep_records (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, check_in_date DATE, sleep_hours NUMERIC(3,1) CHECK(sleep_hours BETWEEN 0 AND 24), sleep_quality INTEGER CHECK(sleep_quality BETWEEN 1 AND 10), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(student_id, check_in_date)). stressor_surveys (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, check_in_date DATE, stressor_type TEXT CHECK(stressor_type IN ('academic_deadline','exam_period','financial','social','family','housing')), severity INTEGER CHECK(severity BETWEEN 1 AND 5), free_text_encrypted TEXT, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). risk_flags (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, scoring_date DATE, composite_risk_score NUMERIC(5,2), severity TEXT CHECK(severity IN ('low','moderate','high','critical')), acknowledged_by UUID FK nullable, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, UNIQUE(student_id, scoring_date)). counselor_notes (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, counselor_id UUID FK, note_content_encrypted TEXT, note_date DATE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). ferpa_audit_log (id UUID PK, accessor_id UUID, student_id UUID, resource_type TEXT, action TEXT, ip_address INET, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ). aggregate_reports (id UUID PK, report_type TEXT, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, cohort_year INTEGER nullable, metrics_json JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Indexes: (student_id, check_in_date) on mood_logs, sleep_records, stressor_surveys; (student_id, scoring_date) on risk_flags; (occurred_at) on ferpa_audit_log. Clinical wellness entities: patient_profiles (id UUID PK, student_id FK, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, assigned_therapist_id UUID, treatment_plan_id UUID, crisis_protocol_flag BOOLEAN), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, student_id FK, encounter_type ENUM intake/session/crisis, therapist_id UUID, chief_complaint TEXT, clinical_notes_encrypted TEXT, vitals_json JSONB, encounter_date DATE), prescriptions (id UUID PK, student_id FK, medication_name VARCHAR, dosage VARCHAR, prescriber_id UUID, status ENUM active/discontinued, start_date DATE, end_date DATE).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1. POST /check-ins (student: submit daily check-in, creates mood_log + sleep_record + stressor_survey[]; returns {check_in_id, streak_count}). GET /check-ins/me?start=&end= (student: own check-in history with mood and sleep time series). GET /students?counselor_me=true (counselor: assigned student list with current risk_flag severity). GET /students/{id}/drilldown (counselor: 90-day longitudinal data for one student; triggers FERPA audit log; RLS enforced at DB layer). POST /counselor-notes (counselor: create note for assigned student). GET /risk-flags?severity=high (counselor: filter assigned students by flag severity). GET /analytics/population?cohort_year=&weeks=12 (admin: aggregate trend data with k-anonymity applied). GET /audit/access-log?student_id=&start=&end= (compliance officer: FERPA audit log query).
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: row-level security policies on students, mood_logs, sleep_records, stressor_surveys, counselor_notes tables restrict counselor queries to assigned_student_ids[]; PostgreSQL SET app.counselor_assigned_ids in every FastAPI request context. Immutable ferpa_audit_log records every student record access (accessor_id, student_id, resource_type, action, timestamp); streamed to Cloud Logging Log Sink -> GCS with 7-year retention. Admin k-anonymity: AggregateAnalyticsService suppresses any cohort cell with student_count < 10 and replaces with '<10' in response. HIPAA considerations: CMEK on Cloud SQL for PHI at rest; TLS 1.3 enforced at Cloud Load Balancing; no PHI in Cloud Logging (RLS audit log records student_id hash, not PHI content). Authentication: Shibboleth SAML SSO with MFA at university IdP level; session JWTs expire 8 hours; Redis cache for JWT validation with 15-minute TTL. WAF rules on Cloud Load Balancing for SQL injection and XSS blocking. Student free-text reflection fields stored encrypted and excluded from all aggregate analytics endpoints.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest with 85% coverage gate (including RLS integration tests asserting cross-caseload query returns empty result set), Docker build, GCR push. Helm chart versioned in /charts/campuswell with values per environment. kubectl rollout applied by GitLab CI deploy job with rollout status watch. Environments: dev (single-replica Cloud SQL, no CMEK for cost), staging (mirrors production sizing, used for FERPA compliance UAT with synthetic student data), production (Cloud SQL HA with CMEK and read replica, Redis 7 HA). GKE CronJob spec: schedule '0 0 * * *', completions=1, backoffLimit=3, activeDeadlineSeconds=21600 (6 hours). Auto-scaling: HPA on FastAPI deployment (CPU 60% target, min 2 max 8 pods); no HPA on Next.js (memory-limited SSR, scale by time-of-day). Cloud SQL failover: automatic with 60-second RTO. Database migrations: Alembic versioned migrations applied in CI deploy job before rolling pod update.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics via GKE Autopilot managed collector. Custom FastAPI middleware exports: http_request_duration_seconds by endpoint and status, check_in_submissions_total, risk_flag_counts_by_severity. Batch job metrics: batch_risk_scoring_duration_seconds, batch_students_processed_total, batch_job_success (gauge). Grafana dashboards: daily active students check-in rate (target >60% during exam weeks), risk flag severity distribution trend, API p95 latency by endpoint, batch job completion time. PagerDuty alerts: API 5xx error rate >1% for 5 minutes, batch CronJob failure or activeDeadlineSeconds exceeded, Cloud SQL connection pool utilization >80%, Redis memory utilization >75%. FERPA audit log completeness checked via daily Cloud Logging metric alert if ferpa_audit_log insert rate drops to zero during clinic operating hours.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot: ~$180/month for API and frontend pods at baseline. Cloud SQL HA db.g1.medium: ~$140/month. Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 Basic M1: ~$40/month. GCS audit log archive: ~$5/month at 1GB/year. BigQuery streaming: ~$10/month. Total estimated: ~$380/month for 5,000 enrolled students. At 20,000 students: API scales to 8 pods (~$450/month), Cloud SQL upgraded to db.g2.medium (~$280/month), Redis standard tier (~$80/month), estimated $830/month. Nightly batch CronJob cost: single 4-vCPU pod running 2 hours = ~$0.50/night.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Shibboleth SAML SSO with three-role RBAC, student daily check-in form (mood, sleep, stressor survey), PostgreSQL schema with RLS policies, FERPA ferpa_audit_log, nightly risk scoring CronJob (composite score and flag generation), counselor dashboard with assigned student list and risk badges, individual student drilldown view, GitLab CI pipeline with RLS integration tests, CMEK Cloud SQL, GKE Autopilot deployment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Student wellness history personal dashboard (mood trend chart, sleep chart, stressor heatmap), counselor_notes SOAP note entry, risk_flag acknowledgment workflow, Grafana dashboards and PagerDuty alerts. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Admin population analytics dashboard with k-anonymity, AggregateAnalyticsService with cohort suppression, aggregate_reports generation and caching, BigQuery pipeline for platform-level analytics.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict and ruff enforced in GitLab CI. FastAPI endpoints typed with Pydantic v2 models for all student and counselor data shapes. async SQLAlchemy 2.0 with execute() pattern; no ORM lazy loading to avoid N+1 in batch contexts. RLS integration tests: pytest fixtures create two counselors each with disjoint student sets; assert that each counselor's API requests return only their assigned students and cannot query the other's caseload. 85% pytest coverage gate. Next.js TypeScript strict mode. All student PHI fields in API responses have TypeScript types marked with a PHI comment annotation for code review awareness. FERPA audit log insertion tested: every test that calls /students/:id/drilldown or /check-ins/:student_id asserts a ferpa_audit_log row was written.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Nightly batch CronJob exceeds 6-hour window as student population scales to 20,000+ with 90-day history. Mitigation: batch students in parallel worker pools using asyncio.gather with semaphore limiting concurrent DB connections; add composite index on (student_id, check_in_date DESC) for scoring queries. Risk: FERPA audit log table grows to hundreds of millions of rows over 7-year retention. Mitigation: partition ferpa_audit_log by occurred_at year; archive partitions older than 1 year to GCS via pg_dump. Risk: University Shibboleth IdP outage blocks all user access. Mitigation: NextAuth.js session JWTs cached in Redis with 8-hour TTL provide grace period; counselors can continue reviewing existing data during IdP maintenance windows. Tradeoff: k-anonymity minimum group size of 10 suppresses small cohorts (graduating seniors in final semester) from admin analytics; accepted as privacy-utility tradeoff.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Composite risk score formula: (10 - mood_avg) * 0.5 + sleep_deficit * 1.0 + high_stressor_frequency * 0.8 with threshold calibration pending clinical validation with counseling center staff. De-identification for admin aggregates: k-anonymity with group size 10 chosen pending university Legal review of Expert Determination alternative. GKE CronJob chosen over Cloud Run Jobs for tighter Kubernetes scheduling control. Redis SETNX distributed lock chosen over Cloud Spanner lock table for simplicity at current scale.

metadata.notes: College counseling mental wellness tracking; first-pass plan covers FERPA RLS enforcement, nightly risk scoring pipeline, and k-anonymized admin aggregates.

profile.domain: Corporate Wellness / Meditation App Marketing

project_goal.value: Conversion-focused landing page for a mobile meditation app targeting corporate wellness programs, with animated benefit highlights, pricing tiers, and CRM-synced lead capture
project_goal.rationale: Core marketing objective to convert corporate HR buyers into demo requests

target_users.value: Corporate HR managers and wellness program coordinators evaluating meditation app subscriptions
target_users.rationale: B2B buyers are the primary conversion target

project_class.rationale: Static marketing page with no persistent user accounts or server-rendered application logic

capabilities.rationale: Static frontend with GA4 event tracking and HubSpot CRM integration via serverless function

complexity_level.rationale: Single-purpose marketing page with minimal state

risk_level.rationale: No authentication, no persistent user data, read-mostly public content

data_sensitivity.rationale: Lead capture collects only name and work email, no sensitive personal data

external_exposure.rationale: Publicly accessible marketing page with no login requirement

access_model.value: Public access with no authentication; demo request form is rate-limited by Vercel Edge
access_model.rationale: Landing page must be fully accessible without friction for conversion

feature_scope.value: Hero section with animated meditation visuals, corporate benefit highlights, pricing tier cards, customer testimonials, demo request form syncing to HubSpot
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for initial launch

mvp_scope.value: Hero, pricing tiers, and working lead capture form with HubSpot sync; testimonials and animations in phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for campaign launch deadline

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel, form honeypot field, Vercel Edge rate limiting on /api/leads endpoint
security_baseline.rationale: Sufficient controls for a public marketing page

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro island architecture delivers zero-JS by default with selective hydration for animated components

backend_stack.value: Vercel serverless function (Node 20) for HubSpot lead push; no persistent backend
backend_stack.rationale: Thin integration layer only, no application server needed

data_platform.value: Headless CMS (Contentful) for pricing and testimonial copy; HubSpot as lead store
data_platform.rationale: Decoupled content management enables marketing team self-service without deploys

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-ops static hosting with automatic preview URLs per PR

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data stored on platform; leads stored in HubSpot subject to HubSpot retention policy; GA4 data retained 14 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR-light posture suitable for no-PII landing page

future_scope.value: A/B testing via Vercel Edge Config, localized pricing tiers for EU markets, in-app deeplink for mobile users

constraints.value: Must achieve Lighthouse score >= 95, page must load in under 2 seconds on 4G, marketing team manages content without engineering involvement
constraints.rationale: Performance and self-service are key stakeholder requirements

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 with custom events for CTA clicks and form submissions; Hotjar session recordings; Vercel deployment status Slack alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard conversion analytics stack for marketing pages

execution_preference.value: Single sprint delivery with daily deploys via Vercel preview
execution_preference.rationale: Fast iteration cycle for marketing

project.goal: Launch a high-converting landing page for Serenity corporate wellness meditation app
project.target_users: HR buyers and wellness coordinators at mid-to-large enterprises

frontend.animations: CSS transitions and Astro partial hydration for animated benefit cards

backend.integration: HubSpot CRM via REST API v3

devops.environments: preview per PR, production on main

open_questions.ab_testing: Deferred to post-launch via Edge Config

reasoner_reviews.product: The landing page scope is sharply focused on a single conversion goal: demo request submissions from corporate HR buyers. Pricing tier presentation and animated benefit highlights directly support this objective. Contentful integration ensures the marketing team can iterate copy without engineering cycles, which is critical for campaign agility.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 island architecture is the right call here. Shipping zero JS by default ensures Lighthouse scores above 95 while selective hydration enables the animated benefit card components. The thin Vercel serverless function for HubSpot sync avoids introducing a persistent backend for a simple webhook-style integration.
reasoner_reviews.security: A public landing page has minimal attack surface. Honeypot fields and Vercel Edge rate limiting on the /api/leads endpoint are proportionate controls. HTTPS is enforced by Vercel. No user accounts means no session management attack surface.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-week timeline is achievable. Contentful webhooks triggering Vercel rebuilds on content publish is a standard pattern. GA4 custom events for CTA and form tracking should be instrumented from day one to capture baseline conversion data.

backend.service_design: Single Vercel serverless function at /api/leads (Node 20 runtime): validates form payload, pushes contact to HubSpot CRM via REST API v3 using a private integration token stored in Vercel environment variables, triggers HubSpot workflow for welcome email sequence. No database writes on the platform side.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/leads accepts JSON body {name, company, email, employee_count, source_campaign}; responds 200 with {success:true} or 422 with field errors. HubSpot contact upsert uses email as deduplication key.
backend.failure_handling: HubSpot API errors are caught and logged to Vercel function logs; form returns a user-facing error message without exposing upstream status codes. Retry not implemented client-side; users can resubmit.

security.auth_design: No authentication required; page is fully public.
security.abuse_prevention: Honeypot hidden field rejects automated submissions at edge. Vercel Edge rate limit: 10 requests/minute per IP on /api/leads. Form submission requires JavaScript to be enabled, reducing simple bot submissions.
security.secrets_management: HubSpot private integration token stored as Vercel environment secret; not exposed to client bundle.

data.entities: leads (captured in HubSpot CRM: email, name, company, employee_count, source_campaign, hubspot_contact_id), pricing_tiers (Contentful: tier_name, price_per_seat, feature_list, cta_label), feature_highlights (Contentful: headline, description, animation_id), testimonials (Contentful: quote, attribution, company_logo_url)
data.storage_design: No platform-side database. All lead data lives in HubSpot. All content lives in Contentful. Vercel serves static build artifacts from CDN edge nodes.
data.analytics_events: GA4 custom events: cta_clicked (section, label), form_started, form_submitted (success/error), pricing_tier_viewed, testimonial_scrolled_into_view

devops.deployment_topology: Astro 4 static build deployed to Vercel global edge CDN. Contentful webhook triggers Vercel rebuild on content publish. GitHub Actions runs Lighthouse CI and link-checker on every PR before Vercel preview deploys.
devops.environments: Feature branch: automatic Vercel preview URL. main branch: production at app domain. No staging environment required for static landing page.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: install deps (pnpm), run Lighthouse CI (score gate >=95), build Astro, post preview URL to PR comment. Vercel handles production deploy on merge to main.
devops.observability_stack: Google Analytics 4 for conversion funnel events. Hotjar for heatmaps and session replay. Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals per edge node. Slack notification on failed production deploys via Vercel webhooks.

frontend.app_structure: Single-page Astro site with scroll-based section layout; no client-side routing; HubSpot form embedded via script island
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero → Benefits → Pricing Tiers → Testimonials → Demo Request Form (HubSpot CRM-synced)
frontend.ui_modules: AnimatedHeroSection, PricingTierCards, TestimonialCarousel, DemoRequestForm with client-side validation
frontend.performance_notes: Lazy-load testimonial images; defer HubSpot script until user scrolls to form; target Lighthouse performance score ≥ 95
frontend.frontend_risks: HubSpot script injection may delay LCP; gate form script load behind Intersection Observer to protect Core Web Vitals

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a flawed plan for a simple landing_page. The contract correctly specifies no backend runtime and no database, but the plan will incorrectly include a server-side database write for lead capture and reference a backend framework that contradicts the landing_page project class constraints. Key intentional flaw: the data_model section will describe a server-side leads table as if the platform persists lead data, contradicting the contract which routes leads to HubSpot only.
target_output.title: Serenity Corporate Wellness Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A conversion-optimized marketing landing page for the Serenity meditation app built with Astro 4 and Tailwind CSS, deployed to Vercel Edge Network. Features animated corporate benefit highlights, pricing tier cards, and a lead capture form that syncs demo requests to HubSpot CRM via a Vercel serverless function. Contentful headless CMS enables marketing team self-service for copy and pricing updates without engineering involvement.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generation with zero-JS baseline and selective island hydration for animated components, deployed to Vercel global CDN edge nodes across 30+ regions. Demo request form submissions POST to a Vercel Node 20 serverless function which validates input, deduplicates contacts in HubSpot via email, and triggers a welcome email workflow sequence. Contentful webhooks fire on content publish events, triggering a Vercel rebuild so pricing and testimonial updates go live within minutes. GA4 and Hotjar are injected as lightweight client-side scripts for conversion tracking. A PostgreSQL database on Supabase stores lead capture events server-side for internal reporting, providing a platform-owned data record independent of HubSpot.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, Tailwind CSS, Alpine.js for animated benefit card interactions. Serverless: Vercel Edge Functions (Node 20) for /api/leads HubSpot integration. CMS: Contentful (content delivery API for pricing tiers and testimonials). Analytics: Google Analytics 4 (gtag.js), Hotjar snippet. CRM: HubSpot REST API v3. Database: Supabase PostgreSQL (lead audit store). CDN: Vercel Edge Network.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero section with CSS-animated mindfulness visual and primary CTA button tracked as cta_clicked GA4 event. Corporate Benefit Highlights: 6 animated cards (stress reduction metrics, absenteeism data, ROI statistics) hydrated as Astro islands. Pricing Tier Cards: Starter/Growth/Enterprise tiers pulled from Contentful, each with per-seat pricing and feature checklist. Testimonials Carousel: quotes from Fortune 500 HR directors sourced from Contentful with company logos. Demo Request Form: name, company email, company size selector, source campaign hidden field; POST to /api/leads.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge CDN serving Astro static build artifacts. Vercel Node 20 serverless function for /api/leads. Contentful CDN delivering pricing_tiers and testimonials content. HubSpot CRM as lead store and email automation trigger. Supabase PostgreSQL instance storing raw lead payloads for internal reporting. GA4 measurement protocol for server-side event mirroring. Hotjar asynchronous snippet for session recording.
target_output.workflows: Demo Request Submission: Visitor fills form (name, company email, employee count, source campaign) and clicks Submit. Browser POSTs JSON to /api/leads serverless function. Function checks honeypot field, validates email domain, calls HubSpot Contacts API v3 to upsert contact, inserts raw payload row into Supabase leads table, returns 200. HubSpot workflow triggers 3-email welcome sequence. GA4 form_submitted event fires client-side. Content Update: Marketing editor updates pricing tier copy or adds new testimonial in Contentful. On publish, Contentful fires webhook to Vercel deploy hook URL. Vercel triggers Astro rebuild pulling updated content via Contentful Delivery API. New build deploys to edge CDN within 90 seconds.
target_output.data_model: leads table (Supabase PostgreSQL): id UUID PK, email VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, company VARCHAR, employee_count_range VARCHAR, source_campaign VARCHAR, hubspot_contact_id VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ip_hash VARCHAR. pricing_tiers (Contentful content type): tier_name, price_per_seat_usd, billing_period, feature_list String[], cta_label, highlighted BOOLEAN. feature_highlights (Contentful): headline, stat_value, stat_label, animation_id, sort_order. testimonials (Contentful): quote_text, attribution_name, attribution_title, company_name, logo_asset_url.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/leads: accepts {name, email, company, employee_count_range, source_campaign, _honeypot}; validates email format and non-empty required fields; returns 200 {success:true,hubspot_contact_id} or 422 {errors:[...]}. No other server routes. Contentful Delivery API v2 accessed at build time for pricing_tiers and testimonials content entries. HubSpot REST API v3 POST /crm/v3/objects/contacts called server-side from Vercel function.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel (automatic TLS). Honeypot hidden field (_honeypot must be empty) checked at serverless function entry. Vercel Edge rate limit: 10 requests per minute per IP on /api/leads. HubSpot integration token stored as Vercel environment secret, never bundled in client JS. Supabase connection string stored as Vercel environment secret. IP address hashed (SHA-256) before storage for GDPR-light compliance. No cookies set beyond GA4 analytics (consent banner for EU visitors via cookie-consent.js library).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline on PR: pnpm install, Lighthouse CI (score gate >=95 for performance, accessibility, best-practices, SEO), broken link checker, Astro dry-run build. Vercel auto-deploys preview URL per PR branch. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deploy to global edge. Contentful publish webhook hits Vercel deploy hook for content-only rebuilds. Rollback: Vercel one-click instant rollback to previous deployment. No migration scripts required; Supabase schema applied via Supabase CLI migration files committed to repo.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 custom events: cta_clicked (section, label), form_started, form_submitted (result: success/error), pricing_tier_viewed (tier_name), testimonial_impression. Hotjar heatmaps on hero and pricing sections; session recordings filtered to form abandonment events. Vercel Analytics for LCP, FID, CLS per edge region. Vercel deploy webhook posts success/failure to Slack #marketing-deploys channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $0/month on Vercel Hobby or ~$20/month Pro for team features and increased serverless invocations. Contentful free tier covers content volume. Supabase free tier covers lead storage at expected volume (<5,000 leads/month). Scales to millions of page views with zero infrastructure changes on Vercel Edge CDN.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (week 1-2): Hero section, pricing tier cards from Contentful, working /api/leads function with HubSpot sync and Supabase write, GA4 base tracking, Lighthouse CI gate, production deploy. Phase 2 (week 3): Animated benefit highlight islands, testimonials carousel, Hotjar instrumentation, form validation UX improvements, cookie consent banner for EU.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components for each page section. Tailwind utility classes only, no custom CSS files. All Contentful content types defined in TypeScript interfaces generated via Contentful CLI. Serverless function tested with Vitest unit tests mocking HubSpot and Supabase clients. Lighthouse CI score gates enforced in GitHub Actions, blocking merge if scores drop below 95.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Supabase lead table is a redundant store not in the original contract — introduces an unnecessary dependency and a GDPR data minimization concern by storing IP hashes. Mitigation: if removed, HubSpot remains the single source of truth for leads. Risk: Contentful webhook triggering full Astro rebuild adds 90-second content publish lag. Mitigation: acceptable for marketing content; real-time not required. Tradeoff: Astro static generation over Next.js for simpler deploy model and better Lighthouse scores at the cost of no incremental static regeneration.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Contentful over Sanity for CMS based on marketing team familiarity. Decided against persistent platform-side database in original contract; Supabase store added for internal reporting without consulting security review — flagged as architectural inconsistency. HubSpot email sequence triggered by workflow automation, not by custom serverless logic.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: introduces a Supabase PostgreSQL lead store that contradicts the landing_page contract constraint of no server-side persistence and was not listed in the approved tech stack.

profile.domain: Consumer Mental Wellness / AI Journaling

project_goal.value: AI-powered conversational journaling assistant that uses LLM-driven prompts to guide reflective writing, detect emotional themes, and produce weekly mental wellness summaries for individual consumers
project_goal.rationale: Core product mission replacing monolithic PHP journaling backend with containerized microservices

target_users.value: Individual consumers seeking guided reflective journaling and mental wellness tracking
target_users.rationale: B2C wellness app with privacy-sensitive personal health data

project_class.rationale: LLM-driven prompt generation and emotional theme classification are the core intelligence layer

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, realtime, devops
capabilities.rationale: Full consumer app stack with LLM streaming, Auth0, real-time journal sessions, and DevOps modernization

complexity_level.rationale: LLM streaming, emotional theme classification pipeline, weekly summarization jobs, and monolith-to-microservice migration

risk_level.rationale: Emotional health content is sensitive personal data; LLM provider data processing agreements required

data_sensitivity.rationale: Emotional health journaling data constitutes sensitive health information

external_exposure.rationale: All endpoints require Auth0 JWT; journal data is never publicly accessible

access_model.value: Auth0 with email/social login; JWT-gated API endpoints; user sees only their own journal data via row-level user_id scoping
access_model.rationale: Consumer authentication with strict data isolation per user

feature_scope.value: LLM-guided journal sessions with streamed prompt responses, emotional theme detection, weekly wellness digest emails, conversation thread history, user preferences for tone and topic focus
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for v1 product

mvp_scope.value: Journal session creation with LLM prompt streaming, emotional theme tagging, and 7-day history view; weekly digest deferred to phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for beta launch to 500 users

security_baseline.value: Auth0 JWT on all API routes, PostgreSQL row-level user_id isolation, journal content encrypted at rest via AWS KMS, TLS 1.3 in transit, LLM prompt/response logging disabled at provider level
security_baseline.rationale: Sensitive emotional data requires encryption and strict tenant isolation

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript and Vite
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Vite dev server enables fast iteration

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async support ideal for LLM streaming responses via SSE

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for journal entries and user data; Redis 7 for active session state and rate limiting
data_platform.rationale: Relational storage for structured journal data; Redis for ephemeral session context

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with containerized microservices replacing monolithic PHP backend
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container runtime enabling independent service scaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: Journal entries retained for life of account; deleted within 30 days of account deletion; LLM provider instructed not to train on user content via API opt-out; weekly summaries retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: User expectation of privacy for emotional health data

future_scope.value: Therapist-sharing feature, longitudinal mood trend analytics dashboard, iOS and Android native apps

constraints.value: LLM provider must support data processing agreement with opt-out of training on user content; backend migration must not break existing user data in PHP database; budget under $3000/month
constraints.rationale: Privacy, migration safety, and cost constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on all Fargate services, structured JSON logging with correlation IDs, LLM token usage and latency metrics, PagerDuty alerts for error rate and latency thresholds
observability_baseline.rationale: Visibility into LLM cost and latency is critical for budget control

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; blue-green deployment for zero-downtime PHP migration cutover
execution_preference.rationale: Migration requires careful cutover strategy

compliance_context.value: HIPAA considerations for emotional health data; data processing agreement required with OpenAI or Anthropic; user consent modal for AI analysis of journal content required at signup
compliance_context.rationale: Emotional health data sensitivity triggers HIPAA review; LLM provider DPA is a hard requirement

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o via API with streaming SSE responses; system prompt encodes user tone preferences and prior emotional themes; weekly summarization uses batch API; prompt/response logging disabled
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o selected for emotional nuance and instruction-following quality; streaming required for conversational UX

project.goal: Migrate monolithic PHP journaling app to containerized AI-powered FastAPI microservices on AWS Fargate
project.target_users: Individual consumers, 500 beta users scaling to 10,000
project.timeline: Q2 migration complete, Q3 full feature launch

frontend.state: Zustand for UI state, React Query for server state

backend.workers: Celery + Redis for weekly summarization jobs

security.auth: Auth0 JWT validation in FastAPI middleware
security.encryption: AWS KMS for journal content at rest

devops.deployment: Blue-green for PHP cutover, rolling for subsequent updates

open_questions.vector_search: Evaluate pgvector for semantic journal search in phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The LLM-guided journaling session UX is the product differentiator. Streaming the reflective prompt response via SSE prevents the dead wait that would kill engagement. Weekly wellness digest email is the retention hook and must be reliable even if the Celery job is delayed. Emotional theme detection accuracy will determine user trust.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async is the correct foundation for SSE streaming to multiple concurrent journal sessions. The PHP data migration needs a shadow-write phase where both PHP and FastAPI write to PostgreSQL simultaneously for at least 2 weeks before cutover, not a big-bang migration. The ISS-002 concern about missing migration rollback path needs to be addressed with explicit rollback steps.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 JWT validation must enforce audience and issuer claims, not just signature verification. Row-level user_id checks on every database query are non-negotiable for a multi-tenant emotional health app. The ISS-001 issue about LLM prompt injection via user-controlled journal content must be addressed with explicit prompt boundary markers.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: OpenAI DPA must be signed before any user emotional content is sent to the API. The $3000/month budget is tight with GPT-4o at scale; token usage logging in Datadog from day one is essential for early cost detection.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan lacked explicit detail on the PHP migration shadow-write phase and rollback steps. The LLM prompt construction showing how user emotional themes are injected into the system prompt was too vague for implementation. These are the two areas requiring the most improvement in this revision.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with route-based code splitting via React.lazy. Routes: /journal (active session), /history (past entries), /themes (emotional theme summary), /settings (tone preferences). Auth0 provider wraps the app root; useAuth0 hook gates all routes.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Journal Session Page: displays current LLM prompt, text area for user response, streaming indicator, and 'Next Prompt' button. Journal History Page: paginated list of conversation_threads with emotional theme badges. Theme Summary: radar chart of top emotional themes over 30 days using Recharts.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for active journal session state (current thread_id, streaming status, pending response). React Query for journal_entries and weekly_summaries fetching with 5-minute stale time.
frontend.frontend_risks: SSE connection drops on mobile background tab must be handled with automatic reconnect and session state recovery from Redis.

backend.service_design: Three FastAPI services: journal-api (session management, entry CRUD, Auth0 JWT validation), llm-gateway (OpenAI streaming proxy with prompt construction and token accounting), summarization-worker (Celery consumer for weekly digest jobs). All services share the PostgreSQL 16 RDS instance via async SQLAlchemy with connection pooling.
backend.llm_integration_design: llm-gateway service constructs system prompt: 'You are a reflective journaling guide. The user prefers a {tone_preference} tone. Their recent emotional themes are: {top_themes_list}. Guide them with open-ended questions.' User message is sandwiched between explicit XML-style delimiters <user_input> and </user_input> to prevent prompt injection. OpenAI streaming response chunked via SSE to React client.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat schedules weekly_summarization task every Sunday 02:00 UTC. Task fetches all journal_entries for user in past 7 days, calls OpenAI batch API with emotional theme extraction prompt, writes weekly_summaries row, triggers SES email with digest HTML template.
backend.failure_handling: LLM gateway implements exponential backoff on OpenAI API 429 and 5xx responses. If LLM is unavailable, journal-api returns a fallback static prompt from the llm_prompts table. Celery task retries up to 3 times with 5-minute intervals before marking job as failed and alerting via PagerDuty.

security.auth_design: Auth0 M2M application for service-to-service calls; Regular Web Application for user-facing Auth0 tenant. FastAPI middleware validates JWT audience=api.serenityjournal.com, issuer=serenityjournal.auth0.com on every request.
security.authorization_model: Every SQL query in journal-api includes WHERE user_id = :current_user_id enforced by a SQLAlchemy query helper. No admin bypass routes in MVP. Emotional health data never returned in list endpoints without explicit user auth.
security.privacy_controls: OpenAI API called with user: hashed_user_id for abuse monitoring without linking to PII. LLM response logging disabled via API parameter. Journal content encrypted at rest via RDS KMS encryption. User consent for AI analysis captured at signup and stored in user_preferences.ai_consent BOOLEAN.
security.security_risks: Prompt injection via crafted journal entries is the primary LLM-specific risk. XML delimiter approach mitigates but does not eliminate; monitor for anomalous system prompt overrides in LLM gateway logs.

data.entities: journal_entries (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, thread_id UUID FK, entry_text TEXT, entry_text_encrypted BOOLEAN, emotional_themes JSONB, word_count INT, created_at). conversation_threads (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, started_at, llm_prompt_id UUID FK, status ENUM active/completed). emotional_themes (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, theme_label VARCHAR, detected_count INT, first_seen_at, last_seen_at). weekly_summaries (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, week_start DATE, summary_text TEXT, theme_counts JSONB, created_at). llm_prompts (id UUID PK, prompt_template TEXT, tone_category VARCHAR, version INT, active BOOLEAN). user_preferences (user_id UUID PK, tone_preference ENUM reflective/direct/gentle, ai_consent BOOLEAN, digest_email_enabled BOOLEAN).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ us-east-1 with KMS encryption at rest. Redis 7 ElastiCache for active session state (thread_id -> last_llm_response_chunk) and Celery broker. S3 for journal entry export archives (user-requested data exports).
data.retention_and_deletion: Soft delete on journal_entries and conversation_threads (deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Hard delete job runs 30 days after account_deletion_requested_at. LLM prompt cache in Redis TTL 24 hours. weekly_summaries retained 2 years then archived to S3 Glacier.
data.migration_notes: PHP MySQL database has tables: journals, users, entries. Migration script maps journals -> conversation_threads, entries -> journal_entries with user_id lookup. Shadow-write phase: PHP writes to MySQL AND PostgreSQL simultaneously for 2 weeks. Validation job compares row counts and checksums daily. Cutover: Auth0 migration of PHP user passwords via bcrypt import, traffic switched via ALB weighted routing rule from 0% to 10% to 100% Fargate over 3 days.

ISS-001.title: LLM prompt injection via user journal content not mitigated
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan sent raw user journal text directly into LLM system prompt without boundary markers, enabling crafted inputs to override assistant persona.

ISS-002.detail: Previous plan described big-bang cutover from PHP to FastAPI with no shadow-write phase, no row count validation, and no rollback procedure if the PostgreSQL data is corrupted.

previous_audits.summary: Solid LLM streaming architecture and FastAPI service decomposition, but migration strategy is dangerously thin and the compliance gate for OpenAI DPA is absent. The prompt injection fix was not present in round 1.
strengths: FastAPI async SSE streaming design is well-suited to the journaling session UX
Auth0 JWT validation with audience and issuer checks correctly specified
PostgreSQL row-level user_id isolation described for multi-tenant emotional data
