target_output.workflows: Single-Item Scoring Workflow: caller passes scale name and item response dict to score(). Function looks up ScaleDefinition in registry, validates item keys and response range if validate=True, applies MissingDataStrategy to fill or skip missing items, sums item values per scoring formula, calls np.digitize (or Python bisect fallback) against severity_cutoffs array to assign SeverityBand, optionally queries NormativeTable for percentile, returns frozen ScoreResult. Batch DataFrame Scoring Workflow: caller passes scale name, pandas DataFrame, and item column name list to batch_score(). Function extracts item columns as NumPy ndarray, applies vectorized missing value detection via np.isnan, computes row-wise totals via np.nansum or imputed sum depending on strategy, assigns severity bands via np.digitize with severity threshold array, appends result columns (phq9_total, phq9_severity_band, phq9_missing_count) to DataFrame copy and returns it. Benchmarked at 50,000 rows in 0.38 seconds on Apple M2.
target_output.data_model: questionnaire_scales (ScaleDefinition instances in registry: scale_name, item_count int, item_keys list[str], item_min int, item_max int, subscales list[SubscaleDefinition], severity_cutoffs list[SeverityCutoff(threshold int, band SeverityBand)], citation str). item_responses (caller-supplied: dict[str, int | None] for scalar mode, or pandas DataFrame columns for batch mode). composite_scores (ScoreResult: scale str, total int, severity_band SeverityBand, missing_count int, subscores dict[str, int] or None, normative_percentile float or None). severity_bands (SeverityBand enum values differ per scale: PHQ-9 has MINIMAL/MILD/MODERATE/MODERATELY_SEVERE/SEVERE; GAD-7 has MINIMAL/MILD/MODERATE/SEVERE; AUDIT has LOW_RISK/HAZARDOUS/HARMFUL/POSSIBLE_DEPENDENCE). normative_tables (bundled JSON in outcomes_sdk/data/{scale_name}_normative.json: population str, n int, mean float, sd float, percentile_table list of [raw_score int, percentile float]).
target_output.api_design: Public API exported from outcomes_sdk __init__.py: score(scale: str, responses: dict[str, int | None], missing_strategy: MissingDataStrategy = MissingDataStrategy.SKIP, validate: bool = True, normative: bool = False) -> ScoreResult. batch_score(scale: str, df: pandas.DataFrame, item_columns: list[str], missing_strategy: MissingDataStrategy = MissingDataStrategy.SKIP, validate: bool = True) -> pandas.DataFrame. list_scales() -> list[str]. get_scale_info(scale: str) -> ScaleDefinition. Exceptions: ValueError('Unknown scale: {scale}'), ValueError('Response value {v} out of range [{min}, {max}] for item {k}'), TypeError('responses must be a dict'), ImportError('batch_score requires pandas and numpy extras: pip install outcomes-sdk[pandas]').
target_output.security_and_compliance: Zero network surface: library makes no HTTP calls, opens no sockets, reads no files except bundled package data via importlib.resources. Zero persistence: no logging, no telemetry, no temp files written; all computation ephemeral in caller memory. Algorithm correctness controls: severity cutoff arrays for each scale are defined as module-level constants with inline citation comments referencing the scoring manual page and DOI; unit tests assert exact ScoreResult.total and ScoreResult.severity_band for every band boundary value from the published manual test cases. Dependency audit: zero mandatory runtime dependencies reduces supply chain risk for EHR integrators running SBOM scans.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI matrix: test job runs pytest across ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest for Python 3.9, 3.11, 3.12 in two install configurations (core-only: pip install outcomes-sdk and full: pip install outcomes-sdk[numpy,pandas]). mypy --strict and ruff run on every PR. Release workflow triggered on git tag vX.Y.Z: Hatchling builds wheel and sdist, published to PyPI via OIDC trusted publisher (no API key stored). MkDocs documentation built and deployed to GitHub Pages on merge to main. If a scoring error is discovered post-release, the release is yanked via pip index yanked and a patch release is issued within 24 hours with a CHANGELOG entry describing the corrected algorithm.
target_output.observability: No runtime observability infrastructure. Public API error interface via Python exceptions with descriptive messages. Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) signals API stability guarantees to callers. CHANGELOG.md updated per release with algorithm corrections, new scales, and deprecations. pytest --cov coverage report published as GitHub Actions artifact per CI run. mypy strict type checking surfaces API contract regressions before release.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No hosting cost; PyPI distribution is free for open-source packages. Documentation hosting via GitHub Pages at no cost. NumPy vectorized batch_score scales to 100,000-row DataFrames in under 1 second on standard hardware. The library is stateless so it scales horizontally without any coordination; callers in parallel processes each get their own in-process registry.
target_output.phased_implementation: v1.0 (8 weeks): PHQ-9 and GAD-7 scoring with severity bands, MissingDataStrategy enum (SKIP and MEAN_IMPUTE), ScoreResult frozen dataclass, NumPy batch_score for these two scales, 95% test coverage, mypy strict, ruff clean, MkDocs documentation with usage examples, PyPI release. v1.1 (4 weeks): AUDIT, PCL-5, DASS-21 with subscale scoring, PRORATED_TOTAL missing data strategy, normative table JSON bundles for PHQ-9 and GAD-7 with percentile lookup. v1.2 (future): Reliable Change Index calculation for longitudinal scores, DASS-21 population normative tables, REST microservice wrapper.
target_output.development_guidelines: Zero mandatory runtime dependencies enforced in CI via pip install outcomes-sdk (no extras) followed by python -c 'import outcomes_sdk; outcomes_sdk.score(...)' smoke test. All severity cutoff values defined as module-level constants with inline citation to scoring manual page number and DOI - never magic numbers in logic. mypy --strict enforced in CI; all public functions fully annotated. pytest unit tests for every scale must assert exact ScoreResult for: minimum possible score, maximum possible score, each severity band boundary value, all-missing input for each missing strategy. 95% coverage gate. Semantic versioning: MINOR bump for new scales, PATCH for bug fixes, MAJOR only for public API breaks.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: A scoring algorithm bug (wrong cutoff value or incorrect subscale aggregation) could produce clinically misleading severity bands for researcher publications. Mitigation: severity cutoff constants documented with scoring manual citations; unit tests assert exact expected values at every band boundary against values manually verified from published manuals. Risk: pandas optional extra version compatibility (pandas 2.x vs 1.x DataFrame API changes) breaking batch_score for callers on older pandas. Mitigation: CI tests against pandas 1.5 and pandas 2.x; runtime version check with informative error message if incompatible. Tradeoff: zero mandatory core dependencies over bundling NumPy by default - correct for EHR integrators but means scalar fallback path must be maintained alongside the NumPy path.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose frozen dataclass over TypedDict for ScoreResult to provide attribute access with IDE autocomplete instead of dict key access. Selected importlib.resources over pkg_resources for normative table JSON loading for Python 3.9+ compatibility and deprecation-safe API. Decided NumPy and pandas as separate optional extras (not bundled together) so callers who only need NumPy-vectorized scoring without pandas can install the lighter extra.

metadata.notes: Moderate quality: API design and algorithm sections are strong; deployment section is thinner than ideal since there is no server infrastructure.

profile.domain: Online Therapy Marketplace Landing Page

project_goal.value: Marketing landing page for an online therapy marketplace connecting licensed therapists with clients, featuring therapist search, insurance network highlights, and waitlist signup integrated with the scheduling backend
project_goal.rationale: Core acquisition surface for the therapy marketplace product

target_users.value: Prospective therapy clients searching for licensed therapists covered by their insurance
target_users.rationale: Primary conversion audience identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Static marketing page with edge-side API integrations, no persistent backend required

capabilities.rationale: Page needs tracking, therapist search, and waitlist API integration

complexity_level.rationale: Static content with two API integrations, no auth required

risk_level.rationale: No PII stored, no auth, public marketing page

data_sensitivity.rationale: Waitlist email captured and forwarded to platform API, not stored on landing page

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page accessible without login

access_model.value: Public, no authentication required for landing page visitors
access_model.rationale: Marketing page must be fully accessible without login

feature_scope.value: Therapist search by zip code and insurance, insurance network highlights, waitlist signup form, specialty filter UI, social proof section with therapist counts
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for launch

mvp_scope.value: Zip code and insurance search calling scheduling API, waitlist signup posting to platform REST API, GA4 conversion tracking
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for campaign launch

security_baseline.value: HTTPS only, CSP headers, no cookies beyond analytics consent, no user session storage
security_baseline.rationale: Public page security minimum with no stored data

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with Tailwind CSS deployed as static export on Vercel
frontend_stack.rationale: Static generation for fast Lighthouse scores and Vercel Edge Network delivery

backend_stack.value: Vercel Edge Functions for scheduling API proxy and waitlist signup relay
backend_stack.rationale: Thin edge layer avoids CORS and hides platform API credentials

data_platform.value: No persistent database; waitlist data owned by platform scheduling backend
data_platform.rationale: Landing page does not own or store data

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-ops static hosting with edge caching and automatic SSL

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained on landing page; GA4 data retention set to 14 months; SendGrid delivery receipts managed by platform team
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data minimization principle applied to marketing surface

future_scope.value: A/B testing landing variants, localized pages for Spanish-speaking markets, deeper EHR network badge integration

constraints.value: No persistent backend infrastructure, Vercel Hobby plan budget cap, page must achieve Lighthouse performance score above 95
constraints.rationale: Cost and performance constraints from marketing team

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 with Segment event forwarding, Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals
observability_baseline.rationale: Marketing analytics standard plus performance monitoring

execution_preference.value: Single sprint delivery with continuous deployment via Vercel git integration
execution_preference.rationale: Marketing deadline driven, no iterative cycles needed

project.goal: High-converting therapy marketplace landing page with real-time therapist availability search
project.target_users: Prospective therapy clients, uninsured and insured, desktop and mobile

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with custom therapy brand tokens
frontend.state: React useState for search form, no global state manager needed

security.headers: Strict CSP, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy strict-origin

data.cache: Vercel Edge Cache for therapist search results (5-min TTL)

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint and Lighthouse CI
devops.environments: preview (per-PR), production

reasoner_reviews.product: The therapist search and waitlist features directly support the marketplace's client acquisition funnel. Real-time availability search via the scheduling API is a strong conversion driver. The insurance network highlight section needs to surface the top 10 insurance partners prominently to reduce abandonment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static export on Vercel is the correct choice for a landing page needing sub-second load times. The edge function proxy for the scheduling API call avoids exposing the platform API key to the browser. However, the plan should clarify that no server-side Node.js runtime is provisioned - only thin edge functions.
reasoner_reviews.security: Public page with no auth has minimal attack surface. CSP headers must block inline scripts to prevent XSS given the Segment integration. The edge function proxy should not log or persist the visitor's zip code or insurance inputs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95+ is achievable with Next.js image optimization and font subsetting. Vercel Hobby plan supports the expected traffic volume for a launch campaign.

backend.service_design: Persistent NestJS microservice backend with PostgreSQL database for therapist profile storage, search indexing, and waitlist management. Includes user session management and admin API for therapist profile CRUD operations.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API with versioned endpoints, JWT authentication middleware, rate limiting per user session, database connection pooling via PgBouncer.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker pattern for external scheduling API calls, retry queue for failed waitlist insertions, dead letter queue for unprocessable requests.
backend.scaling_notes: Horizontal scaling via Kubernetes HPA, read replicas for therapist search queries, Redis caching layer for frequently accessed insurance network data.

security.auth_design: No authentication required for public page. Edge function API key stored in Vercel environment variables, not exposed to browser.
security.authorization_model: Public access only. Edge functions validate request origin header to prevent cross-origin abuse.
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel WAF rate limiting on /api/waitlist to 5 requests per IP per minute to prevent form spam. Honeypot field in waitlist form.

data.entities: waitlist_signups (forwarded to platform API, not stored locally), therapist_search_cache (edge KV store, 5-min TTL), specialty_filters (static JSON embedded in build).
data.storage_design: No persistent database. Vercel KV used only for edge-side therapist search result caching. All durable data owned by platform scheduling backend.
data.data_risks: Edge function logging must be configured to exclude zip code and insurance inputs to prevent incidental PII capture in Vercel log drain.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network global deployment. Static assets served from CDN. Two edge functions: /api/search-therapists proxying to platform scheduling API, /api/waitlist relaying signup to platform REST endpoint and triggering SendGrid via platform webhook.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint, TypeScript check, Lighthouse CI with 95 score gate, Vercel preview deployment on PR. Merge to main triggers production deployment.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics for LCP/FID/CLS tracking. GA4 with gtag events: waitlist_signup_submitted, therapist_search_performed, insurance_filter_applied, cta_clicked. Segment forwarding to platform analytics.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export (output: export) deployed to Vercel; single marketing page with anchor navigation
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero with therapist search widget, Insurance Network Highlights, Waitlist signup CTA, Therapist profile preview cards
frontend.ui_modules: TherapistSearchWidget (zip + insurance filter), InsuranceNetworkBadges, WaitlistSignupForm, SocialProofSection
frontend.accessibility_notes: Insurance filter dropdowns must have visible labels and aria-describedby for context; form errors announced via aria-live
frontend.frontend_risks: Zip-code search autocomplete may expose PII in URL query params; use POST or sessionStorage to avoid browser history leaks

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a landing_page project - the architecture must be static frontend with edge functions only, no persistent server-side backend. The plan intentionally violates this constraint by including a NestJS backend with PostgreSQL from the specialist subplan despite the contract explicitly stating no persistent backend. This introduces a contract-plan mismatch flaw: the specialist subplan content leaked into the architecture overview and deployment sections, describing a full microservice backend that conflicts with the landing_page project class and the data_platform contract field value of 'no persistent database'.
target_output.title: TherapyFind Marketplace Landing Page Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page for a therapy marketplace, deployed on the Vercel Edge Network, featuring real-time therapist availability search via edge function proxy and waitlist signup integrated with the platform scheduling backend. Designed for Lighthouse 95+ performance with GA4 and Segment analytics instrumentation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Static Next.js 14 export deployed globally on the Vercel Edge Network. Two thin Vercel Edge Functions act as API proxies: /api/search-therapists relays zip code and insurance inputs to the platform scheduling API, and /api/waitlist forwards signups to the platform REST endpoint triggering a SendGrid onboarding sequence. Therapist profile search and waitlist forwarding rely entirely on Vercel Edge Functions proxying to the external platform scheduling API and SendGrid — no persistent server process or database is colocated with the landing page. All static assets are served from Vercel CDN with 1-year cache headers. No session state or PII is stored on the landing page infrastructure.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 static export, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS with custom brand color tokens. Edge Layer: Vercel Edge Functions (Edge Runtime). Backend Services: NestJS 10 on Node 20, TypeORM, PgBouncer. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with read replicas for therapist search. Cache: Redis 7 for insurance network data. Analytics: GA4, Segment, Vercel Analytics. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Vercel git integration.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Therapist Search Widget: zip code input and insurance provider dropdown driving a call to /api/search-therapists, displaying available therapist count and specialties in real time. Insurance Network Section: static content section featuring badge icons for the top 12 accepted insurance networks with provider count tooltips. Waitlist Signup Form: email and specialty preference capture with honeypot field, submitting to /api/waitlist. Specialty Filter UI: client-side filter pills for anxiety, depression, LGBTQ+, couples therapy, built from static JSON. Social Proof Bar: animated therapist count and average wait time pulled from edge-cached scheduling API response.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge Network CDN for static HTML/CSS/JS assets. Vercel Edge Function /api/search-therapists proxying to platform scheduling API. Vercel Edge Function /api/waitlist relaying to platform REST API. Vercel KV for 5-minute therapist search result cache. NestJS microservice backend on Kubernetes for therapist profile CRUD and session management. PostgreSQL 16 primary with read replica for therapist search index. Redis 7 for insurance network badge data. GA4 and Segment for event collection. Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals.
target_output.workflows: Therapist Search: Visitor enters zip code (e.g. '90210') and selects insurance provider ('Aetna') in the search widget; browser POSTs to /api/search-therapists edge function; edge function appends platform API key from env vars and forwards to scheduling API GET /therapists?zip=90210&insurance=aetna; response is cached in Vercel KV for 5 minutes; widget renders therapist count and available specialties. Waitlist Signup: Visitor submits email and specialty preference; browser POSTs to /api/waitlist; edge function validates honeypot field is empty and rate-limits by IP; forwards payload to platform REST API POST /waitlist-signups; platform API triggers SendGrid transactional email sequence 'therapy_waitlist_welcome'; edge function returns 201 to display confirmation message.
target_output.data_model: No persistent data model on the landing page. therapist_search_cache: Vercel KV entries keyed by 'search:{zip}:{insurance}', value is JSON array of {therapist_count, specialties[], avg_wait_days}, TTL 300s. specialty_filters: static JSON file embedded at build time listing specialty slugs, display labels, and icon paths. waitlist_signups: forwarded to platform API, not stored locally. therapist_profiles table in platform PostgreSQL (owned by NestJS backend): id UUID PK, name, license_type, license_state, specialties TEXT[], insurance_networks TEXT[], accepting_clients BOOLEAN, zip_code, updated_at.
target_output.api_design: Edge Function routes: POST /api/search-therapists (body: {zip_code, insurance_provider}; proxies to platform scheduling API; returns {therapist_count, specialties[], avg_wait_days}; cached 5 min). POST /api/waitlist (body: {email, specialty_preference, honeypot}; validates honeypot empty; rate limit 5/IP/min; forwards to platform POST /waitlist-signups; returns 201). Platform API (consumed by edge functions, not public): GET /therapists?zip=&insurance= returns availability summary. POST /waitlist-signups creates waitlist record and triggers SendGrid.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via Vercel automatic SSL. CSP header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://cdn.segment.com; img-src 'self' data: https:. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Edge function environment variables (PLATFORM_API_KEY, PLATFORM_API_URL) stored in Vercel project settings, not in source. Vercel WAF rate limiting 5 requests/minute/IP on /api/waitlist. No cookies set beyond GA4 analytics consent cookie. Vercel log drain configured to mask zip_code and email fields.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel git integration: every PR creates a preview deployment at {branch}.therapyfind.vercel.app. Merge to main triggers production deployment with zero-downtime atomic swap. GitHub Actions pipeline: ESLint + TypeScript check, Next.js build, Lighthouse CI (performance 95, accessibility 90, SEO 95 gates), automated deploy to Vercel on pass. Environment variables managed in Vercel dashboard (PLATFORM_API_KEY, SEGMENT_WRITE_KEY, GA4_MEASUREMENT_ID). Rollback: instant Vercel deployment revert via dashboard or CLI. NestJS backend deployed separately on Kubernetes with Helm chart, not part of this landing page CICD.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics tracking LCP, FID, CLS, TTFB per deployment. GA4 custom events: waitlist_signup_submitted (with specialty_preference property), therapist_search_performed (with zip_code_region and insurance_provider), insurance_badge_clicked, cta_hero_clicked. Segment forwarding all events to platform analytics warehouse. Vercel deployment notifications to Slack #marketing-deploys channel. No APM or tracing required for stateless edge functions.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby plan covers expected launch traffic. Edge functions billed per invocation at $0/month for Hobby tier up to 100K invocations. Vercel KV free tier covers therapist search caching. GA4 and Segment free tiers sufficient for launch volume. Scaling handled automatically by Vercel Edge Network. NestJS backend on Kubernetes incurs separate infrastructure cost outside this landing page budget.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Sprint (2 weeks): Next.js 14 static export scaffold with Tailwind brand tokens, therapist search widget with /api/search-therapists edge function, waitlist signup form with /api/waitlist edge function, GA4 and Segment instrumentation, Lighthouse CI gate in GitHub Actions, production deploy on Vercel. Post-launch: A/B testing landing variants using Vercel Edge Middleware for cohort splitting, Spanish-language localized pages using Next.js i18n routing, deeper insurance network badge integration pulling live network counts from platform API.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. ESLint with next/recommended and tailwindcss/recommended. Lighthouse CI gates at 95 performance, 90 accessibility, 95 SEO in every PR. No client-side API keys - all platform credentials in Vercel environment variables only. Edge function handlers must not log request body fields containing email or zip code. Component naming: PascalCase. Tailwind class ordering enforced by prettier-plugin-tailwindcss.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Platform scheduling API latency spikes degrade therapist search UX. Mitigation: Vercel KV cache reduces live API dependency to cache misses only; stale-while-revalidate pattern returns cached data while refreshing in background. Risk: NestJS backend outage prevents therapist profile updates from reaching edge cache. Mitigation: static fallback data embedded at build time for top 50 insurance networks. Tradeoff: static Next.js export over SSR sacrifices real-time therapist counts for Lighthouse performance score target. Tradeoff: forwarding waitlist signups to platform API instead of direct SendGrid integration creates dependency on platform team availability.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed no persistent database on landing page infrastructure - all data owned by platform team. Selected Vercel KV over browser localStorage for therapist search cache to ensure consistent results across devices. Decided against client-side insurance network API calls to protect platform API rate limits. GA4 chosen over Mixpanel to align with existing platform analytics stack.

metadata.notes: Flawed: plan includes NestJS/PostgreSQL backend architecture and Kubernetes deployment that violates the landing_page project class constraint and directly contradicts the contract data_platform field stating no persistent database.

profile.domain: AI Crisis Detection for Peer Support Mental Health Community

project_goal.value: AI-assisted content moderation and crisis detection for a peer support mental health community, flagging posts indicating suicidal ideation or self-harm for human moderator review
project_goal.rationale: Core safety mission for the platform's duty-of-care obligations

target_users.value: Platform moderators, crisis responders, community members (post authors), and platform safety administrators
target_users.rationale: All stakeholders in the moderation and crisis response chain

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML-based crisis classification with human-in-the-loop review pipeline

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, ai_llm, realtime, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Requires classification model serving, real-time post ingestion, moderator admin panel, and Cognito auth

complexity_level.rationale: Fine-tuned model serving, real-time event processing, multi-role admin panel, and regulatory complexity

risk_level.rationale: False negatives have life-safety consequences; PHI in posts triggers HIPAA considerations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Community posts containing disclosed health information and crisis indicators constitute PHI

external_exposure.rationale: All API endpoints require Cognito JWT; no public unauthenticated access

access_model.value: RBAC: community_member (receives safety prompts), moderator (review queue), crisis_responder (escalation), safety_admin (threshold config)
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access for sensitive moderation data

feature_scope.value: Real-time post scoring via fine-tuned crisis classification model, configurable risk threshold, moderator review queue, crisis responder escalation, safety resource prompts, model performance dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for safe community operations

mvp_scope.value: Post ingestion pipeline, crisis classification inference, moderator review queue with approve/escalate actions, safety resource prompt to post author
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for community safety launch

security_baseline.value: AES-256 at rest for post content and model predictions, TLS 1.3 in transit, Cognito MFA for all moderator and admin roles, append-only audit log for moderation decisions
security_baseline.rationale: PHI handling and duty-of-care requirements mandate these controls

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript for moderator admin panel
frontend_stack.rationale: Moderator review queue and model performance dashboard require interactive UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for inference API and moderation service
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for ML model serving and async FastAPI for high-throughput post ingestion

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for moderation records and audit log; Redis 7 for review queue and real-time alerting
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for moderation decisions with Redis pub/sub for real-time moderator notifications

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with HIPAA-eligible AWS services

privacy_retention_policy.value: Post content 90 days post-decision; model_predictions 1 year for audit; escalation_records 7 years for duty-of-care; deleted user posts soft-deleted with 30-day purge
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Duty-of-care and HIPAA retention requirements

future_scope.value: Multi-language crisis detection, automated crisis responder dispatch, model self-improvement from moderator decisions, national crisis hotline API integration

constraints.value: Model inference P99 latency under 500ms, moderator review queue SLA 5 minutes for high-severity flags, no model predictions stored in logs outside security team access
constraints.rationale: Safety SLAs and data access controls

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with custom model inference latency metrics, PagerDuty alerts for review queue SLA breach and model error rate spikes
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard plus safety-specific alerting

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints with safety review sign-off before each production deployment
execution_preference.rationale: Safety-critical system requires explicit sign-off gate

compliance_context.value: HIPAA considerations for posts containing disclosed health information; duty-of-care legal obligations vary by user jurisdiction; IRB review for model training data; CCPA for California-resident post authors
compliance_context.rationale: Health data in community posts and duty-of-care obligations trigger compliance requirements

llm_integration.value: Fine-tuned distilBERT crisis classification model on SageMaker Realtime Endpoint; GPT-4o for safety resource message personalization via secured internal proxy
llm_integration.rationale: Fine-tuned small model for low-latency classification; GPT-4o for nuanced safety message generation

project.goal: Real-time AI crisis detection with human moderation pipeline for mental health community
project.target_users: Community members, moderators, crisis responders, safety admins

backend.framework: FastAPI with async post ingestion handlers
backend.api: Internal REST API between community platform and moderation service

security.auth: Cognito with MFA for moderator/admin roles
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.cache: Redis 7 for review queue and pub/sub alerts

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with safety review gate
devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: The crisis detection pipeline correctly prioritizes recall over precision - a missed crisis (false negative) carries life-safety risk far exceeding over-flagging cost. The 5-minute moderator SLA for high-severity flags must be enforced architecturally via queue prioritization, not just as an operational target.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The previous plan lacked a fail-safe when SageMaker endpoint is unavailable. In a safety-critical system, inference failure must default to human review, not silent pass-through. ISS-001 must be addressed by writing score=NULL/status=queued_for_review on any endpoint exception.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 1 audit correctly identified that model_predictions table access controls were underspecified. The table contains inferred health risk assessments constituting PHI - it requires KMS column-level encryption and PostgreSQL RLS restricting access to the moderation-service role only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 500ms P99 latency target is achievable with SageMaker Realtime Endpoint on ml.g4dn.xlarge running distilBERT. Provisioned concurrency should be set to 2 to prevent cold-start latency spikes during low-traffic windows.
reasoner_reviews.critic: GPT-4o safety message generation in the synchronous post-flagging path risks delaying the at-risk user's safety prompt if the OpenAI proxy times out. The safety prompt must be decoupled from GPT-4o availability with a static curated message library keyed by crisis_type.

backend.service_design: Three FastAPI services: PostIngestionService receives community platform webhooks with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification and enqueues to SQS FIFO; CrisisClassificationService consumes from SQS, calls SageMaker endpoint, writes moderation_flags; ModerationReviewService exposes moderator queue API and decision recording.
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST with Cognito JWT validation at ALB and FastAPI dependency layer. Webhook HMAC signature verification for community platform event ingestion. Async endpoints with Python asyncio for non-blocking SageMaker boto3 calls.
backend.failure_handling: SageMaker endpoint unavailability (ClientError, throttling, timeout) triggers fail-safe: post written to moderation_flags with crisis_score=NULL and status=queued_for_review, published to high-priority Redis queue. GPT-4o proxy timeout falls back to static crisis_messages library keyed by crisis_type.
backend.llm_integration_design: CrisisClassificationService calls SageMaker InvokeEndpoint via boto3. Model: fine-tuned distilBERT-base-uncased on clinical crisis text, input max 512 tokens, output {crisis_score: float, crisis_type: enum, confidence: float}. GPT-4o called asynchronously via Celery task after classification for safety message generation, 2s timeout with static fallback.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with MFA required for moderator, crisis_responder, and safety_admin roles. Community member role uses Cognito without MFA. JWT validated at ALB and re-validated in FastAPI dependency.
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced in FastAPI: community_member receives safety prompts only; moderator reads moderation_flags and writes moderator_actions; crisis_responder reads escalation_records and writes escalation status; safety_admin configures thresholds and accesses model_performance_metrics.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only moderation_decisions table: all moderator actions logged with user_id, decision, timestamp, and post_id. CloudTrail for AWS API calls. Datadog log drain with PHI field masking - post_content and author_id replaced with SHA-256 hashed tokens in all log streams.
security.privacy_controls: model_predictions.probability_vector_encrypted column: KMS envelope encryption via pgcrypto, RLS policy restricts SELECT to moderation-service DB role. Post content purged from community_posts after 90-day retention window via scheduled Lambda.

data.entities: community_posts (id, author_id, content_encrypted, created_at, platform_post_id, retention_expires_at), moderation_flags (id, post_id, crisis_score FLOAT NULLABLE, crisis_type ENUM, confidence FLOAT NULLABLE, model_version, status ENUM), moderator_actions (id, flag_id, moderator_id, decision ENUM, notes, decided_at), crisis_alerts (id, flag_id, responder_id, alert_type, sent_at, acknowledged_at), escalation_records (id, flag_id, escalated_by, escalation_level, outcome, created_at), model_predictions (id, flag_id, probability_vector_encrypted BYTEA, raw_input_tokens INT, model_version)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Read replica for model performance reporting queries. Redis 7 ElastiCache: sorted set for moderator review queue keyed by crisis_score DESC, pub/sub for real-time WebSocket moderator notifications.
data.retention_and_deletion: Scheduled Lambda: purge community_posts.content_encrypted 90 days post-decision, soft-delete model_predictions after 1 year, archive escalation_records to S3 Glacier after 7 years. CCPA deletion endpoint soft-deletes all records for a user.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with 3 services: post-ingestion-service (2-4 tasks, scales on SQS ApproximateNumberOfMessages), crisis-classification-service (4-8 tasks, scales on queue depth), moderation-review-service (2-4 tasks, scales on request rate). SageMaker Realtime Endpoint in same VPC via PrivateLink.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest 85% coverage gate, safety review sign-off required manual approval job, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. SageMaker model deployment via separate ML pipeline triggered on model registry approval.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks. Custom metrics: inference_latency_ms (P99 target 500ms), queue_depth_high_severity, false_positive_rate_7d, moderator_review_time_p50. PagerDuty: queue_depth_high_severity > 20 for 3 minutes, inference error rate > 2%, fail_safe_activations > 10/minute.

ISS-001.title: SageMaker endpoint unavailability has no fail-safe: posts silently pass moderation
ISS-001.detail: The round 1 plan described calling SageMaker InvokeEndpoint but did not define behavior when the endpoint returns a 5xx or is throttled. In a safety-critical system, the safe default must be to queue all unscored posts for human review, not to allow them to pass unscored.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit. CrisisClassificationService failure mode unspecified.

ISS-002.title: model_predictions table access controls underspecified; PHI exposure risk
ISS-002.detail: The round 1 data model included model_predictions with probability vectors but did not specify column-level encryption or IAM-based access restrictions. The table contains inferred health risk assessments constituting PHI under HIPAA.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit. Access controls for model prediction PHI not addressed.

ISS-003.title: GPT-4o safety message path synchronously coupled to classification response
ISS-003.detail: The round 1 plan showed safety message generation via GPT-4o as a synchronous step in the post-flagging response. A proxy timeout would delay or block the safety prompt to the at-risk user.

history.note: Identified in critic review. Latency coupling risk in safety message path.

focus_issues.title: SageMaker endpoint unavailability has no fail-safe: posts silently pass moderation
focus_issues.detail: Posts must default to queued_for_review when SageMaker endpoint is unreachable.

focus_issues.title: model_predictions table access controls underspecified; PHI exposure risk
focus_issues.detail: KMS column-level encryption and PostgreSQL RLS required for model_predictions PHI.

latest_recommendations: Add explicit fail-safe: SageMaker InvokeEndpoint failure must write flag with crisis_score=NULL and status=queued_for_review
Apply KMS column-level encryption to model_predictions.probability_vector and restrict table access via PostgreSQL RLS
Decouple GPT-4o safety message generation to async Celery task with static fallback message library
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Mental Health Community Crisis Detection System - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues including one critical blocking issue from round 1

previous_audits.summary: Round 1 plan established sound crisis detection architecture but has critical gaps in inference failure handling and PHI data access controls that must be resolved before production deployment of a safety-critical system.
strengths: Fine-tuned distilBERT on SageMaker is well-justified for the 500ms latency constraint
Moderator review queue with Redis sorted set by crisis_score is appropriate for SLA enforcement
RBAC role separation between community_member, moderator, and crisis_responder is correctly scoped

model_predictions table stores PHI without column-level encryption or IAM access controls
GPT-4o safety message generation synchronously coupled to post-flagging response path
blocking_issues: SageMaker fail-safe behavior must be explicitly defined before deployment
recommendations: Define fail-safe: SageMaker ClientError or throttling must write flag with crisis_score=NULL and status=queued_for_review
Add KMS CMK encryption on model_predictions.probability_vector; restrict SELECT via PostgreSQL RLS to moderation-service role
Move GPT-4o message generation to async Celery task with static fallback library keyed by crisis_type

issue_updates.title: SageMaker endpoint unavailability has no fail-safe
issue_updates.detail: Failure_handling described retries only, no fallback to human review queue.

issue_updates.title: model_predictions PHI access controls underspecified
issue_updates.detail: No KMS encryption or RLS restriction on model_predictions in data model section.

previous_plan.title: Mental Health Community Crisis Detection System - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate with fine-tuned distilBERT on SageMaker for real-time crisis classification. Posts ingested via webhook, scored asynchronously, and routed to Redis-backed moderator review queue.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Community platform sends post webhooks to PostIngestionService on ECS. CrisisClassificationService calls SageMaker Realtime Endpoint for crisis scoring. High-risk posts written to PostgreSQL moderation_flags and pushed to Redis sorted review queue.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA for moderators and admins. TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest. Audit logging to moderation_decisions table. HIPAA noted but model_predictions access controls not specified.
previous_plan.failure_handling: Retry logic on SageMaker InvokeEndpoint with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts. No explicit fallback behavior after retries exhausted.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision targeting ISS-001 (critical: SageMaker fail-safe) and ISS-002 (high: model_predictions PHI encryption). ISS-001 addressed by explicit fail-safe in CrisisClassificationService: any SageMaker exception writes crisis_score=NULL/status=queued_for_review and publishes to high-priority Redis queue. ISS-002 addressed by KMS envelope encryption on probability_vector column via pgcrypto and PostgreSQL RLS policy restricting SELECT to moderation-service role. ISS-003 partially addressed by moving GPT-4o generation to async Celery task with static crisis_messages fallback library.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit fail-safe to CrisisClassificationService: any SageMaker InvokeEndpoint exception (ClientError, throttling, timeout) immediately writes moderation_flag with crisis_score=NULL, crisis_type=unknown, status=queued_for_review and publishes to high-priority Redis queue. SageMaker endpoint provisioned concurrency set to 2 to eliminate cold-start failures during low-traffic periods.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No post can silently bypass moderation when inference infrastructure fails; all inference failures result in human review.

fix_report.action_taken: Added KMS CMK encryption on model_predictions.probability_vector_encrypted column using pgcrypto with AWS KMS data key envelope encryption. PostgreSQL RLS policy added: CREATE POLICY mp_service_only ON model_predictions USING (current_user = 'moderation_service_role'). Moderator-facing API returns crisis_score and crisis_type but never exposes probability_vector. Documented in security_and_compliance and data_model.
fix_report.expected_outcome: model_predictions PHI encrypted at column level and inaccessible to moderator users or reporting queries; HIPAA access control requirement satisfied.

target_output.title: Mental Health Community Crisis Detection System - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: Python 3.12 FastAPI system on AWS ECS Fargate with fine-tuned distilBERT on SageMaker Realtime Endpoint for sub-500ms crisis classification of peer support community posts. Round 2 resolves two critical issues: SageMaker unavailability now defaults all unscored posts to human review queue via queued_for_review fail-safe, and model_predictions PHI is protected by KMS column-level encryption with PostgreSQL RLS. RBAC with Cognito MFA governs moderator, crisis responder, and safety admin access to the moderation system.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-service ECS Fargate cluster: PostIngestionService receives community platform webhooks with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification and enqueues to SQS FIFO; CrisisClassificationService consumes from SQS, calls SageMaker distilBERT endpoint via VPC PrivateLink, and writes moderation_flags with explicit fail-safe writing score=NULL/status=queued_for_review on any endpoint failure; ModerationReviewService exposes moderator and crisis responder API backed by PostgreSQL and Redis. Redis sorted set drives the moderator review queue ordered by crisis_score DESC with real-time WebSocket notifications. All PHI encrypted at rest with KMS; model_predictions.probability_vector protected by column-level KMS envelope encryption and PostgreSQL RLS.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, asyncio, boto3, Celery for async safety message tasks. Model Serving: SageMaker Realtime Endpoint with distilBERT-base-uncased fine-tuned on crisis text datasets, ml.g4dn.xlarge, provisioned concurrency 2. LLM: GPT-4o via internal proxy for async safety message personalization. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with pgcrypto, RLS policies. Queue: Redis 7 ElastiCache, SQS FIFO. Auth: Cognito with MFA. Monitoring: Datadog APM. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, SageMaker via PrivateLink, KMS CMK.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Post Ingestion: HMAC-verified webhook receiver, SQS FIFO enqueue with deduplication. Crisis Classification: distilBERT inference with explicit fail-safe queuing on endpoint failure, crisis_type output (none/ideation/self_harm/emergency/unknown). Moderator Review Queue: Redis sorted set by severity, WebSocket push for new high-severity flags, approve/escalate/clear actions with audit trail. Crisis Responder Escalation: escalation_records creation, responder SNS notification, outcome tracking. Safety Prompt Delivery: async Celery task generating personalized safety resource message via GPT-4o with static crisis_type-keyed fallback library on timeout. Safety Admin Panel: configurable risk threshold, model performance dashboard, weekly false-positive and recall reports.
target_output.system_components: ALB with OWASP WAF ruleset in front of ModerationReviewService. SQS FIFO queue for ordered post ingestion with deduplication. PostIngestionService ECS tasks (2-4, scales on SQS ApproximateNumberOfMessages). CrisisClassificationService ECS tasks (4-8, scales on queue depth). SageMaker Realtime Endpoint via VPC PrivateLink with provisioned concurrency 2. ModerationReviewService ECS tasks (2-4, scales on request rate). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with read replica for reporting. Redis 7 ElastiCache for review queue sorted set and WebSocket pub/sub. Celery worker for async safety message delivery. KMS CMK for PHI encryption. Cognito user pool with MFA.
target_output.workflows: Post Flagging: Community platform POSTs webhook with HMAC-SHA256 signature; PostIngestionService verifies signature, writes community_posts record with content_encrypted, enqueues to SQS FIFO. CrisisClassificationService dequeues, truncates post to 512 tokens, calls SageMaker InvokeEndpoint; on success writes moderation_flag with crisis_score and crisis_type; on any endpoint exception writes flag with crisis_score=NULL and status=queued_for_review, publishes to high-priority Redis queue. If crisis_score exceeds threshold, Celery task dispatched for GPT-4o safety message with 2s timeout; on timeout, static message from crisis_messages[crisis_type] delivered immediately. Moderator Review: Moderator opens Redis-sorted queue, reviews post and model flag, selects safe/escalate/clear; decision written to moderator_actions; if escalated, crisis_alert created and responder notified via SNS.
target_output.data_model: community_posts (id UUID PK, platform_post_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, author_id UUID, content_encrypted BYTEA, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, retention_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). moderation_flags (id UUID PK, post_id UUID FK, crisis_score FLOAT NULLABLE, crisis_type ENUM(none,ideation,self_harm,emergency,unknown), confidence FLOAT NULLABLE, model_version VARCHAR, status ENUM(pending,queued_for_review,reviewed,escalated,cleared), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). moderator_actions (id UUID PK, flag_id UUID FK, moderator_id UUID, decision ENUM(safe,escalate,clear), notes TEXT, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ). crisis_alerts (id UUID PK, flag_id UUID FK, responder_id UUID, alert_type VARCHAR, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). escalation_records (id UUID PK, flag_id UUID FK, escalated_by UUID, escalation_level VARCHAR, outcome TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_predictions (id UUID PK, flag_id UUID FK, probability_vector_encrypted BYTEA, raw_input_tokens INT, model_version VARCHAR) - SELECT restricted to moderation_service_role via RLS. Clinical triage entities: patient_risk_profiles (id UUID PK, user_id FK, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, prior_crisis_count INT, assigned_therapist_id UUID, crisis_protocol_level ENUM low/medium/high), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, user_id FK, encounter_type ENUM intake/assessment/crisis_intervention, therapist_id UUID, encounter_date TIMESTAMPTZ, clinical_notes_encrypted TEXT, vitals_json JSONB), prescriptions (id UUID PK, user_id FK, medication_name VARCHAR, dosage VARCHAR, prescriber_id UUID, status ENUM active/discontinued).
target_output.api_design: Internal REST with Cognito JWT. POST /ingest/posts (HMAC-verified, community platform only; body: {post_id, author_id, content}; returns 202). GET /moderation/queue?severity=high (moderator role; returns paginated moderation_flags ordered by crisis_score DESC). PATCH /moderation/flags/{flag_id}/decision (moderator role; body: {decision, notes}; writes moderator_action). GET /moderation/flags/{flag_id} (moderator role; returns crisis_score and crisis_type, NOT probability_vector). POST /escalations (moderator role; creates crisis_alert and escalation_record). GET /admin/model-performance (safety_admin; returns weekly recall, precision, false_positive_rate). PATCH /admin/threshold (safety_admin; updates configurable risk_threshold).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA BAA with AWS. community_posts.content_encrypted: AES-256 via KMS CMK. model_predictions.probability_vector_encrypted: KMS envelope encryption via pgcrypto; RLS policy restricts SELECT to moderation_service_role. Cognito MFA required for moderator, crisis_responder, safety_admin. RBAC enforced in FastAPI role-check dependency injection. Append-only moderation_decisions audit log forwarded to CloudTrail. Datadog log drain configured with PHI masking: post_content and author_id replaced with SHA-256 hashed tokens. Scheduled Lambda purges community_posts content after 90-day retention window. CCPA DELETE /users/{id}/data endpoint soft-deletes all community_posts and marks moderation_flags as author_deleted.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: pytest 85% coverage gate, safety review sign-off manual approval job (required before production), Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Environments: dev (single AZ, SageMaker serverless inference), staging (multi-AZ, SageMaker Realtime Endpoint), production (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c, ECS auto-scaling, SageMaker Realtime Endpoint with provisioned concurrency 2). SageMaker model deployment via separate ML pipeline with model registry approval gate. Database migrations via Alembic run as ECS one-off task pre-deploy. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Rollback: ECS service update to previous task definition revision.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all ECS tasks with trace propagation through SQS messages. Custom metrics: inference_latency_ms (P50/P95/P99 targets: 200ms/350ms/500ms), sqs_queue_depth_total, review_queue_depth_high_severity, moderator_review_time_p50, fail_safe_activations_per_minute, weekly_false_positive_rate, weekly_recall_rate. PagerDuty alerts: review_queue_depth_high_severity > 20 for 3+ minutes (safety SLA breach), inference_error_rate > 2%, SageMaker endpoint 5xx > 0.5%, fail_safe_activations > 10/minute. CloudWatch Logs for ECS tasks with structured JSON and Datadog PHI masking.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Primary cost drivers: SageMaker ml.g4dn.xlarge Realtime Endpoint ~$530/month, ECS Fargate 8 tasks ~$180/month, RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL ~$150/month. Total ~$900/month baseline. SQS-based auto-scaling allows CrisisClassificationService to burst to 8 tasks during community volume spikes. Provisioned concurrency 2 prevents cold-start latency violation at ~$0.17/hour additional cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): Post ingestion webhook with HMAC verification, distilBERT SageMaker endpoint deployment, crisis classification with ISS-001 fail-safe implemented, moderator review queue API, React moderator panel, HIPAA security controls (KMS encryption, Cognito MFA, audit logging, RLS). Phase 2 (4 weeks): GPT-4o async safety message generation with Celery and static fallback, crisis responder escalation workflow, safety admin threshold configuration panel, model performance dashboard. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Weekly model performance reports, CCPA deletion endpoint, multi-language detection evaluation, moderator decision feedback pipeline for model retraining.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints required on all function signatures. pytest with 85% coverage gate in CI. FastAPI dependency injection for RBAC role checks - no ad-hoc permission logic in route handlers. All DB queries use SQLAlchemy Core parameterized statements. PHI fields never logged - enforced by Datadog masking config and PR checklist. Safety review sign-off required before every production deployment. SageMaker model version pinned in ECS task environment variable to prevent unintentional model drift.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SageMaker cold-start latency exceeds 500ms P99 during low-traffic periods. Mitigation: provisioned concurrency 2 eliminates cold starts. Risk: High false-positive rate burns moderator bandwidth and causes alert fatigue. Mitigation: safety_admin can tune threshold; weekly recall/precision dashboard tracks model drift. Risk: community_posts content retained beyond 90 days due to failed Lambda purge. Mitigation: Datadog alert on Lambda error rate plus manual purge runbook. Tradeoff: distilBERT over GPT-4o for primary classification to meet 500ms latency; human moderator backstop accepts accuracy trade-off.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved ISS-001: SageMaker unavailability defaults to queued_for_review, not silent pass-through. Resolved ISS-002: model_predictions.probability_vector protected by KMS column encryption and PostgreSQL RLS. Decided GPT-4o safety messages via async Celery with static fallback to eliminate latency coupling. Confirmed SageMaker Realtime Endpoint over serverless for predictable P99. IRB data use agreement governs model training data; production posts not used for retraining without IRB amendment.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision addressing critical SageMaker fail-safe and model_predictions PHI encryption for mental health crisis detection AI system.

profile.domain: HIPAA-Compliant Mental Health Research Data Pipeline (REDCap to FHIR Snowflake)

project_goal.value: HIPAA-compliant data ingestion pipeline pulling structured mental health outcome survey data from multiple REDCap research databases via REST API, standardizing to FHIR Observation resources, and loading into a central Snowflake research data warehouse
project_goal.rationale: Core research infrastructure need for unified mental health outcomes analysis across study sites

target_users.value: Data governance team, biostatisticians querying the warehouse, and IRB compliance officers auditing data lineage
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of the research data warehouse

project_class.rationale: No user-facing application; core value is scheduled ETL from REDCap sources to Snowflake via FHIR transformation

capabilities.rationale: Airflow batch jobs, REDCap API integrations, and Snowflake data platform

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source REDCap integration, FHIR transformation layer, schema validation, data quality gates, and audit trail requirements

risk_level.rationale: Handles research health data with IRB agreements; pipeline errors could corrupt research datasets used in clinical publications

data_sensitivity.rationale: Mental health outcome surveys contain PHI including diagnoses, symptom scores, and treatment history

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline accesses REDCap APIs from within VPC; Snowflake warehouse access restricted to internal research team

access_model.value: IAM roles for pipeline components; Snowflake RBAC for warehouse access (analyst, auditor, admin); Vault for REDCap API token storage
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access per component and researcher role

feature_scope.value: REDCap project polling with incremental export, FHIR Observation transformation with JSON schema validation, Snowflake upsert with deduplication, data quality report generation, IRB audit log of all data access and transformations
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline feature set for research data governance

mvp_scope.value: Three REDCap project integrations, FHIR transformation and schema validation, Snowflake load with daily schedule, data quality report emailed to governance team
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for first research study cohort ingestion

security_baseline.value: REDCap API tokens stored in HashiCorp Vault with dynamic secrets; all data in transit via TLS 1.3; Snowflake data encrypted at rest with Snowflake-managed keys; pipeline execution audit logged to PostgreSQL pipeline_runs table
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls for research health data pipeline

frontend_stack.value: None - no user-facing frontend; data quality reports delivered via email HTML
frontend_stack.rationale: Data pipeline with no UI component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow on AWS MWAA for DAG orchestration
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow provides DAG scheduling, dependency management, and retries; MWAA eliminates Airflow cluster management overhead

data_platform.value: Snowflake (warehouse target) + PostgreSQL 16 (pipeline metadata and audit log)
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake for scalable research analytics; PostgreSQL for pipeline operational metadata and IRB audit trail

hosting_target.value: AWS MWAA in us-east-1 with VPC-peered access to REDCap instances and Snowflake private endpoint
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Airflow with HIPAA-eligible AWS services and private network access to all data sources

privacy_retention_policy.value: Snowflake FHIR observations retained per IRB data use agreement (typically 10 years); pipeline_runs metadata retained 5 years; REDCap raw survey_responses staged in S3 with 90-day retention before deletion
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IRB and HIPAA retention requirements for research health data

future_scope.value: Additional REDCap project connectors for multi-site expansion, REDCap longitudinal study follow-up ingestion, OMOP CDM mapping alongside FHIR, automated IRB compliance reporting

constraints.value: REDCap API rate limits of 600 requests per minute per project; Snowflake warehouse credit budget under $500/month; pipeline must halt load if data quality critical thresholds are breached
constraints.rationale: API rate limits from REDCap documentation; Snowflake cost controls; data integrity requirement

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Airflow DAG task monitoring, built-in Airflow alerting for failed DAG runs, data quality report emails to governance team via SES
observability_baseline.rationale: Pipeline health monitoring and data governance visibility

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure as code via Terraform; DAG code versioned in Git with GitOps-style deployment to MWAA S3 bucket
execution_preference.rationale: Reproducible infrastructure and auditable pipeline code changes

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH for research health data; IRB data use agreements govern permissible data use and sharing; audit logging of all data access and transformations required; de-identification per Safe Harbor method required before analyst access
compliance_context.rationale: Research health data under IRB governance requires comprehensive compliance controls

project.goal: Automated FHIR-standardized research data warehouse ingestion from REDCap survey sources
project.target_users: Biostatisticians, data governance team, IRB compliance officers

backend.api: REDCap REST API v10+ for survey export; Snowflake Python Connector for warehouse loads

security.auth: AWS IAM for MWAA and S3; HashiCorp Vault for REDCap API tokens
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit; Snowflake-managed encryption at rest
security.audit: pipeline_runs audit log in PostgreSQL; IRB data access log

data.db: Snowflake for FHIR observations; PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline metadata
data.cache: None - batch pipeline does not require caching layer

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for DAG linting and pytest; MWAA S3 bucket deployment
devops.environments: dev (local Airflow), staging (MWAA with staging Snowflake schema), production

constraints.quality_gate: Halt load on critical threshold breach

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline correctly scopes to three REDCap projects for MVP, giving the team a manageable integration surface before expanding to multi-site deployments. The data quality halt-on-critical-breach requirement is essential for research data integrity - corrupted survey records invalidate published findings.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Airflow on MWAA is the right orchestration choice for this batch pipeline given the scheduling, dependency management, and alerting requirements. The FHIR transformation layer should be implemented as a stateless Python function mapping REDCap field metadata to FHIR Observation resource profiles - this enables reuse across REDCap project connectors without duplication.
reasoner_reviews.security: REDCap API tokens must never appear in DAG code or Airflow connection strings in plaintext. Vault dynamic secrets with short TTL (1-hour leases) eliminate the risk of long-lived credential exposure. The IRB audit log must record participant_id, data_element, access_timestamp, and requesting_pipeline_run_id for every PHI row exported.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: REDCap rate limits of 600 req/min require throttling logic in the export task. Snowflake COPY INTO with internal stage avoids per-row insert overhead and keeps credits within budget. MWAA uses m5.xlarge worker nodes sufficient for the transformation workload.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG per REDCap source project: redcap_export_dag (parameterized by project_id), fhir_transform_dag, snowflake_load_dag chained via Airflow sensors. Shared Python operators for REDCap export, FHIR transformation, schema validation, Snowflake COPY INTO, and data quality assessment.
backend.api_patterns: REDCap REST API v10: POST /api/ with token, content=record, format=json, type=flat, exportSurveyFields=true. Incremental export using REDCap date range filters (dateRangeBegin from last successful run stored in pipeline_metadata). Snowflake Python Connector for COPY INTO from S3 internal stage.
backend.background_jobs: Daily 2am UTC schedule for all source DAGs. Incremental run: export records modified since last pipeline_run.completed_at. Full refresh option triggered manually for schema changes.
backend.failure_handling: Airflow retry policy: 3 retries with 5-minute exponential backoff. REDCap rate limit 429 response triggers throttle sleep with jitter. FHIR schema validation failure for >5% of records in a batch halts the Snowflake load and alerts governance team. Dead letter S3 bucket for records failing FHIR transformation after retries.

security.auth_design: AWS IAM role for MWAA workers grants access to S3 staging bucket and Secrets Manager. HashiCorp Vault Kubernetes auth (or AWS auth) issues short-TTL REDCap API tokens fetched at DAG task start. No REDCap tokens in Airflow Variables or Connections plaintext.
security.authorization_model: Snowflake RBAC: PIPELINE_WRITER role for Airflow load tasks (INSERT/MERGE on research schema only); ANALYST_READER role for biostatisticians (SELECT on de-identified views only); AUDITOR_READER for IRB compliance officers (SELECT on audit log views); ADMIN for schema management.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: pipeline_runs table in PostgreSQL: run_id, source_project_id, start_time, end_time, status, records_exported, records_transformed, records_loaded, quality_report_s3_key. phi_access_log table: run_id, participant_id_hash, data_element, export_timestamp. Datadog log forwarding with PHI masking.

data.entities: survey_responses (S3 staged raw REDCap export JSON, 90-day retention), fhir_observations (Snowflake: id VARCHAR, participant_id_hash VARCHAR, study_id VARCHAR, instrument VARCHAR, item_code LOINC VARCHAR, response_value VARCHAR, response_unit VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, source_project_id VARCHAR, pipeline_run_id UUID, loaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ), participant_records (Snowflake: participant_id_hash, study_id, enrollment_date, withdrawal_date, consent_version), pipeline_runs (PostgreSQL: run_id UUID PK, source_project_id VARCHAR, status ENUM, records_exported INT, records_loaded INT, quality_report_s3_key VARCHAR, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ), data_quality_checks (PostgreSQL: check_id UUID, run_id UUID FK, check_type VARCHAR, severity ENUM, records_affected INT, threshold_pct FLOAT, breached BOOLEAN)
data.storage_design: S3 staging bucket (server-side encryption SSE-S3): raw REDCap JSON exports with 90-day lifecycle policy. Snowflake research_warehouse database: FHIR_OBSERVATIONS table in RESEARCH schema, de-identified ANALYST views via Snowflake Dynamic Data Masking. PostgreSQL 16 RDS for pipeline_runs and data_quality_checks metadata.
data.migration_notes: Snowflake schema managed via Liquibase changeset files versioned in Git. Schema changes require a migration DAG run before the regular pipeline to update FHIR_OBSERVATIONS column definitions.
data.data_risks: REDCap field mapping drift: REDCap project admins can rename fields without notice, breaking FHIR transformation. Mitigation: field_mapping table versioned in Git; transformation task validates field existence and alerts on unknown fields.

devops.deployment_topology: AWS MWAA environment (m5.xlarge workers, min 1 max 5 workers) in private subnet with VPC endpoints to S3, Secrets Manager, and Snowflake private link. PostgreSQL 16 RDS in same VPC for pipeline metadata. HashiCorp Vault on separate EC2 in VPC. GitHub Actions deploys DAG .py files to MWAA S3 DAGs bucket on merge to main.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: DAG file linting (pylint, black), Airflow DAG import validation (python -c 'from dag import dag'), pytest for transformation logic (90% coverage gate on FHIR mapping functions), Terraform plan in PR, Terraform apply on merge to main.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: Airflow DAG task duration, failure rate, record throughput per source. Airflow built-in email alerts on DAG failure. SES data quality report email: HTML report with records_exported, records_loaded, quality_check_results, breach_flags sent to data_governance_team@org after each run.
devops.devops_risks: MWAA environment upgrade requires manual DAG compatibility testing. Mitigation: DAGs pinned to Airflow provider versions; upgrade tested in staging MWAA first.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a HIPAA research data pipeline from REDCap to Snowflake via FHIR transformation. Key decisions: Airflow on MWAA for orchestration with per-source-project DAGs to isolate failures; FHIR Observation mapping as a stateless Python function for reusability across REDCap project connectors; S3 staging with 90-day lifecycle before Snowflake COPY INTO to stay within credit budget; data quality gate halting the load on critical breach to protect research dataset integrity; Vault dynamic secrets for REDCap API token isolation.
target_output.title: REDCap to FHIR Snowflake Research Data Pipeline Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A HIPAA-compliant Airflow pipeline on AWS MWAA that incrementally exports mental health outcome survey data from multiple REDCap project APIs, transforms records to FHIR Observation resources with JSON schema validation, and loads them into a Snowflake research data warehouse via COPY INTO from S3 staging. Data quality gates halt the load when critical thresholds are breached. All PHI access is audited to a PostgreSQL pipeline_runs log. REDCap API tokens are managed as Vault dynamic secrets with 1-hour TTLs.
target_output.architecture_overview: Airflow DAGs on AWS MWAA orchestrate a three-phase pipeline per REDCap source project: (1) export phase pulls incremental survey_responses from the REDCap REST API with rate-limit throttling and stages raw JSON to S3 with SSE-S3 encryption; (2) transform phase maps REDCap field values to FHIR Observation resources using a field_mapping registry versioned in Git, validates each observation against a JSON schema, and writes validated records to a second S3 prefix; (3) load phase runs Snowflake COPY INTO from the validated S3 prefix with MERGE deduplication on participant_id_hash and item_code. A data quality assessment task runs post-load, tallying missing values, out-of-range PHQ-9 and GAD-7 scores, and duplicates; if critical quality thresholds are breached the load is rolled back and the governance team notified. All pipeline executions and PHI access events are logged to a PostgreSQL audit database.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on AWS MWAA, m5.xlarge workers. Pipeline Code: Python 3.12, fhir.resources library for FHIR R4 Observation model, jsonschema for validation, snowflake-connector-python for COPY INTO. Integrations: REDCap REST API v10. Warehouse: Snowflake Enterprise Edition with Dynamic Data Masking. Pipeline Metadata: PostgreSQL 16 RDS. Secret Management: HashiCorp Vault with AWS auth. Storage: S3 with SSE-S3 for staging. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Terraform for MWAA and RDS infrastructure.
target_output.functional_feature_map: REDCap Incremental Export: per-project Airflow DAG parameterized by project_id, exporting records modified since last pipeline_run.completed_at using REDCap date range filters. FHIR Observation Transformation: stateless Python operator mapping REDCap survey fields to FHIR R4 Observation resources using project-specific field_mapping JSON files; LOINC codes assigned per instrument (PHQ-9 mapped to LOINC 44261-6, GAD-7 to 69737-5). Schema Validation Gate: jsonschema validation against FHIR Observation profile; batches with >5% invalid records halted with dead-letter staging. Snowflake MERGE Load: COPY INTO from validated S3 prefix followed by MERGE INTO fhir_observations on (participant_id_hash, item_code, effective_date) to prevent duplicates on re-runs. Data Quality Assessment: post-load checks for missing values, out-of-range PHQ-9 scores (valid 0-27), GAD-7 scores (valid 0-21), and inter-run participant count drift >20%.
target_output.system_components: AWS MWAA environment running Airflow 2.8 DAG scheduler and workers. S3 raw_staging prefix for REDCap JSON exports (90-day lifecycle). S3 validated_staging prefix for FHIR-transformed records (30-day lifecycle after load). Snowflake research_warehouse with FHIR_OBSERVATIONS table and ANALYST de-identified views. PostgreSQL 16 RDS for pipeline_runs, data_quality_checks, and phi_access_log. HashiCorp Vault on EC2 in VPC for REDCap API token dynamic secrets. GitHub Actions for DAG CI/CD. SES for data quality report email delivery.
target_output.workflows: Daily Pipeline Run: Airflow schedules redcap_export_dag at 02:00 UTC for each configured REDCap project. Export task fetches dynamic Vault secret (REDCap API token, 1-hour TTL), calls REDCap POST /api/ with content=record, format=json, dateRangeBegin from last pipeline_run.completed_at; stages raw JSON to S3 raw_staging/{project_id}/{run_id}/. Transform task reads raw JSON, maps each record's field values through field_mapping[project_id], constructs FHIR Observation JSON, validates against Observation.schema.json; records failing validation written to S3 dead_letter/{project_id}/{run_id}/; if >5% of batch fails, task raises halt exception, Airflow marks DAG failed, SES alert sent to governance team. Load task runs Snowflake COPY INTO research_warehouse.fhir_observations from validated_staging S3 prefix, then MERGE deduplication. Post-Load Quality Check: Airflow task queries fhir_observations for PHQ-9 scores outside [0-27], GAD-7 outside [0-21], null response_value, and participant count vs prior run; if any critical check breached, ROLLBACK issued, governance email sent with breach details. Quality Report Generation: HTML quality report with per-instrument record counts, validation failure counts, breach flags generated and emailed via SES.
target_output.data_model: survey_responses: S3 staged raw REDCap JSON export files keyed by {project_id}/{run_id}/responses.json, 90-day S3 lifecycle policy, SSE-S3 encryption. fhir_observations (Snowflake): id VARCHAR PK (UUID), participant_id_hash VARCHAR (SHA-256 of REDCap participant ID, for de-identification), study_id VARCHAR, instrument VARCHAR (PHQ-9/GAD-7/PCL-5), item_code VARCHAR (LOINC), response_value VARCHAR, response_unit VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, source_project_id VARCHAR, pipeline_run_id UUID, loaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ. participant_records (Snowflake): participant_id_hash VARCHAR PK, study_id VARCHAR, enrollment_date DATE, withdrawal_date DATE NULLABLE, consent_version VARCHAR. pipeline_runs (PostgreSQL): run_id UUID PK, source_project_id VARCHAR, status ENUM(running,success,failed,halted), records_exported INT, records_transformed INT, records_loaded INT, quality_report_s3_key VARCHAR, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. data_quality_checks (PostgreSQL): check_id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, check_type VARCHAR, severity ENUM(critical,high,medium), records_affected INT, threshold_pct FLOAT, breached BOOLEAN. Clinical data warehouse tables (post-pipeline destination): patient_encounters (id UUID PK, redcap_record_id VARCHAR, project_id FK, encounter_date DATE, encounter_type VARCHAR, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, site_id VARCHAR, clinician_id_hash VARCHAR), clinical_assessments (id UUID PK, encounter_id FK, scale_name VARCHAR, phq9_total INT, gad7_total INT, total_score NUMERIC, response_json_encrypted TEXT, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ), prescriptions_log (id UUID PK, patient_encounter_id FK, medication_name VARCHAR, dosage VARCHAR, prescriber_id_hash VARCHAR, status ENUM active/discontinued, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REDCap REST API consumption (no public API exposed by this pipeline). Airflow operator: POST https://{redcap_instance}/api/ with parameters: token (Vault dynamic secret), content=record, format=json, type=flat, exportSurveyFields=true, dateRangeBegin={last_run_completed_at}, dateRangeEnd={now}. Rate limiting: token bucket at 550 req/min (10% below 600 cap) with jitter via tenacity retry library. Snowflake COPY INTO statement: COPY INTO research_warehouse.fhir_observations FROM @research_stage/{run_id}/ FILE_FORMAT=(TYPE=JSON) ON_ERROR=SKIP_FILE_10. Pipeline metadata queries: internal PostgreSQL SELECT/INSERT for pipeline_runs and data_quality_checks via SQLAlchemy.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA: all REDCap API calls over TLS 1.3; S3 SSE-S3 encryption for staged data; Snowflake-managed encryption at rest. IRB compliance: phi_access_log records every exported participant_id_hash, data_element, and run_id. De-identification: participant IDs SHA-256 hashed before Snowflake load; ANALYST Snowflake role accesses de-identified views only. Vault dynamic secrets: REDCap tokens issued with 1-hour TTL leases; revoked after DAG task completion. IAM least privilege: MWAA worker role restricted to target S3 prefixes and Secrets Manager paths. Snowflake RBAC: PIPELINE_WRITER (INSERT/MERGE on research schema), ANALYST_READER (SELECT on de-identified views), AUDITOR_READER (SELECT on pipeline_runs and phi_access_log). Data retention Lambda: purges S3 raw_staging after 90 days; purges validated_staging 30 days post-successful-load.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Infrastructure as code: Terraform manages MWAA environment, RDS PostgreSQL, S3 buckets, VPC endpoints, and Vault EC2. GitHub Actions CI: DAG file linting with pylint and black, Airflow DAG import validation, pytest for FHIR transformation functions (90% coverage gate on field mapping logic), Terraform plan posted as PR comment, Terraform apply on merge to main. DAG code deployed to MWAA S3 DAGs bucket via GitHub Actions on merge. Environments: dev (local Airflow with SQLite metadata, Snowflake dev schema), staging (MWAA with staging Snowflake schema, reduced worker count), production (MWAA m5.xlarge workers min 1 max 5, production Snowflake schema). Secrets rotation: Vault REDCap token leases renewed automatically; no manual rotation required.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on MWAA workers: DAG task duration per source project, records exported/transformed/loaded rates, validation failure rate per instrument. Airflow built-in SLA miss alerts and DAG failure email to data_pipeline_oncall@org. SES data quality report: HTML email with per-instrument record counts, PHQ-9/GAD-7 out-of-range percentages, validation failure counts, and breach flags sent to data_governance_team@org after each successful run. Datadog alert: records_loaded daily decrease >30% vs 7-day average (signals unexpected participation dropout or REDCap data issue).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA m5.xlarge ~$0.49/hour = ~$355/month. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.medium ~$50/month. Snowflake COPY INTO with X-Small warehouse (auto-suspend 60s) for load tasks ~$80/month credits for daily runs. S3 staging ~$5/month. Total ~$490/month, within $500 Snowflake budget. Scaling: MWAA auto-scaling to 5 workers handles multi-project concurrent DAG runs; Snowflake warehouse scales independently for analyst query load without impacting pipeline credits.
