target_output.workflows: Stress Detection and Intervention Delivery: wearable transmits HRV sample at 1Hz to React Native app, app WebSocket sends to stream-gateway after Firebase Auth JWT check, gateway validates biometric payload schema and XADD to Redis biometric-events stream, inference-worker XREAD picks up event, queries user_baselines, computes HRV z-score, if deviation exceeds threshold writes stress_events row, calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash with versioned prompt, validates output against safe-messaging word list, pushes intervention via SSE to mobile within 500ms end-to-end. Device Pairing: user initiates pairing in React Native settings screen, coaching-api generates 24h HMAC-SHA256 device token binding device_id to user_id, token returned to app and stored in secure keystore, app includes token in WebSocket upgrade Authorization header for stream-gateway validation, wearable_devices record created on successful first connection.
target_output.data_model: biometric_streams (user_id UUID, device_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, hrv_ms SMALLINT, skin_conductance_us NUMERIC(6,3), motion_artifact_flag BOOL) — TimescaleDB hypertable partitioned by timestamp with 1-hour chunks and 90-day retention policy. stress_events (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, severity ENUM mild/moderate/severe, hrv_baseline_deviation_pct NUMERIC(5,2), trigger_context JSONB). interventions (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, stress_event_id UUID FK, intervention_type ENUM breathing/grounding/reframe, llm_prompt_version VARCHAR(16), content TEXT, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, efficacy_rating SMALLINT NULL). user_baselines (user_id UUID PK, hrv_mean_7d NUMERIC(6,2), hrv_std_7d NUMERIC(6,2), conductance_mean_7d NUMERIC(6,3), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). wearable_devices (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, hardware_model VARCHAR, firmware_version VARCHAR, paired_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). coaching_sessions (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, stress_event_count SMALLINT, interventions_delivered SMALLINT). Clinical wellness entities: patient_profiles (id UUID PK, user_id FK, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, assigned_therapist_id UUID, treatment_plan_id UUID, crisis_protocol_flag BOOLEAN), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, user_id FK, encounter_type ENUM intake/session/crisis, therapist_id UUID, clinical_notes_encrypted TEXT, vitals_json JSONB — cross-referenced with biometric_streams, encounter_date DATE), prescriptions (id UUID PK, user_id FK, medication_name VARCHAR, dosage VARCHAR, prescriber_id UUID, status ENUM active/discontinued).
target_output.api_design: WebSocket: wss://api.example.com/ws/biometric/{device_id} — Firebase Auth Bearer JWT required in upgrade Authorization header; client sends JSON frames {hrv_ms, skin_conductance_us, motion_artifact_flag, ts}. REST v1: POST /v1/devices/pair (body: device serial, returns HMAC token), GET /v1/sessions?limit=20 (coaching session history paginated), GET /v1/sessions/{id}/interventions (interventions within a session), GET /v1/insights/weekly?week_of=2026-03-17 (weekly digest), PATCH /v1/preferences (intervention type weights, notification schedule), DELETE /v1/account (CCPA deletion trigger, returns deletion_request_id). SSE: GET /v1/coaching/live (real-time intervention stream, EventSource, requires active stress event).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated at WebSocket upgrade handshake — 401 closes the connection immediately before any biometric data is accepted. TimescaleDB row-level security policy enforces user_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id') for all biometric table reads and writes. Vertex AI prompt constructed server-side from GCS-versioned templates with biometric context embedded as structured JSON — user-controlled text never appears in prompt prefix to prevent injection. LLM output scanned against safe-messaging word list (self-harm, suicide, medical diagnosis terms); violations logged to Datadog and fallback pre-validated intervention served. CCPA deletion: immediate soft-delete flag, hard purge job within 30 days, GCS append-only audit log entry. HIPAA alignment: CMEK encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, no biometric data outside us-central1, data processing agreement with Firebase/GCP.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 with three Helm-managed deployments. stream-gateway: HPA scales on custom Prometheus metric websocket_active_connections, min 3 pods / max 20 pods. inference-workers: HPA scales on Redis stream consumer lag metric (redis_stream_lag), min 2 / max 15 pods — lag is the most direct proxy for inference queue depth. coaching-api: HPA on CPU utilization 60%, min 2 / max 8 pods. CircleCI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest with 85% branch coverage gate, Docker build + push to Artifact Registry, helm upgrade --install to staging GKE namespace. Production deployment: manual approval gate in CircleCI, ArgoCD syncs Helm release, canary IngressRoute routes 5% of traffic to new pods for 30 minutes, automatic rollback triggered if error_rate >2% or p95_latency >800ms. Alembic migrations applied in a pre-deployment Kubernetes Job; all migrations are additive-only.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboards on Prometheus metrics: websocket_active_connections per stream-gateway pod (capacity alert >8K), biometric_ingestion_rate events/sec (drop alert), redis_stream_consumer_lag (backpressure alert >1000 events, critical >5000), stress_detection_latency_ms P50/P95 (SLA alert >300ms P95), llm_intervention_latency_ms P50/P95 (SLA alert >400ms P95), intervention_delivery_success_rate (alert <99%). Custom application metrics via Prometheus client: per-device stream_drop_count, per-user stress_event_frequency_1h. Alertmanager routes critical alerts to PagerDuty on-call: stream-gateway CrashLoopBackOff, Redis stream lag >5000, LLM error rate >1% over 5 minutes, end-to-end intervention SLA breach >500ms at P90.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $4,200/month at 50K daily active devices: GKE Autopilot node costs $1,800, TimescaleDB CloudSQL Enterprise $600, Redis Memorystore $400, Vertex AI Gemini Flash at avg $0.006/intervention with 200K daily interventions $1,200, egress $200. LLM cost controls: intervention rate-limited to 6 per user per hour; prompt token count minimized via structured JSON context format vs. natural language; Flash selected over Pro for 4x cost reduction at acceptable coaching quality. Scaling ceiling: Autopilot scales stream-gateway on connection count and inference-workers on Redis stream lag, both direct proxies for actual load, supporting >500K concurrent device connections before cluster reconfiguration.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Firebase Auth integration, WebSocket biometric ingestion for HRV stream only, static per-user stress threshold detection (no personalized baseline yet), 3 intervention types via Vertex AI Gemini Flash, coaching session history REST API, React Native app with real-time stress gauge and intervention cards, CircleCI CI/CD pipeline to GKE staging, beta deployment to hardware partner cohort of 500 test devices. Phase 2 (6 weeks): personalized HRV baseline modeling via nightly calibration job, skin conductance stream addition to biometric_streams hypertable, intervention efficacy rating capture and UI, weekly wellness digest batch job and mobile push notification. Phase 3 (4 weeks): continuous aggregate analytics with Grafana wellness dashboard, Apple Health write-back integration, CCPA self-service deletion flow with confirmation UI.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff linting (E, F, I rules) and mypy strict mode enforced in CircleCI — no merge if type errors present. FastAPI dependency injection for Firebase Auth validation on all routes and WebSocket upgrade handlers — no route proceeds without validated JWT payload. All TimescaleDB access through SQLAlchemy ORM with RLS context setting; no raw SQL that bypasses row-level security. Prompt templates versioned in GCS with semantic versioning; prompt changes require offline A/B quality evaluation before production rollout. Safe-messaging validator runs on every Vertex AI response — no performance exception allowed to bypass it. React Native: Reanimated 3 worklets for biometric gauge animation; no blocking JS thread operations in WebSocket message handler.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: FDA SaMD reclassification if stress detection marketing crosses into diagnostic claims. Mitigation: legal-approved intervention copy templates only, mandatory wellness-only disclaimers in onboarding and intervention UI, quarterly review of App Store listing language against FDA guidance. Risk: Vertex AI Gemini Flash latency spikes causing SLA breaches. Mitigation: pre-cached fallback library of 50 pre-generated breathing/grounding exercises served within 50ms when LLM P95 exceeds 350ms. Risk: Redis Stream consumer lag causing stale stress detections. Mitigation: inference-worker HPA scales on stream lag metric; alert at 1000 events, critical fallback to cached interventions at 5000. Tradeoff: TimescaleDB over InfluxDB — PostgreSQL RLS and Alembic migrations outweigh InfluxDB native time-series query performance for this security and compliance profile. Tradeoff: server-side prompt construction over client-provided context — security benefit of preventing prompt injection outweighs the added network round-trip latency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Firebase Auth selected over Cognito for superior React Native SDK and GCP-native integration. TimescaleDB chosen over InfluxDB for PostgreSQL-compatible RLS and migration tooling. Vertex AI Gemini Flash chosen over OpenAI GPT-4o-mini for GCP data residency compliance. Redis Streams over Kafka for operational simplicity at current scale; Kafka migration path documented for when daily active devices exceed 1M. FDA SaMD legal review initiated; product positioned as wellness coaching until legal opinion received.

metadata.notes: High-scale wearable biometric AI with real-time stress detection and LLM micro-intervention delivery on GKE; FDA SaMD wellness boundary is the primary unresolved risk.

profile.domain: Digital Health / CBT Insomnia Program Marketing

project_goal.value: Privacy-first landing page for a clinical-grade digital CBT-I (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia) program, communicating evidence-based credentialing and capturing waitlist signups via an encrypted intake form without storing personal data on the landing page infrastructure
project_goal.rationale: Product positioning requires strong privacy messaging to build trust with a health-conscious audience

target_users.value: Adults aged 25-55 experiencing chronic insomnia seeking evidence-based digital treatment alternatives to sleep medication
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified in market research; secondary is referring clinicians

project_class.rationale: Single-purpose conversion page with no server-side runtime or database — static deployment only

capabilities.rationale: Static site with client-side analytics; no backend capability required

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with form forwarding via Cloudflare Worker; no stateful backend

risk_level.rationale: No personal data stored on landing page infrastructure; form data forwarded to external CRM immediately

data_sensitivity.rationale: Landing page itself stores no personal data; waitlist email collection handled by external CRM with its own data agreements

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page accessible without authentication

access_model.value: No authentication — fully public page
access_model.rationale: Waitlist signup does not require account creation

feature_scope.value: Hero section with clinical evidence citations, program feature highlights, sleep science explainer, FAQ accordion, waitlist signup form with sleep concern fields, privacy-first trust signals, social proof section
feature_scope.rationale: Full v1 content sections for launch

mvp_scope.value: Hero, 3 feature highlights, waitlist form with email and primary sleep concern field, privacy policy link, basic analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for waitlist launch before full site content is finalized

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced, Content Security Policy headers blocking third-party scripts, Subresource Integrity on all CDN assets, no analytics cookies without consent banner, HSTS max-age 31536000
security_baseline.rationale: Privacy-first brand positioning requires hardened security headers as a trust signal to health-conscious users

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Zero-JS-by-default output ideal for a static landing page; Tailwind for rapid styling

backend_stack.value: None — Cloudflare Worker for form forwarding only (no persistent backend)
backend_stack.rationale: Serverless form handler keeps landing page infrastructure stateless; no server runtime needed

data_platform.value: None — no database on landing page; waitlist data stored in external CRM (Mailchimp)
data_platform.rationale: Data minimization principle: landing page infrastructure holds zero persistent data

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages with Cloudflare Worker for form endpoint
hosting_target.rationale: Cloudflare Pages provides edge CDN delivery globally; Cloudflare Worker co-located for minimal form submission latency

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained on landing page infrastructure; Cloudflare access logs retained 7 days; form payloads forwarded and not stored server-side
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data minimization: landing page is stateless, CRM governs retention of collected emails

future_scope.value: Localized versions for UK and Australia, A/B testing of sleep concern intake fields, integration with in-app onboarding flow post-waitlist acceptance
future_scope.rationale: International expansion and conversion optimization roadmap

constraints.value: No third-party analytics scripts that set cookies without consent; Lighthouse Performance score must exceed 95; page must load in <2s on 4G mobile
constraints.rationale: Privacy brand positioning and Core Web Vitals requirements for organic search ranking

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Analytics (cookieless) for traffic and conversion funnel; Plausible Analytics for goal completions (waitlist form submit); Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions for performance regression detection
observability_baseline.rationale: Privacy-compliant analytics stack; Lighthouse CI prevents performance regressions in CI

execution_preference.value: Single-sprint delivery with GitHub Actions CI and Cloudflare Pages preview deploys for each PR
execution_preference.rationale: Simple static site with rapid iteration cycle

project.goal: Privacy-first CBT-I program landing page with encrypted waitlist form and evidence-based credentialing content
project.target_users: Adults with chronic insomnia seeking digital CBT-I treatment

frontend.approach: Zero-JS static output; Astro islands for FAQ accordion and analytics consent banner only

security.headers: CSP blocking third-party scripts, SRI on CDN assets, HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY
security.analytics: Cloudflare Analytics + Plausible; no Google Analytics or Facebook Pixel without explicit consent

data.form_forwarding: Cloudflare Worker POSTs to Mailchimp API

devops.preview_deploys: Cloudflare Pages branch previews on every PR

constraints.no_third_party_cookies: No analytics cookies without consent banner

reasoner_reviews.product: The CBT-I credentialing content is the primary conversion driver — sleep science citations and clinical evidence badges need to be prominent above the fold. The waitlist form should ask only for email plus a single primary sleep concern field to maximize completion rate; longer intake questions belong in the product onboarding flow after waitlist acceptance.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with zero-JS static output is the ideal choice for this page — the FAQ accordion and consent banner are the only interactive elements and should be implemented as lightweight Astro islands, not a full React bundle. The Cloudflare Worker form handler must validate email format and honeypot field server-side before forwarding to Mailchimp to prevent spam submissions.
reasoner_reviews.security: The privacy-first brand positioning creates an obligation to match the marketing copy with actual technical controls. The CSP header must explicitly block Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, and other common trackers. SRI hashes must be computed in the build pipeline and injected into the HTML head to prevent CDN asset tampering. The consent banner must block Plausible script loading until consent is granted.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Lighthouse >95 performance target is achievable with Astro static output but requires vigilance: no render-blocking scripts, font preloading, and image optimization with Astro Image component. The <400KB total page weight constraint rules out any UI component library — Tailwind utilities only.

backend.service_design: Cloudflare Worker at /api/waitlist handles POST requests from the waitlist form: validates email format with regex, checks honeypot field is empty, forwards to Mailchimp Audiences API, returns 200 on success or 422 on validation failure
backend.failure_handling: Cloudflare Worker returns user-friendly error JSON on Mailchimp API failure; client-side form shows inline error message without page reload; no form data is logged in Worker

security.auth_design: No authentication required for public page; Cloudflare Worker validates CSRF-equivalent honeypot field and origin header to reject non-browser form submissions
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare WAF rate limiting on /api/waitlist: max 3 submissions per IP per hour; honeypot hidden field blocks naive bots; Turnstile CAPTCHA as optional progressive enhancement for high-bot-traffic periods
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloudflare Worker logs submission timestamp and country code only — no email addresses logged; Cloudflare Access Logs retained 7 days then deleted

data.entities: No persistent entities on landing page infrastructure. External CRM stores: waitlist_signups (email, sleep_concern_category, signup_source, created_at) managed by Mailchimp
data.retention_and_deletion: Cloudflare Worker processes and forwards form payloads without storage; access logs purged after 7 days; Mailchimp list governed by separate data processing agreement and unsubscribe flow

devops.deployment_topology: Cloudflare Pages hosts the static Astro build output; Cloudflare Worker deployed in the same Cloudflare project namespace for co-located form endpoint; custom domain via Cloudflare DNS with auto-renewed TLS
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Astro build, Lighthouse CI audit (fail if Performance <95 or any Core Web Vital orange), Cloudflare Pages deployment via wrangler CLI; PR branches auto-deploy to preview URLs for stakeholder review
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics goal tracking on waitlist_form_submit event; Cloudflare Analytics Web Analytics (cookieless) for traffic and geography; no cookie banner required for these tools

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site; privacy-first build with no third-party tracking scripts by default; consent required before any analytics pixel fires
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero with clinical evidence citations, Program Features, Sleep Assessment preview, Pricing, FAQ, CTA to app download
frontend.frontend_security_notes: No cookies set without explicit consent banner acceptance; HIPAA-adjacent content requires no inadvertent PHI capture in analytics events
frontend.performance_notes: Astro zero-JS default; islands only for FAQ accordion and consent banner; target < 100 KB total JS

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a privacy-first CBT-I landing page on Cloudflare Pages. The key architectural decisions are: Astro 4 zero-JS static output to hit Lighthouse >95, a co-located Cloudflare Worker for stateless form forwarding to Mailchimp (no personal data on landing page infrastructure), and a hardened CSP header set as the primary trust signal reinforcing the privacy-first brand. No database, no server-side runtime.
target_output.title: CBT-I Program Privacy-First Landing Page Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A static landing page for a clinical-grade digital CBT-I insomnia program, built with Astro 4 and Tailwind CSS on Cloudflare Pages. The architecture prioritizes privacy-first engineering as a brand signal: zero persistent data on landing page infrastructure, hardened CSP headers blocking third-party trackers, cookieless analytics via Plausible and Cloudflare, and a Cloudflare Worker form handler that forwards waitlist signups to Mailchimp without logging personal data.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static JAMstack architecture with no server-side runtime. Astro 4 generates zero-JS HTML at build time with Tailwind utility classes; interactive elements (FAQ accordion, consent banner) are minimal Astro islands with no external framework bundle. Cloudflare Pages serves the static output from its global edge network. A co-located Cloudflare Worker handles the single dynamic operation — waitlist form submission — by validating inputs and forwarding to Mailchimp API without retaining any personal data. GitHub Actions CI runs Astro build plus Lighthouse CI audit on every pull request, blocking merges if Lighthouse Performance drops below 95 or any Core Web Vital turns orange.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, Tailwind CSS, Astro Image component for responsive image optimization, minimal vanilla JS for FAQ accordion island. Form backend: Cloudflare Worker (JavaScript). CDN/hosting: Cloudflare Pages, Cloudflare Workers, Cloudflare DNS with auto-TLS. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless, self-hosted script via first-party proxy), Cloudflare Web Analytics. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, wrangler CLI, Lighthouse CI. No database, no authentication, no Node.js server runtime.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: headline with clinical efficacy claim (CBT-I vs. sleep medication comparison), primary CTA to waitlist form, trust badges (clinical citations, FDA wellness classification). Program Evidence Section: three evidence pillars (sleep restriction therapy, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring) with peer-reviewed citation links. Sleep Science Explainer: simplified two-component model of sleep drive with inline SVG diagram. FAQ Accordion: 8 common questions implemented as Astro island with no external JS. Waitlist Form: email field plus sleep concern radio (difficulty falling asleep / staying asleep / early waking / non-restorative sleep), honeypot field, Cloudflare Turnstile widget, submit calls /api/waitlist Worker. Privacy Trust Section: explicit statement of what data is not collected, link to full privacy policy.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build (static HTML/CSS output, zero runtime JS except FAQ island and consent banner). Cloudflare Pages (global edge delivery, branch preview deploys, custom domain with Cloudflare TLS). Cloudflare Worker at /api/waitlist (email validation, honeypot check, Mailchimp Audiences API POST, rate limiting via Cloudflare WAF rule). Cloudflare WAF (rate limit rule: 3 requests per IP per hour on /api/waitlist). Mailchimp Audiences (external CRM storing waitlist_signups with email and sleep_concern_category). Plausible Analytics (cookieless, goal event on waitlist form submission). GitHub Actions CI (Astro build, Lighthouse CI, wrangler deploy).
target_output.workflows: Waitlist Signup Flow: visitor completes email and sleep concern radio selection, clicks 'Join Waitlist', client-side JS validates email format inline before submit, form POST to /api/waitlist Cloudflare Worker, Worker validates email regex and honeypot empty field, calls Mailchimp Audiences API to add contact with sleep_concern_category tag, returns 200 JSON with success message displayed inline, Plausible goal event 'waitlist_submit' fired. PR Deployment Flow: developer opens PR, GitHub Actions runs Astro build and Lighthouse CI audit, if Lighthouse Performance <95 the check fails and merge is blocked, on passing checks wrangler CLI deploys to Cloudflare Pages branch preview URL for stakeholder review, on merge to main production deployment runs automatically.
target_output.data_model: No persistent data model on landing page infrastructure. Cloudflare Worker processes: waitlist_form_input (email: string, sleep_concern: enum falling_asleep/staying_asleep/early_waking/non_restorative, honeypot_field: must_be_empty). Cloudflare Worker output to Mailchimp: {email_address, status: 'subscribed', tags: [sleep_concern_category], merge_fields: {SOURCE: 'cbt-i-landing'}}. Cloudflare access logs (retained 7 days): {timestamp, country_code, status_code} — no PII fields logged.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/waitlist (Cloudflare Worker): body {email: string, sleep_concern: 'falling_asleep'|'staying_asleep'|'early_waking'|'non_restorative', _honeypot: ''}; returns 200 {success: true, message: 'You are on the waitlist!'} or 422 {error: 'Invalid email format'} or 429 {error: 'Too many submissions'}. No other API endpoints — all other content is static HTML. Plausible event: plausible('waitlist_submit', {props: {sleep_concern: value}}) fired on 200 response.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content Security Policy header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{build-nonce}' https://plausible.io https://challenges.cloudflare.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' https://plausible.io; frame-src https://challenges.cloudflare.com; object-src 'none' — blocks Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, and all unapproved third-party scripts. Subresource Integrity hashes computed for Plausible script in Astro build and injected as integrity attribute. HSTS header: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Cloudflare WAF rate limit on /api/waitlist: 3 requests per IP per hour. Consent banner blocks Plausible script load until visitor accepts; cookieless Cloudflare Web Analytics requires no consent. No cookies set by landing page infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repository; every push to a feature branch triggers a Cloudflare Pages preview deployment with a unique subdomain URL for stakeholder review. Production deployment triggers on merge to main branch. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: checkout, bun install, bun run build (Astro), Lighthouse CI using lighthouserc.json with Performance:95 / Accessibility:90 / Best-Practices:95 / SEO:95 thresholds, wrangler pages deploy dist/ --project-name=cbti-landing for production. Cloudflare Worker deployed separately via wrangler deploy src/worker.js -- script-name=cbti-waitlist and bound to the Pages project. DNS managed by Cloudflare with auto-renewed TLS. No rollback required — previous deployment restored by reverting the Git commit and re-triggering CI.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard: unique visitors, waitlist_submit goal completions, sleep concern field distribution (falling_asleep / staying_asleep / early_waking / non_restorative), referral sources. Cloudflare Web Analytics: page load times by geography, Core Web Vitals field data (LCP, CLS, INP) across real user sessions. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions provides lab performance scores per pull request with trend comparison. Cloudflare Worker analytics: request count and error rate on /api/waitlist, Mailchimp API failure rate. No server-side log retention of personal data.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Near-zero cost: Cloudflare Pages free tier handles up to 500 deployments/month and unlimited static requests; Cloudflare Workers free tier covers 100K daily form submissions comfortably for a waitlist launch. Plausible Analytics $9/month. Total estimated cost: $9-$15/month. Traffic spike handling: Cloudflare edge CDN absorbs any viral traffic burst without origin scaling — the static architecture has no scaling ceiling for read traffic. Write scaling (form submissions) limited by Mailchimp API rate limits (10 requests/second), sufficient for any realistic waitlist launch traffic.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Waitlist Launch (3 weeks): Hero section, 3 evidence highlights, waitlist form with email and sleep concern radio, Cloudflare Worker form handler, Plausible Analytics, hardened security headers, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate, Cloudflare Pages deployment. Phase 2 — Full Content (2 weeks post-launch): Sleep science explainer with SVG diagrams, FAQ accordion island, social proof / testimonials section, clinical citation library with links, privacy trust section with detailed data practices. Phase 3 — Optimization (ongoing): A/B test sleep concern field options using Cloudflare Pages A/B routing, localize hero copy for UK/AU markets, add referring clinician form variant.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro 4 strict TypeScript config; no client-side JS outside explicit Astro island directives. All images processed through Astro Image component for WebP output and responsive srcsets — no raw img tags. Tailwind classes only for styling; no custom CSS files. Lighthouse CI threshold defined in lighthouserc.json at repository root; merges blocked if any threshold fails. SRI hashes for Plausible and Turnstile scripts computed in build script and injected via Astro Head component — never hardcoded. Cloudflare Worker must return no Set-Cookie headers. PR description must include Lighthouse score screenshot from CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Mailchimp API outage blocks waitlist signups during launch campaign. Mitigation: Cloudflare Worker queues failed submissions in Cloudflare KV for a maximum 5-minute retry window before returning error to user. Risk: CSP header blocking Turnstile CAPTCHA during bot-traffic spike requiring rapid header update. Mitigation: Turnstile domain configured in Cloudflare dashboard; CSP nonce-based approach allows adding the challenges.cloudflare.com source without full rebuild. Tradeoff: Plausible over Google Analytics — loses demographic data and retargeting pixels in exchange for consent-free deployment and privacy brand alignment. Tradeoff: Cloudflare Worker over Netlify Functions — co-location with Cloudflare Pages eliminates cold-start latency for form submissions.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Cloudflare Pages selected over Netlify for unified platform with Cloudflare Worker form handler. Plausible Analytics selected over Google Analytics for cookieless operation matching privacy brand positioning. Mailchimp selected for waitlist CRM over HubSpot due to lower cost at waitlist scale. Turnstile selected over reCAPTCHA v3 to avoid Google script loading. No database needed — data minimization principle confirmed with legal team.

metadata.notes: Privacy-first CBT-I landing page on Cloudflare Pages; hardened CSP and stateless form forwarding are the architectural centerpiece matching the privacy brand positioning.

project.goal: Core Web Vitals-optimized landing page for HR EAP SaaS with Calendly demo booking and Salesforce CRM integration
project.target_users: HR directors at mid-market companies

frontend.framework: Astro 4 with React islands and Tailwind CSS
frontend.cms: Contentful headless CMS for case studies and blog posts

security.headers: X-Frame-Options DENY, nosniff, no inline scripts
security.analytics: GA4 via GTM with consent mode v2

data.crm: Salesforce via Zapier on Calendly booking confirmation

devops.content_deploys: Contentful webhook triggers GitHub Actions rebuild

constraints.cwv: All three Core Web Vitals green

reasoner_reviews.product: The ROI calculator is the highest-engagement feature for HR buyers — it should be interactive but must not compromise LCP. Lazy-loading the React island for the calculator is essential. The Calendly embed is the primary conversion point and must be tested for performance impact; a custom lightweight booking trigger that opens Calendly in a popup is preferable to an inline embed that adds significant page weight.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Contentful webhook-triggered rebuild pattern is correct for marketing team self-service. The build must be incremental where possible to avoid full site rebuilds on minor content updates. Astro's content collections with Contentful source adapter is the right integration point. The ROI calculator React island should be loaded with client:visible directive to defer hydration until scrolled into view.
reasoner_reviews.security: GA4 loaded via GTM with consent mode v2 is the correct approach — GTM's consent mode integration blocks GA4 from firing until the user accepts the consent banner. Calendly's embed script must be added to the CSP script-src allowlist. The inline Calendly script snippet must be avoided; load via external script tag with integrity attribute.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Two rounds of Lighthouse regressions have been introduced by the ROI calculator React bundle. The fix is client:visible deferred hydration. The Calendly popup approach reduces the Calendly script to a deferred load rather than an eager inline embed. CLS regressions were caused by late-loading fonts — font preload link tags in the Astro head resolve this.

backend.service_design: No server-side backend. Contentful Content Delivery API fetches case studies and blog post data at Astro build time. Calendly handles booking infrastructure independently. Zapier automation syncs Calendly booking completions to Salesforce as new Lead records.
backend.failure_handling: Contentful API unavailability during build: Astro build fails gracefully with error logging; previous deployment remains live on CloudFront. Calendly widget unavailability: lightweight fallback CTA linking to direct Calendly URL.

security.auth_design: No authentication on public page. GTM consent mode v2 blocks GA4 data collection until user accepts consent banner. Calendly popup loaded only after user interaction.
security.abuse_prevention: No form submission endpoint on landing page to abuse. CloudFront WAF standard rule set blocks common web attack patterns at CDN layer.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudFront access logs retained 30 days in S3 log bucket with lifecycle policy. No personal data in access logs.

data.entities: No persistent entities on landing page. Contentful CMS manages: case_studies (company, industry, employee_count, roi_metric, quote, logo_asset), pricing_plans (tier_name, features_list, price_per_employee), blog_posts (title, slug, excerpt, published_at).
data.retention_and_deletion: Static site stores no runtime data. CloudFront access logs purged after 30 days via S3 lifecycle rule.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket in us-east-1 stores Astro build output. CloudFront distribution with Origin Access Control reads from S3. Route53 ALIAS record for custom domain. ACM certificate for HTTPS. CloudFront cache headers set to max-age=3600 for HTML, max-age=31536000 for hashed static assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Astro build with Contentful data fetch, Lighthouse CI on built output with thresholds (Performance:90, Accessibility:90, SEO:95), aws s3 sync to bucket, CloudFront cache invalidation for HTML files. Contentful webhook triggers GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch for content-only rebuilds without code changes.
devops.observability_stack: Google Analytics 4 via GTM for pageviews, demo_booking_click events, ROI calculator engagement. Lighthouse CI stores performance trend in GitHub Actions artifacts. CloudFront metrics (cache hit rate, 4xx/5xx rates, bandwidth) in CloudWatch dashboard.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 with React islands for interactive components (ROI calculator, case study slider); static sections server-rendered
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero (EAP value prop), ROI Calculator, Customer Case Studies, Pricing for HR teams, Demo Request Form
frontend.ui_modules: ROICalculatorIsland (React), CaseStudyCarousel, PricingTable, DemoRequestForm with multi-step flow
frontend.performance_notes: ROI calculator hydrated lazily; case study images use width/height to prevent layout shift; target LCP < 2.5 s on 4G
frontend.frontend_risks: ROI calculator inputs may be crawled as form data; use non-identifying field labels and avoid storing inputs in query params

ISS-001.title: Calendly inline embed causing LCP regression
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan embedded Calendly widget inline on page load, adding 180KB of third-party script to critical path and pushing LCP to 3.8s on mobile 4G, exceeding the 2.5s threshold.

ISS-002.title: ROI calculator React bundle blocking main thread on mobile
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan loaded ROI calculator React island with client:load directive, adding 45KB of JS to the critical render path and causing INP of 340ms on mobile, failing the 200ms threshold.

ISS-003.title: CLS from FOUT on custom heading font
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 plan did not include font preload link tags, causing Cumulative Layout Shift of 0.18 from Flash of Unstyled Text on the hero heading, exceeding the 0.1 CLS threshold.

focus_issues.title: CLS from FOUT on custom heading font
focus_issues.detail: Round 2 plan did not include font preload link tags, causing CLS of 0.18 from Flash of Unstyled Text on the hero heading, exceeding the 0.1 CLS threshold.

latest_recommendations: Add font preload link tags for the custom heading font in the Astro Head component to eliminate CLS from FOUT
Verify Lighthouse CI CLS threshold is set to 0.1 in lighthouserc.json to catch future regressions
