target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 fix focuses entirely on ISS-003: CLS of 0.18 from FOUT on the hero heading font. The solution is font preload link tags in the Astro Head component and font-display: swap in the @font-face rule. I also need to add the CLS:0.1 threshold to lighthouserc.json so future regressions are caught in CI. The rest of the architecture from Round 2 is correct — Calendly popup trigger and ROI calculator client:visible remain unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Added <link rel='preload' as='font' type='font/woff2' crossorigin> tags for the hero heading font (Inter-Bold.woff2) in the Astro Head component. Added font-display: swap to the @font-face declaration in the global CSS to prevent invisible text during load. Added CLS:0.1 threshold to lighthouserc.json alongside existing LCP and INP thresholds so future CLS regressions are caught in CI.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CLS drops from 0.18 to <0.05 by eliminating FOUT layout shift on hero heading. Lighthouse CI will block merges if CLS exceeds 0.1 in future PRs.

target_output.title: EAP SaaS Landing Page — Round 3 Core Web Vitals Fix
target_output.executive_summary: Static Astro 4 landing page for a workplace mental wellness EAP SaaS, deployed on CloudFront + S3 and optimized to pass all three Google Core Web Vitals thresholds. Round 3 resolves the final CLS regression by adding font preload tags for the hero heading font and updating Lighthouse CI configuration to enforce the CLS 0.1 threshold. The Calendly popup trigger and ROI calculator client:visible patterns from Round 2 are retained.
target_output.architecture_overview: Static Astro 4 site on CloudFront + S3 in us-east-1 with Contentful headless CMS powering case studies and blog post content fetched at build time. Interactive elements are minimal React islands: ROI calculator with client:visible deferred hydration (loaded only when scrolled into viewport), Calendly popup trigger button that loads the Calendly script on user click (zero page-load cost). Font preload link tags in Astro Head for Inter-Bold.woff2 hero heading font with font-display: swap eliminate FOUT CLS. Contentful webhooks trigger GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch for content-only rebuilds. Lighthouse CI enforces Performance >90, LCP <2.5s, INP <200ms, CLS <0.1 thresholds on every PR.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, React 18 (islands only), Tailwind CSS, Astro Image component (WebP output), Astro Content Collections with Contentful source adapter. CMS: Contentful. Hosting: AWS S3 (static assets), CloudFront (CDN, Origin Access Control), Route53, ACM. Analytics: GA4 via GTM with consent mode v2. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Lighthouse CI (lighthouserc.json), AWS CLI (s3 sync + CloudFront invalidation). Integrations: Calendly (popup, deferred script), Zapier (Calendly to Salesforce Lead sync).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: company headline targeting HR EAP buyer persona with LCP-optimized hero image (WebP, preloaded), Calendly demo booking CTA button. ROI Calculator: React island loaded with client:visible, calculates estimated EAP program ROI per employee based on industry and company size inputs. Case Studies: 3 customer cards fetched from Contentful at build time showing company size, industry, and quantified EAP utilization improvement. Pricing Comparison: static table comparing Starter/Growth/Enterprise tiers with per-employee pricing. Blog Teasers: 3 most recent posts from Contentful sorted by published_at. Trust Badges: SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance logos with tooltip explanations. Consent Banner: blocks GA4 GTM firing until visitor accepts; Cloudflare-style minimal UI.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build system (static HTML generation, Contentful Content Delivery API fetch at build time, Astro Image for WebP optimization, Head component with font preload tags). S3 bucket (static asset origin, versioned objects with content-hash filenames for immutable caching). CloudFront distribution (Origin Access Control to S3, cache behaviors: HTML max-age=3600, hashed assets max-age=31536000, compression enabled). Route53 + ACM (ALIAS record, auto-renewed TLS cert). Contentful CMS (case_studies, pricing_plans, blog_posts content types). Calendly popup (deferred script loaded on button click, no page-load cost). ROI Calculator React island (client:visible, isolated bundle ~42KB). GitHub Actions CI (Astro build, Lighthouse CI, s3 sync, CloudFront invalidation). Zapier automation (Calendly booking webhook to Salesforce Lead creation).
target_output.workflows: Content Update Deploy: marketing editor publishes updated case study in Contentful, Contentful webhook fires POST to GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch endpoint, GitHub Actions triggers Astro build fetching updated Contentful content, Lighthouse CI runs performance audit on built output, on passing thresholds aws s3 sync pushes changed files, CloudFront invalidation clears HTML cache, updated content live within 5 minutes of Contentful publish. Demo Booking Flow: HR visitor reads case study and clicks 'Book a Demo' button in hero or pricing section, click event fires Calendly script lazy-load (adds ~85KB deferred, no LCP impact), Calendly popup opens in overlay, visitor selects time slot, Calendly fires confirmation webhook to Zapier, Zapier creates Salesforce Lead with company, email, and selected_time fields, GA4 'demo_booking_complete' event fires.
target_output.data_model: No persistent data model on landing page infrastructure. Contentful CMS content types: case_studies (fields: company_name, industry, employee_count_range, eap_utilization_lift_pct, hr_quote, logo_asset). pricing_plans (fields: tier_name, monthly_price_per_employee, feature_list, cta_label, is_highlighted). blog_posts (fields: title, slug, excerpt, author, published_at, cover_image). All fetched at Astro build time via Contentful Delivery API; no runtime API calls from browser. CloudFront access logs (stored in S3 log bucket, 30-day lifecycle): {timestamp, edge_location, status_code, uri, bytes_sent}.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API endpoints on landing page. Build-time only: Contentful Delivery API GET /spaces/{space_id}/entries?content_type=case_studies&order=-fields.published_at (fetched in Astro getStaticProps). Client-side: Calendly widget JavaScript API (loaded deferred on button click, no custom calls). GA4 events via GTM: page_view, demo_booking_click (button click), demo_booking_complete (Calendly confirmation), roi_calculator_engage (first interaction with ROI calculator island). No form submission endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Security headers deployed via CloudFront Response Headers Policy: Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). No inline scripts — all JS loaded via external script tags. GA4 GTM container fires only after consent banner acceptance (consent mode v2: ad_storage denied, analytics_storage denied until accept). Calendly script loaded lazily on user click — not present in initial page load to minimize third-party script exposure. CloudFront WAF with AWS managed rule group (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet) for standard attack pattern blocking.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline (triggers on push to main and PR branches): checkout, bun install, bun run build (Astro with Contentful env vars), Lighthouse CI audit using lighthouserc.json (thresholds: performance:90, accessibility:90, seo:95, lcp:2500, inp:200, cls:0.1 — CLS threshold added in Round 3), fail pipeline if any threshold missed, aws s3 sync dist/ s3://eap-landing-prod/ --delete --exclude '*.html' --cache-control max-age=31536000 then sync HTML files separately with --cache-control max-age=3600, aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*' for HTML files only. PR branches deploy to S3 staging bucket + separate CloudFront distribution for stakeholder preview. Contentful webhook endpoint configured as GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch PAT-authenticated URL for content-only rebuilds.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 via GTM consent mode v2: key goals tracked — demo_booking_complete (primary conversion), roi_calculator_engage, case_study_view, pricing_section_view. Lighthouse CI performance trend stored in GitHub Actions artifacts per commit; Slack notification on threshold failure. CloudFront metrics in CloudWatch: cache hit rate (target >90%), 4xx rate (alert >1%), total requests and bandwidth by region. Lighthouse CI thresholds in lighthouserc.json now include CLS:0.1 alongside Performance:90, LCP:2500ms, INP:200ms — all four Core Web Vitals enforced in CI.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $25-40/month: CloudFront data transfer ~$8, S3 storage $1, CloudFront requests $5, ACM free, Route53 $1, GA4 free. Total well within budget for a marketing landing page. Contentful free tier sufficient for case studies and blog posts volume. Calendly hosted externally at no cost on their infrastructure. Traffic spike handling: CloudFront edge cache absorbs viral traffic bursts with >90% cache hit rate; S3 origin not hit for cached HTML. No scaling ceiling for static reads.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 1 (completed): Initial static Astro build, CloudFront + S3 deployment, GA4 GTM integration, basic content sections. Round 2 (completed): Calendly popup trigger replacing inline embed (ISS-001 resolved), ROI calculator client:visible deferred hydration (ISS-002 resolved), Contentful CMS integration for case studies. Round 3 (current): Font preload tags for Inter-Bold.woff2 hero heading font (ISS-003 resolved), CLS:0.1 Lighthouse CI threshold added, font-display: swap CSS rule added. Phase 4 (upcoming): Trust badge section with SOC 2 and HIPAA logos, blog post teasers from Contentful, localized hero copy for UK market via URL parameter.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro Head component is the single source of truth for all link preload tags — font preloads must be added here, not in individual page components. Font preload tags must include crossorigin='anonymous' attribute to match the CORS-enabled S3 font file. lighthouserc.json is version-controlled in the repository root; all four Core Web Vitals thresholds (performance, lcp, inp, cls) must be present. React islands must use client:visible unless interaction before scroll is required; client:load requires written justification in the PR description. All Astro Image usage must specify width and height props to prevent CLS from image dimension shifts. Calendly script must never be added to the page head — load via dynamic import on button click event only.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful API outage during marketing campaign causing stale content deploy. Mitigation: Astro build caches Contentful responses; previous CloudFront deployment remains live; rebuild retried automatically on next commit. Risk: CLS regression reintroduced by future font or layout changes. Mitigation: Lighthouse CI CLS:0.1 threshold now enforced in CI — merges blocked on regression. Risk: Calendly script version update changing popup API breaking the lazy-load integration. Mitigation: pin Calendly widget.js version in the script URL and document upgrade process. Tradeoff: Astro over Next.js — zero-JS static output achieves Lighthouse >95 baseline impossible with Next.js hydration overhead for a page this simple. Tradeoff: CloudFront + S3 over Vercel — company standard AWS tooling and lower egress cost at scale outweigh Vercel's simpler DX for this use case.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Inter-Bold.woff2 confirmed as the font file causing FOUT — identified via Lighthouse filmstrip analysis. font-display: swap confirmed safe for hero heading (no invisible text period). CLS threshold of 0.1 added to lighthouserc.json and confirmed with QA team. Calendly popup approach confirmed acceptable by sales team — no requirement for inline embed.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving final CLS regression from FOUT on hero heading font; ISS-001 and ISS-002 were resolved in Round 2; fix_report addresses ISS-003 with font preload and CLS CI threshold.

profile.domain: Mental Health Crisis Services / AI Triage

project_goal.value: LLM-based triage chatbot for a mental health crisis text line pre-screening incoming messages to classify risk level and route acute-risk contacts to available counselors, reducing queue wait times and operational costs through intelligent routing
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need: counselors are overwhelmed by volume; intelligent pre-screening routes only high-risk contacts to live counselors

target_users.value: People in mental health crisis contacting the text line (public internet) and licensed counselors managing triage queue (counselor portal)
target_users.rationale: Two distinct user personas: crisis texter (public, unauthenticated) and counselor (authenticated, admin portal)

project_class.rationale: Core system function is LLM-based risk classification and intelligent routing, not a traditional web application

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, ai_llm, realtime, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: No frontend SPA needed — SMS interface via Twilio; counselor portal is admin panel; real-time queue updates required

complexity_level.rationale: LLM integration, real-time counselor queue, Twilio SMS webhooks, mandatory audit logging, and duty-of-warn compliance create significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect triage classification directly risks human life; HIPAA applies to crisis communication data; mandatory reporting obligations in most jurisdictions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Crisis communication transcripts contain disclosed mental health information; classified as PHI under HIPAA

external_exposure.rationale: Crisis texters reach the system via public SMS (Twilio); counselor portal is private_authenticated but overall system boundary is public_internet

access_model.value: Twilio webhook for crisis texter ingress (no authentication for texters by design — barrier-free access required); Cognito-authenticated counselor portal with MFA and RBAC for counselor/supervisor/admin roles
access_model.rationale: Crisis line must have zero authentication friction for texter; counselors require MFA per HIPAA

feature_scope.value: SMS-based triage chatbot with LLM risk classification, counselor real-time queue dashboard, contact escalation to 911/partner crisis services, conversation transcript storage, weekly operational analytics, LLM prompt management interface
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational feature set

mvp_scope.value: Twilio SMS ingestion, LLM triage with 3 risk levels (low/moderate/acute), counselor queue dashboard with SSE updates, conversation transcript logging, basic escalation to static 911 message
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot launch with a partner crisis organization

security_baseline.value: Twilio webhook signature validation, TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, AES-256 encryption at rest for conversation transcripts, Cognito MFA for counselors, append-only audit log for all triage decisions and escalations
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA requirements plus duty-of-warn compliance mandate comprehensive audit trails and strong access controls

frontend_stack.value: None for crisis texters — SMS interface via Twilio; counselor portal built as server-rendered FastAPI Jinja2 templates with Alpine.js for SSE-driven queue updates
frontend_stack.rationale: No SPA needed for counselor portal at MVP; server-rendered approach reduces build complexity

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async WebSocket/SSE support; strong Python LLM integration ecosystem

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for crisis contacts and transcripts; Redis 7 for counselor session state and queue management
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for durable transcript storage and audit logs; Redis for real-time queue ordering and counselor presence

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: HIPAA-eligible AWS region; Fargate managed containers; BAA with AWS in place

privacy_retention_policy.value: Conversation transcripts retained 7 years per healthcare data retention requirements; triage decision audit logs retained 7 years; texter phone numbers stored hashed (SHA-256 with per-contact salt); PII fields encrypted with KMS
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Healthcare data retention law and potential litigation hold requirements; phone number hashing balances re-contact capability with privacy

future_scope.value: Multi-language triage (Spanish, Mandarin), voice channel integration via Twilio Voice, integration with 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline national routing, counselor performance analytics dashboard

constraints.value: LLM triage classification must complete within 8 seconds of message receipt; system must handle 500 concurrent active conversations; triage must never silently fail — all LLM errors must fall back to immediate counselor escalation
constraints.rationale: 8s SLA keeps conversation flow natural; 500 concurrent is peak demand estimate; no-silent-failure is a safety requirement

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with custom metrics for triage classification latency, queue depth per risk level, escalation rate, and LLM classification confidence distribution; PagerDuty for queue backlog and triage failure alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Operational safety requires real-time visibility into queue depth and triage failure rate

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with continuous deployment to staging; production deployments require clinical review sign-off due to life-safety implications
execution_preference.rationale: Production changes to triage logic require clinical team approval to prevent regressions in safety-critical routing

llm_integration.value: Anthropic Claude Haiku via AWS Bedrock for triage classification; structured output (JSON mode) with strict schema validation; system prompt versioned in AWS Parameter Store; all prompts reviewed by clinical advisors before deployment
llm_integration.rationale: Claude Haiku meets 8s SLA with room to spare; AWS Bedrock keeps PHI within AWS HIPAA-eligible boundary; structured JSON output enables reliable risk_level extraction

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH for crisis communication PHI; 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline compliance standards; mandatory reporting and duty-to-warn obligations vary by state jurisdiction; BAA with AWS and Twilio required; all triage decisions and escalations must be auditable
compliance_context.rationale: Crisis line operates in a highly regulated space with life-safety implications; audit trail completeness is non-negotiable

project.goal: LLM triage chatbot for mental health crisis text line with counselor queue management portal
project.target_users: Crisis texters (SMS, public) and licensed counselors (authenticated web portal)

frontend.counselor_portal: Server-rendered FastAPI Jinja2 templates with Alpine.js for SSE-driven real-time queue updates
frontend.approach: No SPA — Jinja2 templates reduce build complexity at MVP

backend.api: Twilio webhook endpoint for SMS ingestion, SSE for counselor queue updates, REST for counselor actions

security.auth: Cognito user pool with MFA for counselors; Twilio webhook signature HMAC-SHA1 validation
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest via RDS KMS, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.audit: Append-only triage_audit_log table, CloudTrail for all AWS API calls

data.cache: Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for queue state and counselor presence

devops.environments: dev, staging, production; production deploys require clinical sign-off

constraints.failsafe: All LLM errors escalate immediately to counselor — no silent triage failure

open_questions.jurisdiction_mapping: Legal review of jurisdiction-specific mandatory reporting thresholds ongoing

reasoner_reviews.product: The triage chatbot must clearly communicate to crisis texters that they are interacting with an AI, not a human counselor, to maintain trust and comply with best practice guidance. The system prompt must be designed with clinical advisors — the AI's initial responses during triage must follow safe-messaging guidelines and never provide specific method information or dismissive responses. The counselor queue UI must show risk level prominently and age of each contact to prevent acute-risk contacts from aging in the queue.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The no-silent-failure constraint is the most important architectural requirement. The triage pipeline must be designed so that any exception — LLM timeout, Bedrock API error, schema validation failure, database write failure — automatically routes the contact to immediate counselor escalation rather than dropping the message. A dead-letter pattern with automatic escalation fallback must be designed into every step of the message processing pipeline. The 500 concurrent conversation estimate requires careful ECS task sizing — each active conversation holds a Redis session with conversation history for the LLM context window.
reasoner_reviews.security: Twilio webhook signature validation using the X-Twilio-Signature header and HMAC-SHA1 must be the first middleware on the ingestion endpoint — unsigned requests must be rejected before any processing. Texter phone numbers must be hashed immediately on receipt; the raw phone number must not be stored in any table. The LLM system prompt must be retrieved from AWS Parameter Store at startup, never hardcoded, so clinical advisors can update prompts via Parameter Store without a code deployment. Conversation transcripts must use KMS envelope encryption with a per-contact data key to limit blast radius of key compromise.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 8-second LLM triage SLA is achievable with Claude Haiku via Bedrock (typical P90 ~2-4 seconds), but the full pipeline — Twilio webhook processing, message persistence, LLM call, risk classification extraction, queue update, and counselor SSE push — must complete serially within 8 seconds. Any synchronous database write in the hot path must use a connection pool and async SQLAlchemy to avoid blocking the event loop.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The most dangerous failure mode in this system is not a technical failure but a clinical one: the LLM misclassifying an acute-risk texter as low-risk and routing them to the automated self-help flow. The confidence score from the LLM classification must be used as a safety gate — any classification with confidence below a threshold (e.g., 0.80) must be escalated to a counselor regardless of the predicted risk level. Clinical advisors must review this threshold in the pilot phase based on real classification data.

backend.service_design: Single FastAPI application with three routers: triage_webhook (Twilio SMS ingestion and LLM orchestration), counselor_portal (Jinja2 server-rendered queue dashboard with SSE), and admin_api (prompt management, analytics, escalation log review)
backend.api_patterns: POST /webhook/twilio/sms (Twilio webhook with signature validation middleware); GET /portal/queue (Jinja2 counselor dashboard with SSE endpoint at /portal/queue/stream); POST /portal/contacts/{id}/accept (counselor takes a contact); POST /portal/contacts/{id}/escalate (manual escalation to 911/crisis service); GET /admin/prompts (list deployed prompt versions); PUT /admin/prompts/active (promote prompt version to active)
backend.business_modules: TwilioIngressModule (signature validation, phone hash, contact upsert), TriageOrchestrator (LLM call, structured output parse, risk classification, failsafe escalation on any error), QueueManager (Redis sorted set keyed by risk_level and received_at, SSE broadcast), CounselorSessionModule (counselor presence tracking in Redis, contact assignment), EscalationModule (logs escalation events, sends 911 message or partner service handoff)
backend.failure_handling: All exceptions in TriageOrchestrator are caught by a safety wrapper that calls EscalationModule.escalate_immediately(contact_id). Bedrock API timeout (8s hard timeout) triggers the same failsafe. Dead-letter queue in SQS for messages where Twilio webhook acknowledgment failed — reprocessed after 30 seconds.

security.auth_design: Twilio X-Twilio-Signature HMAC-SHA1 validation on every POST to /webhook/twilio/sms — request rejected with 403 before any processing if signature invalid. Cognito user pool for counselors with MFA (TOTP) enforced; Cognito JWT validated by ALB before routing to counselor portal.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: counselor role (view queue, accept contacts, send messages), supervisor role (view all counselors, reassign contacts, view analytics), admin role (manage prompts, export transcripts, configure escalation rules). Roles stored in Cognito custom attributes.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: triage_audit_log table (append-only, no UPDATE/DELETE): records every incoming message, LLM classification result with confidence score, risk_level assigned, routing decision, counselor_id if accepted, escalation events. CloudTrail captures all AWS API calls including Bedrock invocations with redacted PHI in log metadata.
security.privacy_controls: Phone numbers hashed with SHA-256 and per-contact salt immediately on Twilio webhook receipt; raw phone number never written to database. Conversation transcript fields encrypted with KMS per-contact data key. Bedrock API call includes conversation content — ensure BAA with Anthropic via AWS Bedrock covers PHI in API payloads.

data.entities: crisis_contacts (id UUID PK, phone_hash VARCHAR(64), salt VARCHAR(32), first_contact_at TIMESTAMPTZ, total_messages INT, current_risk_level ENUM low/moderate/acute, status ENUM active/transferred/closed, assigned_counselor_id UUID NULL). conversation_messages (id UUID PK, contact_id UUID FK, direction ENUM inbound/outbound, content_encrypted TEXT, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, llm_classified BOOL). triage_assessments (id UUID PK, contact_id UUID FK, message_id UUID FK, risk_level ENUM low/moderate/acute, confidence_score NUMERIC(4,3), llm_prompt_version VARCHAR, reasoning_summary TEXT, processing_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). triage_audit_log (id BIGSERIAL PK, contact_id UUID, event_type VARCHAR, actor_id UUID NULL, detail JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). counselor_actions (id UUID PK, counselor_id UUID FK, contact_id UUID FK, action ENUM accepted/escalated/transferred/closed, notes TEXT, acted_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with KMS encryption for conversation_messages content fields. Redis 7 ElastiCache (counselor_queue sorted set keyed by {risk_level_priority}:{received_at}, counselor presence hash). S3 for transcript export archives with SSE-KMS.
data.analytics_events: Weekly operational report: messages_received, triage_distribution_by_risk_level, avg_triage_latency_ms, escalation_rate, counselor_acceptance_rate, avg_queue_wait_time_by_risk_level, llm_confidence_distribution.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a/1b with ALB. Two Fargate services: triage-api (handles Twilio webhooks and counselor portal, min 2 / max 10 tasks) and background-worker (SQS dead-letter reprocessing, nightly analytics job, min 1 / max 3 tasks). ALB routes /webhook/ to triage-api and /portal/ to triage-api (same service, different path). Cognito JWT authorizer on ALB for /portal/ and /admin/ routes.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff + mypy, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Docker build, push ECR, deploy ECS rolling update to staging. Production deployment requires manual approval gate plus documented clinical sign-off in PR description for any change touching TriageOrchestrator, prompt templates, or escalation logic.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: triage_classification_latency_ms (alert P90 >6s), queue_depth_by_risk_level.acute (alert >10 contacts), llm_error_rate (alert >0.5%), counselor_queue_wait_ms.acute (alert >120s). PagerDuty escalation for: queue_depth.acute >10, triage system down, LLM error rate >1%.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a life-safety-critical LLM triage system for a crisis text line. The dominant architectural concern is the no-silent-failure requirement: every exception in the triage pipeline — LLM timeout, Bedrock error, JSON parse failure, database write failure — must trigger immediate counselor escalation rather than dropping the message. This drives a safety-wrapper pattern around TriageOrchestrator with automatic fallback. The second critical decision is texter phone number hashing on first receipt — the raw number must never touch the database. Claude Haiku via Bedrock with structured JSON output meets the 8s SLA while keeping PHI within the AWS HIPAA boundary.
target_output.title: Mental Health Crisis Text Line AI Triage Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A life-safety-critical AI triage system for a mental health crisis text line, built on FastAPI on ECS Fargate with Claude Haiku via AWS Bedrock for risk classification and a real-time counselor queue dashboard. The architecture implements a no-silent-failure safety guarantee: every exception in the LLM triage pipeline automatically triggers immediate counselor escalation. Conversation transcripts are encrypted with KMS per-contact data keys; texter phone numbers are hashed on first receipt. All triage decisions are written to an append-only audit log to satisfy HIPAA and 988 compliance requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-region AWS architecture on ECS Fargate in us-east-1 handling two traffic classes: incoming SMS from crisis texters via Twilio webhooks, and counselor portal traffic authenticated via Cognito with MFA. Twilio POSTs to the triage-api Fargate service; HMAC-SHA1 signature validation is the first middleware before any processing. The TriageOrchestrator calls Claude Haiku via AWS Bedrock with structured JSON output schema, extracts risk_level and confidence_score, and writes triage_assessments. An 8-second hard timeout on the Bedrock call and a safety wrapper on all TriageOrchestrator exceptions automatically call EscalationModule.escalate_immediately() to ensure no contact is silently dropped. Counselor queue state is maintained in a Redis sorted set ordered by risk_level priority and received_at timestamp. SSE pushes queue updates to the counselor dashboard in real time. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ stores crisis_contacts, conversation_messages (KMS-encrypted), triage_assessments, and the append-only triage_audit_log.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy (async), Alembic. AI: Anthropic Claude Haiku via AWS Bedrock (structured JSON output mode), prompt templates in AWS Parameter Store. SMS: Twilio Programmable Messaging with HMAC-SHA1 webhook signature validation. Counselor portal: FastAPI Jinja2 templates, Alpine.js, Server-Sent Events. Data: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with KMS, ElastiCache Redis 7, SQS dead-letter queue. Auth: Amazon Cognito user pool with MFA (TOTP), ALB JWT authorizer. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1, ALB, ACM, ECR, AWS Secrets Manager, AWS Parameter Store, CloudTrail. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty. CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SMS Triage Pipeline: Twilio delivers incoming SMS to /webhook/twilio/sms; HMAC-SHA1 validation; phone hash computed; contact upserted; message persisted KMS-encrypted; TriageOrchestrator calls Bedrock Claude Haiku with versioned system prompt plus conversation history; structured JSON response parsed for risk_level (low/moderate/acute) and confidence_score; if confidence <0.80 or any exception, escalate to counselor; otherwise route by risk_level. Counselor Queue Dashboard: server-rendered Jinja2 page showing contacts sorted by acute/moderate/low with age indicator; Alpine.js SSE client receives queue push events and re-renders contact list without page reload; counselor clicks 'Accept' to take ownership of a contact. Escalation Module: acute risk contacts or low-confidence classifications pushed to top of queue with alert; 911 contact escalation sends pre-approved safety message and logs external escalation event. Prompt Management: admin interface to view deployed prompt versions from Parameter Store, stage new prompt versions, and promote with clinical sign-off.
target_output.system_components: Twilio Programmable Messaging (SMS ingress and outbound message delivery). ALB with HMAC signature validation middleware path for /webhook/ and Cognito JWT authorizer for /portal/ and /admin/. triage-api ECS Fargate service (FastAPI: TwilioIngressModule, TriageOrchestrator, QueueManager, CounselorPortal, AdminAPI; min 2 / max 10 tasks with ALB target group health checks). background-worker ECS Fargate service (SQS dead-letter consumer, nightly analytics aggregation job; min 1 / max 3 tasks). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ us-east-1 with KMS encryption (crisis_contacts, conversation_messages, triage_assessments, triage_audit_log, counselor_actions). ElastiCache Redis 7 (counselor_queue sorted set, counselor presence hash, Bedrock response cache for deduplication). AWS Bedrock (Claude Haiku endpoint in us-east-1 with BAA coverage). SQS dead-letter queue for Twilio webhook retry. AWS Parameter Store (versioned LLM system prompts). AWS Secrets Manager (Twilio auth token, Cognito service account, database credentials). CloudTrail (all AWS API audit logs).
target_output.workflows: Crisis Texter Triage: Twilio delivers POST to /webhook/twilio/sms with message body and From number. Middleware validates X-Twilio-Signature HMAC-SHA1 (reject 403 on failure). TwilioIngressModule computes phone_hash = SHA256(from_number + per_contact_salt), upserts crisis_contacts, writes conversation_messages with KMS-encrypted content. TriageOrchestrator wrapped in safety_wrapper: calls Bedrock Claude Haiku with versioned system prompt plus last 10 conversation messages as context, 8s hard timeout; on success parses structured JSON {risk_level, confidence_score, reasoning_summary}; if confidence_score < 0.80 → calls escalate_immediately(); else writes triage_assessments row; QueueManager.push(contact_id, risk_level, received_at) to Redis sorted set; broadcasts SSE queue_update event to all connected counselor dashboards. Safety Failsafe: any exception in TriageOrchestrator (LLM error, timeout, JSON parse failure, DB write failure) caught by safety_wrapper → EscalationModule.escalate_immediately(contact_id) → writes triage_audit_log event type=failsafe_escalation → QueueManager.push(contact_id, risk_level='acute') → Datadog metric triage_failsafe_count incremented. Counselor Acceptance: counselor clicks Accept on queue item → POST /portal/contacts/{id}/accept → contact assigned to counselor_id in Redis and PostgreSQL → contact removed from queue sorted set → SSE broadcast removes contact from all other counselors' queue views.
target_output.data_model: crisis_contacts (id UUID PK, phone_hash VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL, salt VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL, first_contact_at TIMESTAMPTZ, total_messages INT DEFAULT 0, current_risk_level ENUM low/moderate/acute, status ENUM active/transferred/closed, assigned_counselor_id UUID NULL). conversation_messages (id UUID PK, contact_id UUID FK, direction ENUM inbound/outbound, content_encrypted BYTEA, kms_data_key_encrypted BYTEA, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, llm_classified BOOL DEFAULT FALSE). triage_assessments (id UUID PK, contact_id UUID FK, message_id UUID FK, risk_level ENUM low/moderate/acute, confidence_score NUMERIC(4,3), llm_prompt_version VARCHAR(32), reasoning_summary TEXT, processing_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). triage_audit_log (id BIGSERIAL PK, contact_id UUID NOT NULL, event_type VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, actor_id UUID NULL, detail JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) — no UPDATE/DELETE permissions granted on this table. counselor_actions (id UUID PK, counselor_id UUID FK, contact_id UUID FK, action ENUM accepted/escalated/transferred/closed, notes TEXT, acted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). users (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, display_name VARCHAR, role ENUM counselor/supervisor/admin, last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Clinical triage entities: patient_profiles (id UUID PK, contact_id FK, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, prior_crisis_count INT, assigned_physician_id UUID, treatment_plan_id UUID, care_level ENUM outpatient/intensive_outpatient/inpatient), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, contact_id FK, encounter_type ENUM intake/crisis_call/safety_check, clinician_id UUID, encounter_date TIMESTAMPTZ, clinical_notes_encrypted TEXT, vitals_json JSONB, disposition ENUM safe/hospitalized/referred), prescriptions (id UUID PK, patient_profile_id FK, medication_name VARCHAR, dosage VARCHAR, prescriber_id UUID, status ENUM active/discontinued).
target_output.api_design: POST /webhook/twilio/sms — Twilio webhook; validates X-Twilio-Signature header HMAC-SHA1; body: From, Body (message text); returns 200 TwiML with initial acknowledgment message. GET /portal/queue — Jinja2 server-rendered counselor queue page with contact cards sorted by risk priority. GET /portal/queue/stream — SSE endpoint; emits queue_update events as JSON {contact_id, risk_level, age_seconds, counselor_assigned}. POST /portal/contacts/{id}/accept — counselor accepts contact; assigns counselor_id; returns 200. POST /portal/contacts/{id}/escalate — body: {escalation_type: '911'|'partner_service'|'supervisor', notes}; logs counselor_action and triage_audit_log event; returns 200. GET /admin/analytics/weekly — returns weekly triage distribution, avg latency, escalation rate. GET /admin/prompts — lists Parameter Store prompt versions with deployment status. PUT /admin/prompts/active — body: {version_id}; promotes prompt version to active; requires admin role; writes audit log entry.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Twilio webhook HMAC-SHA1 validation: computed as HMAC(twilio_auth_token, url + sorted_params); request rejected 403 before any database access if invalid — prevents spoofed message injection. Phone number privacy: SHA-256(phone_number + per_contact_salt) computed in memory on receipt; raw phone number never written to any table or log. Conversation transcript KMS encryption: per-contact data key generated by AWS KMS, used to AES-256-encrypt content_encrypted field; encrypted data key stored in kms_data_key_encrypted — limits blast radius if database is compromised. triage_audit_log: PostgreSQL table with GRANT SELECT, INSERT only (no UPDATE, DELETE) to application role; covers all triage decisions, escalations, counselor actions, and failsafe events to satisfy HIPAA audit trail and 988 compliance requirements. Cognito MFA mandatory for all counselor, supervisor, and admin roles. ALB JWT authorizer validates Cognito JWT on all /portal/ and /admin/ routes before routing to ECS. LLM system prompt retrieved from AWS Parameter Store on startup — never hardcoded; prompt updates require Parameter Store change + clinical sign-off in PR. HIPAA controls: BAA with AWS (Fargate, RDS, ElastiCache, Bedrock, S3), BAA with Twilio for PHI in SMS payloads.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1 with two services. triage-api: ALB target group with /health health check, desired 2 tasks, max 10, auto-scaling on ALB RequestCountPerTarget. background-worker: SQS poller, desired 1 task, max 3, scales on queue depth. GitHub Actions pipeline: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest 85% coverage gate, Docker build + push to ECR, ECS rolling update to staging (0 downtime, deregister old tasks after health checks pass). Production deployment: manual approval gate in GitHub Actions; changes to TriageOrchestrator, escalation logic, or prompt templates require documented clinical sign-off added to PR description before approval is unlocked. Database migrations: Alembic applied via ECS run-task pre-deployment job; migrations are additive-only. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation for Twilio auth token and database credentials. CloudTrail enabled in all regions for complete AWS API audit coverage.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task. Custom metrics: triage_classification_latency_ms (P50/P90; alert P90 >6s, critical >8s), counselor_queue_depth by risk_level (PagerDuty alert: acute >10), triage_failsafe_count (alert >0 in 5min — any silent failure triggers immediate investigation), llm_confidence_score_distribution (histogram; alert if median drops below 0.75), escalation_rate (daily). Structured JSON logs with correlation_id threading contact_id through all log entries for incident investigation. PagerDuty integrations: queue depth acute >10, triage-api task count <1 (service down), LLM error rate >1% over 5 minutes. Weekly operational report to clinical leadership: messages_received, triage distribution percentages, avg wait time per risk level, counselor acceptance rate, failsafe escalation count.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,800/month at 5,000 daily messages: ECS Fargate triage-api ($800), RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL ($700), ElastiCache Redis ($200), Bedrock Claude Haiku at $0.00025/1K input tokens and $0.00125/1K output tokens (~$150 at avg 500 tokens per triage call), Twilio messaging ($250 at $0.05/message), Datadog ($500), SQS/CloudWatch/misc ($200). Scaling: ECS auto-scales triage-api on ALB request rate; Redis sorted set queue handles 500 concurrent contacts without performance degradation; RDS read replica for analytics queries to avoid impacting triage write path.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Twilio SMS ingestion with HMAC validation, Claude Haiku triage (3 risk levels), no-silent-failure safety wrapper, counselor queue dashboard with SSE, KMS-encrypted transcript storage, append-only audit log, Cognito MFA for counselors, ECS Fargate deployment, Datadog alerting, acute queue depth PagerDuty alert. Pilot deployment with one partner crisis organization, 50 concurrent counselors. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Low-confidence escalation threshold tuning based on pilot data, prompt management admin interface, counselor performance analytics, supervisor role and reassignment UI, weekly operational report generation. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Spanish-language triage prompt variant, partner crisis service escalation routing table, 988 Lifeline national routing API integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: safety_wrapper decorator must be applied to every method in TriageOrchestrator — no triage logic runs without it. All SQLAlchemy queries use async sessions from the connection pool — no synchronous database calls in the FastAPI event loop. Phone number hashing must occur in TwilioIngressModule before any other processing — unit tests must verify no raw phone number appears in any log output. LLM system prompt retrieved from Parameter Store at application startup and cached; prompt version logged in every triage_assessments row for reproducibility. Any change to triage routing logic, risk level thresholds, or escalation rules requires a PR with clinical advisory team sign-off documented in the PR description. Append-only audit log integrity: application database role has INSERT only on triage_audit_log — verify this in the Alembic migration test suite.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LLM misclassification of acute-risk texter as low-risk routing them to automated flow. Mitigation: confidence_score <0.80 threshold forces counselor escalation regardless of risk_level prediction; pilot phase includes weekly clinical review of all low-confidence triage decisions to calibrate threshold. Risk: Bedrock API regional outage causing complete triage system failure. Mitigation: EscalationModule.escalate_immediately() is designed to function without Bedrock — pure PostgreSQL + Redis operations; all contacts routed to counselors during Bedrock outage. Risk: Twilio webhook replay attack if auth token is compromised. Mitigation: Twilio signature validation uses request URL + body — replaying to a different URL or after auth token rotation fails validation. Tradeoff: Jinja2 server-rendered counselor portal over React SPA — eliminates build complexity and JS bundle maintenance at the cost of slightly less dynamic UX; acceptable for MVP counselor team of 50. Tradeoff: Claude Haiku over Claude Sonnet — 3x lower cost and 2x lower latency at acceptable accuracy for binary acute/non-acute triage; Sonnet available as escalation path if pilot accuracy is insufficient.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Claude Haiku via Bedrock selected over OpenAI GPT-4o-mini for AWS BAA PHI coverage in API payloads. Phone number hashing with per-contact salt selected over encryption to prevent reverse lookup while preserving re-contact capability via hash lookup. Structured JSON output mode selected over free-text parsing for reliable confidence_score extraction. 0.80 confidence threshold is a starting value for pilot — clinical team will review after 30 days of real data. Jurisdiction-specific mandatory reporting rules to be implemented as a configurable rule table in Phase 2 after legal review completes.

metadata.notes: Life-safety-critical crisis text line AI triage; no-silent-failure safety wrapper pattern and KMS per-contact encryption are the defining architectural decisions; strong plan quality with detailed safety controls.

profile.domain: Behavioral Health EHR / Cloud Infrastructure Modernization

project_goal.value: Kubernetes-based infrastructure modernization for a behavioral health EHR vendor, migrating from on-premise bare-metal servers running legacy Java monoliths to a cloud-native, HIPAA-compliant deployment architecture on GCP GKE Autopilot
project_goal.rationale: Bare-metal infrastructure is end-of-life in 18 months; cloud-native migration enables horizontal scaling and operational efficiency

target_users.value: Internal engineering and operations teams at the EHR vendor; end-user EHR clinicians and behavioral health staff are downstream beneficiaries of improved reliability
target_users.rationale: Infrastructure project serves internal teams; clinician experience improvements are indirect

project_class.rationale: Platform and environment architecture — Terraform, Kubernetes, networking — not a user-facing application

capabilities.rationale: No frontend or auth capabilities in scope — infrastructure project; containerization of existing Java services and database migration are the core work

complexity_level.rationale: Multiple Java monolith services, Oracle-to-PostgreSQL database migration, HIPAA compliance controls on GCP, and zero-downtime cutover requirement

risk_level.rationale: Live behavioral health EHR data being migrated; HIPAA breach notification requirements; database cutover risk with patient record integrity obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: EHR system contains behavioral health PHI including psychiatric diagnoses, medication records, and therapy session notes

external_exposure.rationale: EHR system is not public-facing; clinician access requires VPN + SSO authentication via Keycloak

access_model.value: Keycloak SSO for all EHR service access; GCP IAM for infrastructure access with principle of least privilege; break-glass procedure for emergency access with full audit logging
access_model.rationale: Existing Keycloak SSO migrated to GKE; GCP IAM replaces on-premise AD for infrastructure access

observability_baseline.rationale: Standard GKE observability stack with existing Grafana expertise

execution_preference.value: Iterative service-by-service migration with parallel run validation before each cutover; each service migration is an independent deployment
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces migration risk by never cutting over all services simultaneously

project.target_users: Internal engineering and operations teams

backend.registry: Google Artifact Registry with Binary Authorization signing

data.target_db: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL Enterprise Plus
data.migration: Striim for CDC replication; Pgloader for initial bulk load

devops.iac: Terraform (GKE, VPC, Cloud SQL, Memorystore, Secret Manager)

open_questions.connection_pooling: PgBouncer vs Cloud SQL Auth Proxy for Spring Boot connection management — analysis pending

reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migration is the highest-risk component of this project. The recommended approach is Striim CDC for continuous replication during the parallel-run phase, with Pgloader handling the initial bulk schema and data transfer. Each Spring Boot service must be validated against the PostgreSQL replica before any cutover — the parallel run window should be at least 2 weeks per service to catch any Oracle-specific SQL that Pgloader schema conversion missed. Cloud SQL Auth Proxy as a sidecar is the recommended connection approach for GKE pods as it handles IAM-based authentication without embedding database credentials in the application.
reasoner_reviews.security: VPC Service Controls is the correct network isolation approach for HIPAA on GCP, but the perimeter configuration must be set up before any data migration begins — not retrofitted after services are running. Binary Authorization must be configured in 'warn' mode initially during migration, then switched to 'enforce' mode in production before each service goes live. The GCP BAA addendum must explicitly list each GCP service that handles PHI: GKE, Cloud SQL, GCS, Cloud Logging, Secret Manager.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour RTO requires that the database restore procedure be rehearsed in a staging environment before the first production cutover. Cloud SQL automated backups with point-in-time recovery (PITR) enabled satisfies the 1-hour RPO. The Cloud SQL failover to the standby replica should be tested quarterly. The 4-hour maintenance window for database cutover is tight — the cutover runbook must be rehearsed and timed in staging to confirm it fits within the window including rollback.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The Round 1 plan lacked a rollback procedure for database cutover — if the Oracle-to-PostgreSQL cutover fails mid-window, there is no documented path back to the Oracle source without data loss. The Striim CDC replication must continue running in parallel during the cutover window so that rollback to Oracle is possible by simply re-pointing the Spring Boot services at the Oracle JDBC URL, with Striim having kept Oracle current during the GKE switchover attempt.
reasoner_reviews.product: The Kubernetes migration must preserve behavioral health EHR data access patterns — clinicians cannot tolerate degraded query latency during the cutover window. The phased namespace-per-service migration approach reduces blast radius for rollbacks. Prioritise migrating stateless services first to validate the k8s networking model before cutting over the Oracle-dependent clinical data services.

backend.service_design: 6 Spring Boot services containerized iteratively: patient-service, clinical-notes-service, scheduling-service, billing-service, reporting-service, auth-proxy-service. Each service gets a multi-stage Dockerfile (build stage with Maven, runtime stage with eclipse-temurin:21-jre). No code changes — containerization only. Cloud SQL Auth Proxy deployed as a sidecar container in each Pod's spec to handle PostgreSQL IAM authentication.
backend.scaling_notes: GKE Autopilot manages node provisioning. Each Spring Boot Deployment configured with HPA: min 2 replicas, max 10, target CPU 60%. patient-service and clinical-notes-service are highest-traffic and have dedicated resource requests: 1 vCPU, 2Gi memory per pod.
backend.failure_handling: Kubernetes liveness probes on Spring Boot Actuator /health/liveness; readiness probes on /health/readiness. PodDisruptionBudget for each service: maxUnavailable 1 during rolling updates. ArgoCD performs rolling update with 0 maxUnavailable for production deployments.

security.auth_design: Workload Identity binds each GKE service account to a GCP service account with IAM roles scoped to only the GCP APIs it needs (e.g., patient-service GKE SA bound to cloud-sql-client role only). Keycloak deployed in GKE as a StatefulSet with PostgreSQL backend, fronted by a GKE Ingress with Google-managed TLS certificate.
security.secrets_management: All Spring Boot application secrets (database passwords replaced by Workload Identity, Keycloak client secrets, third-party API keys) stored in GCP Secret Manager. External Secrets Operator syncs secrets to Kubernetes Secrets in each namespace. Secret rotation triggers a rolling pod restart via a mutation webhook.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: GKE audit logging enabled (data access audit logs for all GCP APIs). Cloud Logging PHI-scrubbing log sink processes application logs before storage — strips content matching PHI patterns. Cloud Logging locked bucket for audit logs (7-year retention, deletion prevention). Access Transparency logs enabled for GCP support access events.

data.entities: Infrastructure entities in Terraform state: ehr_services (6 Spring Boot Deployment manifests), deployment_manifests (ArgoCD Application CRDs per service), migration_runbooks (versioned Google Docs linked from ADRs), infrastructure_components (Terraform modules: gke-cluster, vpc-network, cloud-sql-instance, memorystore-redis, artifact-registry, secret-manager-secrets), compliance_controls (HIPAA control mappings: encryption at rest CMEK, network isolation VPC SC, audit logging Cloud Logging). Database migration: Oracle 11g source schema → Pgloader schema conversion → PostgreSQL 16 target schema with Striim CDC for continuous sync during parallel run.
data.migration_notes: Oracle 11g to PostgreSQL 16 migration via Pgloader for schema conversion (handles NUMBER→NUMERIC, VARCHAR2→VARCHAR, DATE→TIMESTAMP mappings) plus initial data load. Striim CDC streams Oracle redo log changes to PostgreSQL during parallel run period (2 weeks per service). Parallel validation script runs hourly row count comparisons across critical tables (patient_records, clinical_notes, medications, appointments). Cutover checklist: stop writes to Oracle, verify Striim replication lag is 0, run final validation script, re-point Spring Boot JDBC URL to Cloud SQL Auth Proxy, start Kubernetes readiness probe validation.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 with three namespaces: dev (shared by all engineers), staging (mirrors production config, used for migration rehearsal), production (VPC Service Controls perimeter, Binary Authorization enforced). Separate GCP projects per environment: ehr-dev, ehr-staging, ehr-production.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline per service: Maven test + build, Docker multi-stage build, Cosign image signing, push to Artifact Registry, update image tag in the service's ArgoCD Application manifest repo. ArgoCD watches the manifest repository and applies changes to GKE (automatic sync for dev/staging, manual sync with approval gate for production). ArgoCD automated rollback: if new deployment fails liveness probe within 5 minutes, ArgoCD reverts to previous image tag automatically.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Spring Boot Actuator /metrics endpoints via PodMonitor CRDs. Grafana dashboards: per-service pod count, JVM heap usage, request latency P50/P95, Cloud SQL connection pool saturation, Oracle-to-PostgreSQL replication lag during migration (Striim metric). Alertmanager routes to PagerDuty: pod CrashLoopBackOff, Cloud SQL IOPS saturation, Striim replication lag >5 minutes during migration.
devops.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD manages all Helm/Kustomize releases. Image tags are immutable (SHA-based from Artifact Registry). Rollback procedure: ArgoCD UI or CLI sets desired image tag to previous SHA; rolling update applies; total rollback time <5 minutes for a single service. Database rollback: Striim CDC keeps Oracle current during migration window, allowing re-point to Oracle JDBC URL within maintenance window if cutover fails.

ISS-001.title: No database cutover rollback procedure documented
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan describes the Oracle-to-PostgreSQL cutover but contains no explicit rollback procedure. If cutover fails mid-window, there is no documented path to restore Oracle-based operations without data loss risk.

ISS-002.title: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy connection approach not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentions PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL but does not specify how Spring Boot services will authenticate to Cloud SQL in GKE. Workload Identity with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar is the correct pattern; embedding database credentials in Secrets is the wrong approach for HIPAA compliance.

focus_issues.title: No database cutover rollback procedure documented
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan has no rollback path if Oracle-to-PostgreSQL cutover fails mid-maintenance-window, risking data loss or extended downtime beyond the 4-hour RTO.

focus_issues.title: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy connection approach not specified
focus_issues.detail: Spring Boot to Cloud SQL authentication mechanism undefined; must use Workload Identity + Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar, not embedded credentials.

Specify Cloud SQL Auth Proxy as sidecar container in all Spring Boot pod specs with Workload Identity binding; remove any database password references from application Secrets

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses two focus issues. ISS-001 (critical): the database cutover rollback procedure requires Striim CDC to remain active throughout the maintenance window, enabling rollback to Oracle by simply re-pointing the Spring Boot JDBC URL — this must be explicit in the data_model and deployment_and_operations sections. ISS-002: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar must be specified in the backend service design with Workload Identity binding in Terraform — no database passwords in Kubernetes Secrets. Both fixes are self-contained; the rest of the Round 1 GKE architecture is sound.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit database cutover rollback procedure to data_model and deployment_and_operations sections: Striim CDC continues running during the 4-hour maintenance window, keeping Oracle current with all PostgreSQL writes via reverse replication. Rollback procedure: if cutover validation fails, re-point Spring Boot JDBC_URL environment variable to Oracle JDBC URL via ConfigMap update and rolling restart (<15 minutes), with Striim having maintained Oracle consistency throughout. Rollback capability expires when Striim CDC is decommissioned after 2-week post-cutover validation window.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Zero data loss guaranteed during cutover window via Striim CDC rollback capability. 4-hour RTO maintained even in failure scenario. Explicit rollback checklist added to the cutover runbook.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Cloud SQL Auth Proxy as a sidecar container in every Spring Boot Pod spec. Each Deployment has a cloudsql-proxy container running the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy binary, binding to 127.0.0.1:5432 inside the pod. Spring Boot JDBC URL points to 127.0.0.1:5432 (localhost). Workload Identity binds each GKE Kubernetes service account to a GCP service account with roles/cloudsql.client IAM role only. No database passwords stored in Kubernetes Secrets — authentication is handled by Workload Identity token exchange. Terraform module updated to create one GCP service account per EHR service with scoped IAM binding.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Zero database credentials in Kubernetes Secrets or application configuration. Each pod authenticates to Cloud SQL via Workload Identity, satisfying HIPAA access control requirements and eliminating credential rotation risk.

target_output.title: Behavioral Health EHR GKE Migration — Round 2 Cutover Runbook and Auth Fix
target_output.executive_summary: Round 2 revision of the behavioral health EHR GKE infrastructure modernization plan, resolving two critical gaps from Round 1: an explicit Oracle-to-PostgreSQL database cutover rollback procedure using Striim CDC continuity during the maintenance window, and the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar pattern with Workload Identity eliminating database credentials from Kubernetes Secrets. The GKE Autopilot, Terraform IaC, ArgoCD GitOps, and VPC Service Controls architecture from Round 1 are retained.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster in GCP us-central1 managing three namespaces across three GCP projects (ehr-dev, ehr-staging, ehr-production). Six Java 21 Spring Boot EHR services containerized with multi-stage Dockerfiles and deployed via ArgoCD GitOps from a manifest repository. Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar in every pod handles PostgreSQL connection authentication via Workload Identity — no database credentials in Kubernetes Secrets. VPC Service Controls perimeter isolates ehr-production, with Private Google Access on all GKE nodes (no public IPs). Oracle 11g on-premise is migrated to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via Pgloader initial load and Striim CDC continuous replication. The Striim CDC stream remains active during the cutover maintenance window to maintain Oracle rollback capability until post-cutover validation completes. Binary Authorization policy enforces Cosign-signed images in production namespace.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Java 21 with Spring Boot, eclipse-temurin:21-jre base image. Containerization: Docker multi-stage builds, Cosign image signing, Google Artifact Registry. Orchestration: GKE Autopilot, Kubernetes 1.29, Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. IaC: Terraform (GKE, VPC, Cloud SQL, Memorystore, Artifact Registry, Secret Manager, IAM Workload Identity bindings). GitOps: ArgoCD, GitLab CI. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 Enterprise Plus with PITR, Redis 7 Memorystore. Migration: Pgloader (initial schema + bulk load), Striim CDC (continuous replication from Oracle redo log). Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring, Alertmanager, PagerDuty. Security: VPC Service Controls, Binary Authorization, External Secrets Operator, GCP Secret Manager, Cloud Armor (ingress WAF).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Service Containerization: 6 Spring Boot Deployments in GKE (patient-service, clinical-notes-service, scheduling-service, billing-service, reporting-service, auth-proxy-service), each with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar and HPA (min 2, max 10 replicas). Database Migration: Pgloader bulk schema conversion from Oracle 11g DDL to PostgreSQL 16, initial data load to Cloud SQL, Striim CDC job streaming Oracle redo log changes to Cloud SQL during parallel run. GitOps Pipeline: GitLab CI builds/signs container images; ArgoCD Application CRDs per service auto-sync to dev/staging, manual-sync with approval for production. Compliance Infrastructure: VPC Service Controls perimeter, Workload Identity per service, CMEK for Cloud SQL and GCS, Cloud Logging locked bucket for audit log retention. Disaster Recovery: Cloud SQL PITR (RPO 1h), documented DR runbook rehearsed quarterly in staging (RTO 4h).
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1, 3 namespaces: dev/staging/production, Workload Identity enabled, Binary Authorization policy). 6 Spring Boot Deployments (each with app container + cloud-sql-proxy sidecar, HPA, PodDisruptionBudget, liveness/readiness probes on Spring Actuator). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 Enterprise Plus (Multi-AZ, PITR enabled, CMEK, Private IP only, Cloud SQL Auth Proxy for all connections). Redis 7 Memorystore (Private Service Connect). Google Artifact Registry (container images, Cosign attestation metadata). ArgoCD (GitOps controller watching manifest repository, automated rollback on failed liveness probe). GitLab CI (Maven build, Docker build, Cosign sign, Artifact Registry push, manifest SHA update). Striim CDC appliance (Oracle redo log reader, PostgreSQL writer, replication lag metric exported to Prometheus). External Secrets Operator (syncs GCP Secret Manager secrets to Kubernetes Secrets, rotation triggers rolling restart). VPC Service Controls perimeter (ehr-production GCP project, Access Policy with ingress rules for VPN CIDR only). Cloud Logging locked bucket (audit logs, 7-year retention, deletion prevention enabled).
target_output.workflows: Service Migration Workflow (per service, repeated 6 times): containerize Spring Boot service with multi-stage Dockerfile and Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar spec, build and Cosign-sign image in GitLab CI, deploy to ehr-dev namespace via ArgoCD, run integration tests against Cloud SQL PostgreSQL replica, promote to ehr-staging via manifest SHA update in GitLab CI, run 2-week parallel validation (service reads/writes PostgreSQL while Oracle remains primary), confirm hourly row count parity between Oracle and Cloud SQL via validation script, submit production cutover request with clinical sign-off. Database Cutover Procedure (per service): schedule 4-hour maintenance window with clinic operations team, enable maintenance page, stop write traffic to Oracle for this service's data domain, confirm Striim CDC lag = 0 (Oracle fully replicated to Cloud SQL), update Spring Boot JDBC_URL ConfigMap to Cloud SQL Auth Proxy localhost:5432, rolling restart of Deployment pods, run smoke tests against Cloud SQL, if smoke tests pass: disable maintenance page, update Striim job to monitor-only mode. Rollback Procedure (ISS-001 fix): if smoke tests fail during cutover window, Striim CDC has kept Oracle current — update JDBC_URL ConfigMap back to Oracle JDBC URL, rolling restart, verify Oracle connectivity, disable maintenance page; Striim reverse replication ensures Oracle has all writes made during cutover attempt.
target_output.data_model: Infrastructure state managed in Terraform: ehr_services (Deployment manifests for 6 Spring Boot services, each with cloud-sql-proxy sidecar spec and Workload Identity annotation). deployment_manifests (ArgoCD Application CRDs per service pointing to manifest repository paths). migration_runbooks (Oracle-to-PostgreSQL cutover checklist including rollback procedure per service, stored in version-controlled repository and referenced from ADRs). infrastructure_components (Terraform modules: gke-cluster, vpc-network with VPC Service Controls, cloud-sql-instance with PITR and CMEK, memorystore-redis, artifact-registry, workload-identity-bindings per service, external-secrets-operator). compliance_controls (HIPAA control evidence: CMEK key IDs per resource, VPC SC perimeter policy, Cloud Logging locked bucket ID, Binary Authorization policy attestation, Workload Identity binding documentation). disaster_recovery_plans (Cloud SQL PITR configuration, RPO: 1h from PITR, RTO: 4h from DR runbook, quarterly DR drill results in staging). Behavioral health clinical domain (EHR service data contracts): patient_records (patient_id UUID PK, mrn_hash VARCHAR, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, care_team_json JSONB, active_treatment_plan_id UUID, last_clinical_encounter_date DATE), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, encounter_type ENUM intake/session/group/crisis, clinician_id UUID, encounter_date DATE, clinical_notes_ref S3_ARN, vitals_json JSONB), prescriptions (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, medication_name VARCHAR, dosage VARCHAR, prescriber_id UUID, pharmacy_id UUID, status ENUM active/discontinued, prescribed_at DATE).
target_output.api_design: No user-facing API endpoints — infrastructure project. Internal integration points: Spring Boot to Cloud SQL via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar at 127.0.0.1:5432 (Workload Identity authenticated, no password). Spring Boot to Secret Manager via Workload Identity (External Secrets Operator syncs to K8s Secrets at pod start). GitLab CI to Artifact Registry (service account token, push image + Cosign attestation). ArgoCD to GKE API (Workload Identity from ArgoCD namespace, cluster-admin in managed namespaces). Striim CDC: Oracle JDBC (redo log reader) to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL JDBC (writer) — Striim job config specifies connection strings and replication tables. Prometheus scrape targets: Spring Boot Actuator /metrics, Striim replication_lag_seconds metric, Cloud SQL metrics via Cloud Monitoring adapter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Workload Identity: every Spring Boot service has a dedicated Kubernetes service account annotated with a GCP service account email; GCP service account has only roles/cloudsql.client (no broader IAM). Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar spec in each Deployment: container named 'cloud-sql-proxy', image gcr.io/cloud-sql-connectors/cloud-sql-proxy:2.x, args including instance connection name and --port=5432 --auto-iam-authn. No GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS or database password in any environment variable or Kubernetes Secret. VPC Service Controls: ehr-production GCP project in a VPC SC perimeter; only VPN CIDR (clinic VPN and GKE node pools) allowed as ingress; Cloud SQL, GCS, Secret Manager, Artifact Registry all within perimeter. Binary Authorization: attestor configured in Artifact Registry; production namespace policy requires Cosign attestation before pod admission; policy in 'enforce' mode after first service cutover. HIPAA controls: CMEK on Cloud SQL (Cloud KMS key in ehr-production project), CMEK on GCS audit archive, Cloud Logging locked bucket (7-year retention, deletionProtection: true), Access Transparency logs enabled, Cloud Audit Logs: data access logging enabled for Cloud SQL, GCS, Secret Manager. GCP BAA addendum covers GKE, Cloud SQL, GCS, Cloud Logging, Secret Manager, Artifact Registry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform state stored in GCS bucket with versioning and CMEK in ehr-infra project (separate from workload projects). Terraform workspaces: dev, staging, production. GitLab CI Terraform pipeline: validate, plan (reviewed in MR), apply on merge to main (dev/staging auto-apply, production requires manual approval). GitLab CI service pipeline per Spring Boot service: mvn test, docker build (multi-stage), cosign sign --key gcpkms://..., docker push to Artifact Registry, update image SHA in manifest repository, ArgoCD auto-sync triggers rolling update (0 maxUnavailable in production). Production deployment approval gate: GitLab CI manual job; for services in migration phase, requires database migration sign-off comment in MR. ArgoCD automated rollback: if new Deployment pods fail liveness probe (Spring Actuator /health/liveness) within 5 minutes of rollout, ArgoCD reverts to previous image SHA automatically. Post-cutover validation: 2-week parallel monitoring period with hourly Oracle-Cloud SQL row count parity checks before Striim CDC is decommissioned for a given service domain.
target_output.observability: Prometheus PodMonitor CRDs scrape Spring Boot Actuator /metrics endpoints. Grafana dashboards: per-service pod count and restart count (alert on CrashLoopBackOff), JVM heap usage P95, Cloud SQL connection pool active vs max (alert >80%), Cloud SQL IOPS vs quota, Striim oracle_to_cloudsql_replication_lag_seconds (alert >300s during migration window, critical >600s), Cloud SQL PITR recovery point freshness. Cloud Logging: structured JSON from all Spring Boot services with EHR correlation_id field for patient record access tracing. Cloud Monitoring SLO: availability >99.9% per service per month. Alertmanager routes to PagerDuty: CrashLoopBackOff for any production pod, Cloud SQL IOPS saturation, Striim replication lag >10 minutes, Cloud Logging export failures (audit log integrity risk).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $8,500/month post-migration: GKE Autopilot node costs for 6 services at steady state ($2,800), Cloud SQL Enterprise Plus PostgreSQL ($2,400 including standby), Memorystore Redis ($600), Artifact Registry ($100), Cloud Logging and Monitoring ($400), VPN (existing), Striim license during migration period ($1,200 amortized, decommissioned after migration completes), misc GCP services ($1,000). Comparison to bare-metal: eliminates $12,000/month in data center colocation costs for EHR infrastructure — net savings of $3,500/month after migration. GKE Autopilot auto-scales per-service; no reserved instances needed with Autopilot. Cloud SQL scales storage automatically; compute tier can be right-sized after 90 days of production metrics.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Foundation (Weeks 1-8, MVP): Terraform provisions GKE Autopilot cluster, VPC with VPC Service Controls perimeter, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 Enterprise Plus, Memorystore Redis, Artifact Registry, External Secrets Operator, ArgoCD. GitLab CI pipeline for first two services (patient-service, clinical-notes-service). Pgloader schema conversion and initial data load to Cloud SQL. Striim CDC job configured and validated. First two services deployed to ehr-dev with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. Phase 2 — Iterative Migration (Weeks 9-24): Remaining 4 Spring Boot services containerized and migrated using the established pattern. Each service follows 2-week parallel validation then cutover. Oracle-to-PostgreSQL cutover with rollback procedure (ISS-001 fix) executed per service in maintenance windows. Phase 3 — Hardening (Weeks 25-32): Binary Authorization enforce mode enabled production-wide, quarterly DR drill in staging, Cloud SQL PITR restoration test, post-migration cost right-sizing, Striim CDC decommissioned after all services validated, bare-metal decommission schedule confirmed.
target_output.development_guidelines: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar is mandatory in every Spring Boot Deployment spec — no exceptions and no database passwords in Kubernetes Secrets or application properties files. Workload Identity annotation on every Kubernetes service account must be added in Terraform — never applied manually via kubectl. All Terraform changes go through GitLab CI plan review in MR before apply; no direct terraform apply from developer workstations against staging or production. ArgoCD Application manifests are the single source of truth for what is running in each namespace — no kubectl apply of workload resources outside ArgoCD. Striim CDC replication lag must be zero before any database cutover is initiated — the cutover runbook has an explicit gate checking the Grafana metric before proceeding. Binary Authorization attestation is required for all production images — CI pipeline fails if cosign sign fails. ADR documents required for: any change to Workload Identity IAM bindings, VPC Service Controls perimeter rules, or Binary Authorization policy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle-specific SQL functions (Oracle-only date arithmetic, sequences, CONNECT BY hierarchical queries) causing runtime errors post-cutover despite Pgloader schema conversion. Mitigation: 2-week parallel validation period runs the migrated service against PostgreSQL while Oracle remains primary; error rate dashboards compared between the two; any Oracle-specific SQL surfaced as application bugs fixed before production cutover. Risk: Striim CDC license cost and operational complexity during migration period. Mitigation: Striim decommissioned per service within 2 weeks of successful cutover; total Striim period is 18 months maximum; cost included in migration budget. Risk: VPC Service Controls perimeter misconfiguration blocking legitimate GKE node traffic. Mitigation: perimeter configured in dry-run mode for 2 weeks before enforcing; VPC SC violations logged to Cloud Logging and reviewed before switching to enforce. Tradeoff: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar over pg_bouncer connection pooler — Auth Proxy handles Workload Identity complexity and is Google-supported; PgBouncer provides better connection pooling but adds configuration overhead; Auth Proxy selected for HIPAA auth compliance, PgBouncer can be added if connection count becomes a bottleneck. Tradeoff: GKE Autopilot over Standard mode — Autopilot eliminates node management and is better for HIPAA because Google manages node OS patching, at the cost of less control over node configuration; acceptable given the team's small ops headcount.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar selected over PgBouncer for Workload Identity authentication compliance (ISS-002 resolved). Striim CDC selected over pg_logical or AWS DMS for Oracle redo log compatibility and rollback capability (ISS-001 resolved). Binary Authorization in enforce mode from Day 1 in production — warn mode used only during initial staging validation. Oracle 11g decommission date set to 6 months after last service cutover to allow emergency rollback window. Workload Identity binding per service confirmed as Terraform-managed to prevent manual IAM drift.

metadata.notes: Round 2 infrastructure migration revision; ISS-001 critical rollback gap resolved via Striim CDC continuity pattern; ISS-002 Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar with Workload Identity specified; behavioral health EHR GKE modernization on GCP.

project.goal: Migrate WordPress content to Hugo static site preserving SEO

data.redirects: _redirects file mapping 200+ old WordPress slugs to new Hugo paths

devops.ci: GitHub Actions running htmlproofer and redirect validation before deploy hook
devops.environments: main branch deploys to production; PRs get preview URLs

reasoner_reviews.product: Content parity is the primary success criterion. The 200+ resource pages represent years of SEO equity; any broken links or missing redirects directly harm community access to mental health resources. Crisis hotline contact information must be prominently placed and tested first.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Hugo is well-suited for this corpus size with sub-second build times per page. The custom Tailwind theme should use Hugo taxonomy system for resource categories. The redirect map from WordPress pretty permalinks to Hugo URL structure needs systematic generation and CI-gated validation before cutover.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static hosting on Netlify eliminates the PHP vulnerability surface from WordPress. CSP headers configured in netlify.toml prevent XSS from third-party script injection. Netlify Forms must be configured for email-forward-only mode to avoid retaining any visitor contact data.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 60-second build constraint is achievable with Hugo parallel rendering for 200 pages. The content freeze window must be communicated to staff one week before cutover to prevent post-migration content gaps.

security.auth_design: No authentication required; public read-only site with no login functionality
security.secrets_management: Netlify environment variables for GA4 measurement ID only; no secrets stored in repository
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify Forms rate limiting for contact form submissions; honeypot field on all Netlify Forms to block bot submissions

data.entities: resource_pages (slug, title, category, last_reviewed_date, content in Markdown), staff_profiles (name, title, bio, photo_path in /data/ YAML), news_posts (slug, date, author, body in Markdown), redirect_rules (source_path, target_path, status_code in _redirects)
data.storage_design: Git repository as single source of truth; content in /content/ directory with Hugo taxonomy for service categories and target populations
data.retention_and_deletion: No data retained by Netlify platform; Forms configured for email-forward-only mode; Git history retains all content versions

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN serves static assets from global edge nodes; GitHub Actions validates build before Netlify build hook triggers deploy
