devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: checkout repo, Hugo build, htmlproofer on /public directory, redirect-validation script parsing _redirects and checking HTTP 301 responses, POST to Netlify deploy hook on all checks pass
devops.rollback_strategy: Netlify atomic deploys enable one-click rollback to any previous deploy from Netlify dashboard with zero downtime

frontend.app_structure: Hugo static site with custom Tailwind CSS theme; content in Markdown with Hugo front matter; Hugo Pipes handles asset bundling and CSS purging
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home, Resource Pages (200+), Service Descriptions, Staff Profiles, News, Crisis Hotline Directory, Donor Portal CTA

fix_report.expected_outcome: No redirect regressions reach production; CI blocks deploy if any WordPress URL is unmapped or returns wrong status code.

target_output.title: Community Mental Health Center Hugo Migration Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: Hugo static site replacing an unmaintained WordPress installation for a community mental health center, hosted on Netlify CDN. Migration preserves 200+ resource pages including service descriptions, staff profiles, crisis hotline pages, and news posts. GitHub Actions CI now validates all WordPress-to-Hugo redirects before any production deploy fires.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture: Hugo SSG reads Markdown content files from Git and builds a complete static HTML/CSS site. No server-side runtime exists at request time. Netlify CDN serves built assets from global edge nodes. GitHub Actions pipeline handles build validation, htmlproofer link checking, and redirect map verification before triggering the Netlify deploy hook. All 200+ old WordPress URLs are mapped in a _redirects file that Netlify processes natively, issuing HTTP 301 responses to new Hugo URL slugs on any incoming legacy path request.
target_output.technology_stack: Hugo SSG with Go template layouts and Hugo Pipes for Tailwind CSS asset bundling. GitHub Actions for CI with htmlproofer and redirect-validation bash script. Netlify CDN for atomic deploy hosting. Google Analytics 4 via Hugo partial template injected in base layout. Netlify Forms with email-forward-only mode for contact pages.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Resource Pages: 200+ mental health service pages organized via Hugo taxonomy by service type and target population. Staff Profiles: Hugo data files powering team directory with photos. News Posts: Hugo archetypes for dated articles with category tags. Crisis Hotline Section: High-visibility partial rendered in every page header. Contact Forms: Netlify Forms with honeypot spam protection.
target_output.system_components: Hugo build process generating /public directory. Git repository with /content/resources, /content/staff, /content/news directories. _redirects file with 200+ WordPress slug mappings. netlify.toml with security headers. GitHub Actions workflow stages: lint, build, htmlproofer, redirect-validate, deploy hook. Netlify CDN with edge caching and atomic rollback capability.
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: Staff author edits Markdown file in /content, opens pull request, GitHub Actions builds preview deploy on Netlify, staff reviews at unique preview URL, PR merged triggers production deploy pipeline. Redirect Validation Workflow: CI job parses _redirects file line by line, extracts source paths, runs curl requests against locally served Hugo build output, asserts each source path returns HTTP 301 to correct target path; any non-301 response fails the job and blocks the Netlify deploy hook from receiving the trigger.
target_output.data_model: resource_pages: Hugo content files with front matter fields slug, title, content_type (service/crisis/educational), target_population, date_last_reviewed. staff_profiles: YAML data files in /data/staff/ with name, credentials, role, bio, photo filename. news_posts: Markdown with front matter date, author, summary, tags. redirect_rules: plain-text entries in _redirects as 'source_path target_path 301'. Hugo taxonomy terms for service categories and populations stored in /config/taxonomies.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Netlify Forms endpoint receives POST from contact form HTML; configured for email-forward-only to staff inbox. GA4 Measurement Protocol not used; client-side gtag.js handles all analytics events. Netlify deploy hook (POST webhook URL) triggered by GitHub Actions after all CI checks pass.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify with automatic TLS certificate renewal via Let's Encrypt. Security headers in netlify.toml: Content-Security-Policy restricting scripts to self and GA4 domains, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Netlify Forms honeypot field blocks automated bot submissions. No visitor data retained on platform; form submissions forwarded to staff email only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: checkout, Hugo build to /public, htmlproofer crawls /public for broken internal links, redirect-validation script parses _redirects and curls each source path against local server asserting 301 status, on all checks pass POST to Netlify deploy hook. Netlify performs atomic swap of CDN edge assets. Rollback via Netlify dashboard one-click to any prior atomic deploy. No server management required post-deploy.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 tracks pageviews, crisis hotline link clicks as custom event crisis_link_click, contact form submissions as form_submit. Netlify Analytics provides bandwidth and unique visitor counts without cookie-consent requirements. No uptime monitoring needed for CDN-served static content.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Netlify free tier covers hosting for typical community health center traffic volumes. GA4 free tier. GitHub Actions free-tier minutes sufficient for sub-60-second Hugo builds. CDN edge caching handles traffic spikes during mental health awareness campaigns without origin scaling.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Export and convert all 200+ WordPress pages to Hugo Markdown, build Tailwind theme matching existing visual design, generate _redirects map from WordPress XML sitemap export. Phase 2 (Weeks 4-5): CI pipeline with redirect validation live, staff preview review, content sign-off. Phase 3 (Week 6): DNS cutover to Netlify, post-launch redirect smoke test against live domain, WordPress set to read-only maintenance mode.
target_output.development_guidelines: Hugo content authors use archetype templates to ensure consistent front matter fields. All new redirects appended to _redirects with CI enforcing no duplicate source paths. Tailwind configured with content paths for purging unused CSS from production builds.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Missing redirects post-launch causing SEO ranking drops for key mental health service pages. Mitigation: CI redirect-validation step now blocks deploy on any unmapped source path. Tradeoff: Hugo Go templates have a steeper learning curve for staff vs WordPress; accepted because it eliminates ongoing server maintenance. Risk: Netlify free tier bandwidth limits during high-traffic awareness campaigns. Mitigation: Netlify Pro upgrade budgeted if monthly bandwidth exceeds free tier threshold.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Netlify Forms chosen over self-hosted form backend to maintain zero-server constraint. GA4 chosen over self-hosted Matomo to avoid additional infrastructure. Decap CMS deferred to Phase 2 after migration stability confirmed over 30 days.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of WordPress-to-Hugo migration for community mental health center; fixes redirect validation CI gap; plan quality deliberately weak with thinner sections.

profile.domain: Multilingual Mental Wellness App Landing Page

project_goal.value: Globally distributed landing page for a multilingual mental wellness app targeting emerging markets, with locale-specific content variants and a lead capture flow that scales during paid social media campaign bursts
project_goal.rationale: Primary acquisition surface for app installs across 8 locales

target_users.value: Prospective app users across emerging markets; primary locales: EN, ES, PT-BR, HI, ID, SW, FR, AR
target_users.rationale: App targets users in Latin America, South/Southeast Asia, and Sub-Saharan Africa

project_class.rationale: No persistent backend; pure acquisition funnel with edge-function form handling

capabilities.rationale: Static locale pages, GA4 plus Mixpanel analytics, Klaviyo email integration for lead capture

complexity_level.rationale: Edge-rendered locale variants with form submission; no persistent data store

risk_level.rationale: No PII beyond email collection; GDPR consent handled at CMP layer

data_sensitivity.rationale: Email addresses passed directly to Klaviyo; not persisted in app infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: Public acquisition landing page open to all visitors globally

access_model.value: Public, unauthenticated; no login required for any visitor
access_model.rationale: Pre-install acquisition funnel with no account creation

feature_scope.value: Locale-specific hero copy and CTAs, app store download links, feature highlights, social proof testimonials, lead email capture form, A/B test variant routing, GDPR consent banner for EU visitors
feature_scope.rationale: Full acquisition funnel features across all 8 locales

mvp_scope.value: All 8 locale variants live, lead capture routing to Klaviyo, GA4 plus Mixpanel tracking, GDPR CMP for EU locales
mvp_scope.rationale: Campaign launch requires all locales day one

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel, GDPR consent management platform for EU locales, no server-side secrets exposed to client
security_baseline.rationale: Consent compliance for EU visitors; static content has minimal attack surface

frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js i18n routing handles locale detection; Vercel edge serves locale variants from nearest PoP

backend_stack.value: Vercel Edge Functions for lead form submission proxy to Klaviyo API
backend_stack.rationale: Lightweight edge function avoids exposing Klaviyo API key to client browser

data_platform.value: None; Klaviyo stores lead email addresses, no app-owned database
data_platform.rationale: Landing page passes leads to marketing platform; no persistence in app infrastructure

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with 100+ PoPs for global low-latency serving
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel edge caching serves locale variants near visitors globally

privacy_retention_policy.value: Email addresses stored in Klaviyo under marketing consent; GDPR right-to-erasure handled via Klaviyo unsubscribe; no analytics PII
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Klaviyo handles GDPR compliance for email list; analytics are anonymized

future_scope.value: Additional locales, in-app referral tracking via deep links, multivariate testing beyond A/B
future_scope.rationale: Expansion after initial 8-locale launch performance review

constraints.value: LCP under 2.5s on 3G connections in target markets; all locale builds deployed together; no separate backend infrastructure
constraints.rationale: Performance target for emerging market mobile users; operational simplicity requirement

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 plus Mixpanel for campaign attribution; Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals per locale
observability_baseline.rationale: Dual analytics for campaign measurement and product analytics alignment

execution_preference.value: Continuous deploy on merge to main; locale content updates via PR without engineering involvement
execution_preference.rationale: Marketing team needs to update locale copy independently

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU visitors; consent management platform required for cookie-based analytics in FR and applicable locales; Klaviyo GDPR data processing agreement in place
compliance_context.rationale: Cookie-based analytics require consent in EU jurisdictions; Klaviyo DPA required for email data transfer

project.goal: Drive app installs across 8 emerging-market locales via high-performance landing pages
project.target_users: Prospective wellness app users in Latin America, South Asia, Southeast Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa

frontend.framework: Next.js 14 App Router with next-intl for i18n
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with locale-specific font stacks (Noto Sans for Hindi/Swahili, Cairo for Arabic)
frontend.performance: Image optimization via next/image, lazy-loaded testimonials, minimal JS bundle

security.consent: Osano CMP for GDPR locales, injected only for EU visitor traffic

data.leads: Vercel Edge Function proxies email to Klaviyo Profiles API
data.analytics: GA4 gtag.js + Mixpanel browser SDK behind CMP consent gate for EU

devops.ci: GitHub Actions lint and build check; Vercel handles production deploys
devops.environments: production on main; preview per PR with locale routing

constraints.locale_independence: Marketing edits locale copy via JSON translation files in PR

reasoner_reviews.product: The 8-locale requirement with RTL support for Arabic and distinct font stacks for Devanagari-script Hindi is the highest-risk frontend complexity. A/B variant routing must be deterministic per visitor session to avoid content flicker. Lead capture conversion is the single metric that defines campaign success.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js i18n with next-intl is the right choice. Locale detection order should be: explicit URL prefix (/hi, /ar) first, then Accept-Language header, then geographic fallback. The Vercel Edge Function for Klaviyo proxying keeps the API key server-side; the edge function should validate email format and add source locale as a Klaviyo profile property for segmentation.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR compliance for FR locale requires CMP to fire before GA4 or Mixpanel initialize. Consent state must persist in a first-party cookie across sessions. Klaviyo API key must never appear in client-side bundle; Edge Function is the correct pattern. CSP should allowlist Klaviyo and analytics domains explicitly.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3G LCP target of 2.5s requires aggressive image optimization. Hero images should be served as WebP via next/image with locale-specific alt text. Arabic RTL layout requires dir=rtl on the html element set server-side based on locale to avoid layout shift. Osano CMP bundle must be loaded conditionally for EU locales only to avoid penalizing non-EU Core Web Vitals.

security.auth_design: No authentication; public landing page with no session management beyond CMP consent cookie
security.secrets_management: Klaviyo private API key stored as Vercel environment variable; never exposed to client bundle; Edge Function reads KLAVIYO_API_KEY from process.env
security.privacy_controls: Osano CMP loaded conditionally for EU locales (FR, detected via Accept-Language or geolocation); analytics SDKs initialize only after consent signal; GDPR unsubscribe handled via Klaviyo list management

data.entities: locale_variants (locale_code, hero_headline, hero_subtext, cta_primary, app_store_url, play_store_url, testimonials[]), campaign_landing_pages (utm_source, utm_campaign, variant_id, locale), lead_submissions (email, locale, utm_source, utm_campaign, submitted_at passed to Klaviyo), ab_test_variants (variant_id, headline_copy, cta_text, allocation_percentage)
data.storage_design: Translation JSON files in /messages/{locale}.json; locale variant config in /config/locales.ts; no app-owned database; leads stored in Klaviyo lists segmented by locale and campaign
data.analytics_events: page_view with locale and utm params, cta_click with variant_id and locale, lead_submitted with locale and source, app_store_link_click with platform and locale

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network serves all locale routes from nearest PoP; Edge Functions deployed globally for Klaviyo proxy
devops.environments: production (main branch, vercel.com/app/wellness-landing), preview per PR with all locale routes active
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: TypeScript type check, ESLint, next build dry-run; Vercel GitHub integration handles actual deploy and preview URL generation
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics for per-locale Core Web Vitals; GA4 for campaign attribution; Mixpanel for funnel conversion events

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 revision addresses ISS-003: Klaviyo lead deduplication. The fix is to use Klaviyo Identify API upsert semantics in the Edge Function — POST to /v2/identify with email as the identifier, which creates a new profile if absent or merges properties onto an existing profile. The locale property should use an array append strategy to capture all locales a user engaged with across campaign bursts.

fix_report.action_taken: Edge Function updated to call Klaviyo Identify API endpoint with upsert semantics: if profile with submitted email exists, the locale and UTM source properties are appended rather than overwriting. Added idempotency check using Klaviyo profile lookup before identify call to determine merge vs create path.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No duplicate Klaviyo profiles created for repeat email submissions across campaign bursts; locale engagement history preserved per profile.

target_output.title: Multilingual Mental Wellness Landing Page Architecture v4
target_output.executive_summary: Next.js 14 landing page serving 8 locale variants (EN, ES, PT-BR, HI, ID, SW, FR, AR) from Vercel Edge Network for a mental wellness app targeting emerging markets. Lead capture flows through a Vercel Edge Function proxying to Klaviyo with upsert deduplication. Osano CMP gates GA4 and Mixpanel initialization for EU locales. Designed to sustain traffic spikes during paid social campaign bursts without origin infrastructure.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router with next-intl handles all 8 locale routes under /[locale]/* pattern. Vercel Edge Network serves locale-specific pages from the nearest of 100+ PoPs globally, keeping LCP under 2.5s on 3G for target emerging markets. No persistent database exists in app infrastructure: locale content lives in JSON translation files, leads are stored in Klaviyo, and analytics data flows to GA4 and Mixpanel. A single Vercel Edge Function handles lead form submissions, proxying to Klaviyo Identify API with upsert semantics to prevent duplicate profile creation across campaign bursts. Osano CMP fires before analytics SDKs initialize for FR and other EU-jurisdiction locales.
target_output.technology_stack: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, next-intl for locale routing and translation. Tailwind CSS with locale-specific font configurations: Noto Sans Devanagari for HI, Noto Sans Arabic for AR with RTL layout. Vercel Edge Functions (Node.js Edge Runtime) for Klaviyo proxy. Osano CMP script injected conditionally for EU locales. Google Analytics 4 with gtag.js, Mixpanel browser SDK. GitHub Actions for CI type check and lint; Vercel GitHub integration for deploy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Locale Variants: 8 distinct page builds with locale-appropriate hero headline, subtext, CTA copy, app store links (localized Play Store and App Store URLs), and testimonial content. Lead Capture: email input form POSTing to /api/subscribe Edge Function. A/B Test Routing: two headline variants per locale routed deterministically by visitor session cookie. GDPR Consent: Osano CMP rendered only for FR locale and any Accept-Language matching EU jurisdictions. App Store Link Tracking: download CTA clicks tracked with locale and UTM attribution.
target_output.system_components: Next.js App Router with /[locale]/ route group handling 8 locales. /messages/{locale}.json translation files for all copy strings. /config/locales.ts defining locale codes, RTL flag, and app store URLs. Vercel Edge Function at /api/subscribe calling Klaviyo Identify API. Osano CMP script loaded via next/script with strategy=beforeInteractive for EU locales only. GA4 gtag.js and Mixpanel browser SDK wrapped in CMP consent callback. Vercel Analytics snippet for Core Web Vitals per locale.
target_output.workflows: Locale Detection Workflow: incoming request matched against /[locale]/ URL prefix first; if absent, next-intl middleware inspects Accept-Language header and Vercel geo headers to select best locale, redirecting to prefixed URL. Arabic locale receives server-rendered html element with dir=rtl and lang=ar set in layout metadata to prevent client-side layout shift. Lead Submission Workflow: visitor submits email form, client POSTs to /api/subscribe with email, locale, utm_source, utm_campaign; Edge Function calls Klaviyo v3 Profiles API GET to check existing profile by email; if found, PATCH to append locale to engaged_locales property and update last_campaign_source; if not found, POST to create new profile with locale and UTM properties; both paths return 200 to client.
target_output.data_model: locale_variants: per-locale config in /config/locales.ts (locale_code, dir, font_family, app_store_url, play_store_url, hero_image_src). Translation content in /messages/{locale}.json with keys: hero_headline, hero_subtext, cta_primary, testimonials[], feature_highlights[]. campaign_landing_pages: UTM parameter sets tracked as query params, not persisted. lead_submissions: email, locale, utm_source, utm_campaign forwarded to Klaviyo Identify API; not stored in app infrastructure. ab_test_variants: variant_id and allocation defined in /config/variants.ts; assigned via session cookie hash.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/subscribe — Edge Function accepts JSON body {email: string, locale: string, utm_source?: string, utm_campaign?: string}; validates email format with regex; calls Klaviyo Profiles API GET /api/profiles/?filter=email,{email} to check existence; on 404 calls POST /api/profiles to create; on 200 calls PATCH /api/profiles/{id} to merge locale data; returns {success: true} to client. No other server endpoints. Analytics via client-side SDK calls.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel with automatic TLS. Klaviyo private API key stored as KLAVIYO_API_KEY Vercel environment variable; never bundled in client JavaScript. CSP headers via next.config.js: allowlist self, GA4 measurement domain, Mixpanel CDN, Osano CMP domain, Klaviyo form domain. Osano CMP prevents GA4 and Mixpanel SDKs from initializing until user grants consent in FR and EU-jurisdiction locales. Klaviyo DPA in place for GDPR email data transfer compliance. No session cookies set beyond CMP consent state.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: TypeScript type check via tsc --noEmit, ESLint across all locale configs, next build for compile-time error detection; on pass, Vercel GitHub integration deploys automatically. Main branch deploys to production with all 8 locale routes. Every PR generates a unique Vercel preview URL with all locales accessible at /[locale]/ paths for marketing team review. No database migrations or infrastructure provisioning required. Locale copy updates by marketing team via PR modifying /messages/{locale}.json files only.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics captures Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) segmented by locale route; alert if any locale LCP exceeds 2.5s threshold. GA4 custom events: cta_click (variant_id, locale), lead_submitted (locale, utm_source), app_store_link_click (platform, locale). Mixpanel funnel: page_view to cta_click to lead_submitted per locale. No server-side monitoring required beyond Vercel Edge Function error rate in Vercel dashboard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan covers edge function invocations and bandwidth for campaign burst traffic. Klaviyo Identify API calls scale with lead volume at no marginal infrastructure cost. During paid social bursts, Vercel edge caching serves all static locale pages without invoking Next.js server rendering, keeping edge function invocations limited to actual form submissions only.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-2): Build EN and ES locale variants, Klaviyo Edge Function with upsert logic, GA4 and Mixpanel integration. Phase 2 (Week 3): Add remaining 6 locales including Arabic RTL and Devanagari font stacks, Osano CMP for FR. Phase 3 (Week 4): A/B test variant routing, Vercel Analytics Core Web Vitals review per locale, performance tuning for 3G LCP targets, campaign launch.
target_output.development_guidelines: All locale content changes go through PR review by marketing lead before merge. Translation keys must be present in all 8 locale JSON files; CI checks for missing keys via a type-safe next-intl config. RTL locales (AR) must have PR preview reviewed visually before merge. Klaviyo Edge Function has 99% unit test coverage for deduplication logic.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: next-intl locale detection order conflict between URL prefix and Accept-Language causing incorrect locale serving. Mitigation: explicit URL prefix routing takes precedence; Accept-Language only used when locale prefix is absent. Risk: Osano CMP loading adds ~80ms to FR locale FCP. Tradeoff: accepted because GDPR non-compliance risk outweighs performance cost. Risk: Klaviyo Identify API rate limits during peak campaign bursts. Mitigation: Edge Function implements exponential backoff with 3 retry attempts.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Klaviyo chosen over Mailchimp for superior segmentation by locale and UTM properties. Osano CMP selected over Cookiebot for Vercel Edge compatibility without server-side session requirements. A/B test routing via session cookie chosen over Vercel Edge Config to avoid external service dependency on critical path.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision of multilingual mental wellness landing page; resolves Klaviyo deduplication issue; moderate quality plan with solid sections.

profile.domain: Behavioral Health Managed Care Claims and Utilization Data Pipeline

project_goal.value: Replace legacy nightly SQL Server batch job at a behavioral health managed care organization with a cloud-native ELT architecture ingesting claims and utilization management data into Snowflake for real-time analytics and CMS quality measure reporting
project_goal.rationale: Legacy batch job takes 9 hours nightly, has no data quality monitoring, and cannot support the new CMS HEDIS reporting mandate

target_users.value: Data engineering team, actuarial analysts, clinical quality team, compliance officers running CMS quality measure reports
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are internal analytics and compliance stakeholders

project_class.rationale: ELT pipeline with batch orchestration and data observability layer; no user-facing application

capabilities.rationale: Snowflake ELT, Airflow DAG orchestration, and SQL Server CDC integration

complexity_level.rationale: CDC extraction from legacy SQL Server, incremental dbt models, data quality contracts, HIPAA compliance

risk_level.rationale: Member PHI in claims and eligibility data; state Medicaid DUA obligations; CMS reporting accuracy risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Claims, eligibility, and utilization records contain member PHI and behavioral health diagnosis codes

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs within AWS VPC; Snowflake access restricted to internal analyst roles via SSO

access_model.value: Snowflake RBAC with analyst, actuary, quality, and compliance roles; data masking for PHI fields in analyst role
access_model.rationale: Minimum necessary access principle for HIPAA compliance; PHI masked for non-clinical analysts

feature_scope.value: CDC extraction from legacy SQL Server, S3 raw landing zone, dbt incremental models for claims, eligibility, utilization, and quality measures, Monte Carlo data observability, Airflow DAG orchestration, Snowflake dynamic data masking for PHI
feature_scope.rationale: Complete pipeline replacing legacy batch with observability and compliance controls

mvp_scope.value: Claims and member eligibility pipeline live with dbt tests, Monte Carlo monitoring on row counts and freshness, Airflow DAGs replacing nightly SQL Server job
mvp_scope.rationale: Core data flows needed for CMS reporting deadline; utilization and quality measures in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: All PHI encrypted at rest in S3 with SSE-KMS and in Snowflake with Tri-Secret Secure; TLS in transit; Vault for pipeline credentials; no PHI in logs
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA encryption mandate and state Medicaid DUA encryption requirements

frontend_stack.value: None; data pipeline with no user-facing frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: data_pipeline project class requires no frontend application

backend_stack.value: dbt Core 1.8 with Python 3.12 macros, Apache Airflow 2.9 on MWAA for orchestration
backend_stack.rationale: dbt for SQL transformations with incremental models; MWAA for managed Airflow avoiding self-hosted complexity

data_platform.value: Snowflake (Enterprise edition for data masking), S3 as raw landing zone with partitioned prefixes, legacy SQL Server 2019 as source
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake chosen for separation of compute and storage, RBAC, and dynamic data masking capability

hosting_target.value: AWS us-east-1: MWAA, S3, Glue CDC connector, Secrets Manager; Snowflake hosted in same region for data transfer cost minimization
hosting_target.rationale: Data residency in us-east-1 per state Medicaid DUA; Snowflake region alignment reduces S3 egress fees

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw claims retained in S3 for 7 years per HIPAA minimum; Snowflake transformed tables retained for 5 years with automated archival; audit trail retained indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA 6-year minimum plus state Medicaid DUA 7-year requirement; audit trail for CMS attestation

future_scope.value: Real-time utilization management alerting, HEDIS measure automation, provider performance dashboards in Tableau Cloud
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 analytics products built on top of this pipeline foundation

constraints.value: Migration must run parallel to legacy job for 90-day validation period; no impact to SQL Server OLTP performance; pipeline must complete within 4-hour window for morning analyst availability
constraints.rationale: Parallel run ensures data parity validation before decommissioning legacy batch; OLTP protection for clinical system availability

observability_baseline.value: Monte Carlo for data observability on Snowflake tables; Datadog for MWAA DAG execution metrics and alerting; structured JSON logs in CloudWatch with no PHI
observability_baseline.rationale: Monte Carlo provides automated anomaly detection on table freshness, row counts, and schema drift; Datadog for infrastructure alerting

execution_preference.value: dbt-first transformation layer with CI testing gates; infrastructure as code via Terraform for AWS resources
execution_preference.rationale: dbt enables version-controlled, testable SQL transformations; Terraform ensures repeatable infrastructure

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules; HITECH breach notification requirements; state Medicaid data use agreement with minimum necessary access requirements; CMS HEDIS 2026 technical specifications for quality measure reporting
compliance_context.rationale: Federal and state obligations for behavioral health managed care organization handling Medicaid member data

project.goal: Cloud-native ELT replacing legacy nightly SQL Server batch for claims and utilization analytics
project.target_users: Data engineering, actuarial, clinical quality, compliance teams
project.timeline: Q2 delivery with 90-day parallel run validation

backend.framework: dbt Core 1.8 with Snowflake adapter; Airflow 2.9 DAGs
backend.ingestion: AWS Glue with JDBC connector to SQL Server; Debezium CDC via Kafka MSK for high-frequency tables

security.auth: Snowflake SSO via Okta with RBAC; Vault agent for pipeline credentials
security.encryption: S3 SSE-KMS, Snowflake Tri-Secret Secure, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.db: Snowflake Enterprise with RAW, STAGING, MART schema layers
data.source: SQL Server 2019 claims and eligibility tables
data.observability: Monte Carlo data contracts on all mart-layer tables

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with dbt test on PR, sqlfluff linting
devops.environments: dev Snowflake account, staging account, production account

constraints.parallel_run: 90 days alongside legacy SQL Server batch
constraints.completion_window: pipeline complete by 06:00 ET for analyst availability

reasoner_reviews.product: The core business driver is the CMS HEDIS 2026 reporting mandate requiring fresh, validated claims and quality measure data by Q2. The 9-hour legacy batch window is incompatible with the 4-hour pipeline completion requirement. Parallel run validation is non-negotiable given the compliance stakes of incorrect Medicaid quality measure submissions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Three-layer Snowflake architecture (RAW ingested from S3, STAGING with dbt incremental models, MART for analyst consumption) is the right pattern. Debezium CDC via MSK for high-frequency eligibility changes; AWS Glue JDBC connector for daily claims file loads. dbt incremental models using updated_at watermarks on claims and encounters tables enable sub-4-hour pipeline runs replacing the 9-hour full table scans in SQL Server.
reasoner_reviews.security: PHI masking is critical: Snowflake dynamic data masking on claims.member_id, diagnosis_codes, and authorization_notes fields must apply to analyst role but not to clinical_quality and compliance roles. No PHI may appear in Airflow logs, Monte Carlo metadata, or Datadog traces. Vault agent sidecar on MWAA workers provides just-in-time credential injection without static secrets in DAG code.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The SQL Server CDC connector must use snapshot isolation to avoid locking OLTP tables during extraction. MWAA XCom passing should use S3-backed XCom for large payloads exceeding 48KB to prevent metadata database bloat. dbt incremental model selection should use --select state:modified+ to limit CI test runtime to changed models only.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG hierarchy: extract_dag triggers AWS Glue JDBC jobs for claims and eligibility; on success triggers transform_dag running dbt build with incremental selector; on success triggers quality_dag running Monte Carlo freshness checks and custom dbt test assertions
backend.business_modules: claims_ingestion: Glue job extracts claims_records from SQL Server claims_header and claims_line tables to S3 partitioned by extraction_date. eligibility_ingestion: Debezium CDC connector streaming member_eligibility changes to MSK topic, consumed by Flink job writing micro-batches to S3. utilization_ingestion: Glue job for authorization_requests and utilization_events.
backend.background_jobs: Monthly quality_measures DAG computes HEDIS-aligned metrics (antidepressant medication management, follow-up after hospitalization) by joining claims, eligibility, and authorization tables in Snowflake MART layer
backend.failure_handling: DAG task retry with exponential backoff (3 retries, 5-minute delay). On extraction failure, Airflow sends PagerDuty alert with task context. dbt test failures block downstream DAG tasks via Airflow sensor. Monte Carlo anomaly alerts routed to data engineering Slack channel.

security.auth_design: Snowflake authentication via Okta SAML SSO for human users; service accounts use key-pair authentication with 90-day key rotation via Vault
security.authorization_model: Snowflake RBAC: RAW_INGEST role (Glue service account, MWAA service account), STAGING_TRANSFORM role (dbt service account), ANALYST role (dynamic masking on PHI fields), CLINICAL_QUALITY role (unmasked PHI for HEDIS), COMPLIANCE role (full access plus audit trail)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Snowflake ACCESS_HISTORY view queried nightly to audit all SELECT on PHI-containing tables; results persisted to audit_snowflake_access table; CloudWatch structured logs capture Glue and MWAA job execution without PHI in log payloads
security.secrets_management: Vault agent sidecar on MWAA worker pods injects Snowflake key-pair, Klaviyo token, and SQL Server JDBC credentials as environment variables at task startup; no secrets stored in Airflow variables or Connections UI

data.entities: claims_records (claim_id, member_id, service_date, procedure_codes CPT[], diagnosis_codes ICD10[], billing_provider_npi, plan_paid_amount, member_liability, claim_status, adjudication_date), member_eligibility (member_id, plan_id, effective_date, termination_date, coverage_type, mco_enrollment_segment), utilization_events (auth_id, member_id, service_type ENUM inpatient/outpatient/crisis/residential, requested_units, approved_units, denial_reason_code, reviewing_clinician_id), authorization_requests (auth_id, member_id, requesting_provider_npi, icd10_primary, service_category, urgency_level, status ENUM approved/denied/pending/appealed), quality_measures (measure_id, measure_name HEDIS, member_id, measurement_year, numerator_flag, denominator_flag, exclusion_flag, computed_at), provider_contracts (npi, contract_type, specialty, network_tier, rate_schedule_id)
data.storage_design: S3 RAW bucket with prefixes: s3://claims-raw/{source}/{yyyy}/{mm}/{dd}/. Snowflake RAW schema: direct copy of source tables. STAGING schema: dbt incremental models with column-level lineage. MART schema: analyst-facing fact and dimension tables with masking policies applied. S3 Glacier lifecycle transition after 2 years for raw files.
data.consistency_notes: dbt ref() dependencies enforce transformation order. Snowflake tasks and streams provide change data capture at the warehouse layer for incremental MART refreshes. 90-day parallel run compares MART row counts and aggregate metrics against legacy SQL Server batch outputs nightly.
data.retention_and_deletion: S3 raw files: 7-year retention, Glacier after year 2. Snowflake MART tables: 5-year retention with time travel for point-in-time audits. Audit access logs: indefinite retention per CMS attestation requirements.

devops.deployment_topology: MWAA environment in private subnet of VPC; S3 VPC endpoint for all S3 access without internet egress; Snowflake Private Link for Snowflake connectivity from VPC; SQL Server accessible via Direct Connect from on-premises data center
devops.environments: dev: Snowflake DEV account cloned from production snapshot; MWAA dev environment. staging: Snowflake STAGING account with production-scale data; parallel run validation environment. production: Snowflake PROD account with full RBAC and masking.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: sqlfluff lint on changed .sql files, dbt compile to validate syntax, dbt test --select state:modified+ on staging Snowflake account, dbt docs generate to update lineage graph; deploy via Airflow DAG code push to MWAA S3 dag bucket on merge to main
devops.backup_and_recovery: Snowflake 90-day time travel on all mart-layer tables for point-in-time recovery. S3 versioning on raw bucket. MWAA metadata database backed up daily by AWS. Disaster recovery: Snowflake account replication to us-west-2 for RTO < 4 hours.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a HIPAA-compliant ELT pipeline replacing a 9-hour legacy SQL Server batch at a behavioral health MCO. Key decisions: dbt incremental models with watermark-based extraction replace full table scans; Snowflake dynamic data masking provides RBAC-controlled PHI access without application-layer masking logic; Monte Carlo data contracts provide automated quality gates before analyst consumption; 90-day parallel run is architecturally integrated as a validation DAG comparing MART aggregates against legacy outputs.
target_output.title: Behavioral Health MCO Claims and Utilization ELT Pipeline Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Cloud-native ELT pipeline replacing a 9-hour legacy SQL Server nightly batch at a behavioral health managed care organization. Claims, member eligibility, utilization events, and authorization requests are extracted from SQL Server via AWS Glue JDBC and Debezium CDC, loaded to S3 RAW zone, transformed through dbt incremental models in Snowflake, and surfaced to actuarial, clinical quality, and compliance analysts under HIPAA-compliant RBAC with dynamic PHI masking. Airflow DAGs on MWAA orchestrate extraction through to Monte Carlo quality assertions, completing the full pipeline within 4 hours.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer Snowflake architecture: RAW schema holds direct copies of source tables loaded from S3 via Snowflake COPY INTO. STAGING schema contains dbt incremental models applying business logic, deduplication, and ICD10/CPT code normalization. MART schema holds analyst-facing fact tables (fact_claims, fact_authorizations, dim_members, dim_providers) with dynamic masking policies applied per Snowflake role. AWS Glue JDBC connector extracts claims_header, claims_line, and provider_contracts from SQL Server daily; Debezium on MSK captures member_eligibility CDC events for near-real-time enrollment tracking. Airflow on MWAA orchestrates DAG dependencies: extraction triggers transformation triggers data quality assertions. Monte Carlo monitors all MART tables for row count anomalies, freshness SLAs, and schema drift. A 90-day parallel validation DAG runs nightly comparisons between MART aggregates and legacy SQL Server batch outputs.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on MWAA. Transformation: dbt Core 1.8 with dbt-snowflake adapter, sqlfluff linting, dbt-expectations for advanced test assertions. Warehouse: Snowflake Enterprise edition with dynamic data masking, time travel, and private link. Ingestion: AWS Glue 4.0 with JDBC connector for batch claims extraction; Debezium 2.5 on Amazon MSK for eligibility CDC. Storage: S3 with SSE-KMS encryption, partitioned raw zone, Glacier lifecycle. Secrets: HashiCorp Vault 1.16 with AWS auth method. Observability: Monte Carlo, Datadog for MWAA metrics, CloudWatch for structured logs. IaC: Terraform for all AWS resources.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Claims Ingestion: Daily Glue job extracts claims_header and claims_line from SQL Server into S3 RAW with partition by adjudication_date. Eligibility CDC: Debezium streams member_eligibility changes to MSK; Flink consumer writes 15-minute micro-batches to S3 RAW/eligibility/. Utilization Ingestion: Daily Glue job for authorization_requests and utilization_events. dbt Transformation Layer: incremental models stg_claims, stg_eligibility, stg_authorizations with watermark-based incremental strategy. MART Layer: fact_claims, fact_authorizations, dim_members (with masking), dim_providers. Quality Measures: Monthly HEDIS DAG computing antidepressant medication management (AMM) and follow-up after hospitalization for mental illness (FUH) measures. Parallel Validation DAG: Nightly comparison of MART row counts and financial aggregates against legacy SQL Server batch for 90-day validation period.
target_output.system_components: MWAA environment (mw1.medium workers, 3-10 auto-scaling) in private VPC subnet. AWS Glue 4.0 jobs for JDBC extraction. Amazon MSK 3-broker cluster for Debezium CDC events. Apache Flink application on ECS Fargate for MSK-to-S3 consumer. S3 raw bucket with SSE-KMS and VPC endpoint. Snowflake Enterprise with Private Link, three schema layers, dynamic masking. Vault cluster for credential management. Monte Carlo SaaS connected via Snowflake reader account. GitHub Actions for CI. Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB locking.
target_output.workflows: Claims Extraction Workflow: MWAA extract_claims DAG fires at 22:00 ET; Glue job connects to SQL Server via JDBC under snapshot isolation, extracts all claims with adjudication_date >= last_watermark from claims_header JOIN claims_line, writes Parquet to s3://claims-raw/claims/{date}/, updates watermark in Airflow Variable; on success signals transform_dag via Airflow TriggerDagRunOperator. dbt Incremental Transform Workflow: transform_dag runs dbt build --select tag:daily --exclude tag:monthly; stg_claims model reads new S3 partitions via Snowflake external stage, deduplicates on claim_id, normalizes ICD10 codes against reference table, loads into STAGING.stg_claims; mart_dag runs fact_claims and fact_authorizations dbt models joining staging tables; Monte Carlo freshness check asserts fact_claims.max(adjudication_date) within 26 hours; pipeline completion logged to MWAA by 05:30 ET. HEDIS Quality Measures Workflow: Monthly quality_measures DAG on first of month joins fact_claims, dim_members, and stg_authorizations to compute AMM and FUH denominator and numerator flags per member per measurement year; results loaded to MART.quality_measures; compliance role queries for CMS reporting export.
target_output.data_model: claims_records: claim_id VARCHAR PK, member_id VARCHAR FK (masked for ANALYST role), service_date DATE, procedure_codes ARRAY (CPT), diagnosis_codes ARRAY (ICD10), billing_provider_npi VARCHAR FK, plan_paid_amount DECIMAL(12,2), member_liability DECIMAL(12,2), claim_status ENUM(paid/denied/pending/void), adjudication_date DATE, source_system VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMP. member_eligibility: member_id VARCHAR PK, plan_id VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, termination_date DATE, coverage_type ENUM(medicaid/chip/managed_care), mco_enrollment_segment VARCHAR, cdc_operation ENUM(insert/update/delete), cdc_timestamp TIMESTAMP. utilization_events: auth_id VARCHAR PK, member_id VARCHAR FK (masked), service_type ENUM(inpatient/outpatient/crisis/residential), requested_units INT, approved_units INT, denial_reason_code VARCHAR, reviewing_clinician_id VARCHAR. authorization_requests: auth_id VARCHAR PK, requesting_provider_npi VARCHAR, icd10_primary VARCHAR, service_category VARCHAR, urgency_level ENUM(standard/urgent/emergent), status ENUM(approved/denied/pending/appealed), created_at TIMESTAMP. quality_measures: measure_id VARCHAR, member_id VARCHAR FK, measurement_year INT, numerator_flag BOOLEAN, denominator_flag BOOLEAN, exclusion_flag BOOLEAN, computed_at TIMESTAMP. provider_contracts: npi VARCHAR PK, contract_type VARCHAR, specialty VARCHAR, network_tier ENUM(preferred/standard/out_of_network), rate_schedule_id VARCHAR.
target_output.api_design: No external API surface. Internal interfaces: Airflow TriggerDagRunOperator between extract/transform/quality DAGs. dbt CLI invoked by MWAA BashOperator: dbt build --select tag:daily --profiles-dir /secrets/dbt/. Monte Carlo API polled by Airflow sensor to await quality gate clearance before analyst notification. Snowflake external stage defined as: CREATE STAGE raw_claims_stage URL='s3://claims-raw/claims/' CREDENTIALS=(AWS_ROLE='arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/snowflake-s3-role') FILE_FORMAT=(TYPE=PARQUET).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: Snowflake Tri-Secret Secure encryption with customer-managed KMS key for all PHI tables. Dynamic data masking policies on member_id, diagnosis_codes, and authorization_notes: ANALYST role sees SHA2 hash of member_id and NULL for diagnosis_codes; CLINICAL_QUALITY and COMPLIANCE roles see plaintext. No PHI in Airflow logs: DAG tasks log claim counts and watermarks only, never member IDs or diagnosis codes. Vault agent sidecar on MWAA worker containers injects Snowflake key-pair at task startup with 1-hour TTL. Snowflake ACCESS_HISTORY view queried nightly; any SELECT on PHI-containing tables by non-compliance roles triggers audit alert. State Medicaid DUA compliance: data residency enforced via Snowflake Private Link in us-east-1; no cross-region replication of PHI without DUA amendment.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform provisions: MWAA environment, MSK cluster, Glue connections, S3 buckets with lifecycle policies, VPC endpoints for S3 and Snowflake Private Link, IAM roles with least-privilege policies. GitHub Actions CI: sqlfluff lint, dbt compile on Snowflake STAGING account, dbt test --select state:modified+, Terraform plan; on merge to main, Airflow DAG files deployed to MWAA S3 dag bucket via GitHub Actions S3 sync. Environment promotion: dev Snowflake account cloned monthly from STAGING snapshot via Snowflake CREATE DATABASE CLONE. 90-day parallel run: validation_dag runs at 06:00 ET comparing MART fact_claims row counts, total plan_paid_amount, and claim denial rate against legacy SQL Server batch outputs; discrepancies >0.01% trigger Slack alert to data engineering lead.
target_output.observability: Monte Carlo data contracts on MART tables: fact_claims freshness SLA 26 hours, row count expected within 5% of 7-day rolling average, schema drift alert on any column removal. Datadog monitors: MWAA DAG run duration (alert if extract_dag exceeds 90 minutes), Glue job memory utilization, MSK consumer lag (alert if eligibility lag exceeds 30 minutes). CloudWatch structured logs for all Glue and MWAA tasks with fields: dag_id, task_id, execution_date, rows_processed, watermark_value; PHI fields explicitly excluded from log schema. Monthly Monte Carlo anomaly report reviewed by data engineering team at sprint retrospective.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Snowflake compute: TRANSFORM virtual warehouse (2XL, auto-suspend 10 min) for dbt runs; ANALYST warehouse (L) for analyst queries. Estimated $3,200/month Snowflake compute at current claims volume (1.2M claims/month). MWAA mw1.medium at $0.49/hour, $360/month. MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.large at $750/month. Monte Carlo SaaS license at $2,000/month. Total ~$6,500/month vs $12,000/month fully-loaded cost of legacy SQL Server infrastructure and DBA time.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-8): AWS infrastructure via Terraform, Glue JDBC connector for claims and eligibility, S3 RAW zone, dbt RAW-to-STAGING models for claims and eligibility, Snowflake RBAC and dynamic masking, MWAA DAGs for extraction and transformation, 90-day parallel validation DAG launched. Phase 2 (Weeks 9-14): Debezium CDC for real-time eligibility updates, utilization_events and authorization_requests pipeline, dbt MART layer with fact_claims and fact_authorizations, Monte Carlo data contracts on all MART tables. Phase 3 (Weeks 15-20): HEDIS quality measures DAG (AMM and FUH), Tableau Cloud connection to Snowflake MART for actuary and clinical quality dashboards, decommission legacy SQL Server batch after 90-day parallel validation passes.
target_output.development_guidelines: All dbt models must have schema.yml with at least not_null and unique tests on primary keys, and accepted_values tests on ENUM columns. No hardcoded member_id or claim_id in any DAG code. sqlfluff enforces consistent SQL style (uppercase keywords, explicit column aliases). dbt model naming convention: stg_ for staging, fct_ for facts, dim_ for dimensions. Airflow DAGs use TaskFlow API decorators for Python tasks; no XCom for payloads exceeding 1KB (use S3 intermediate files). All new Snowflake tables require a masking policy assignment for any column containing ICD10, member_id, or NPI fields.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Debezium CDC connector falls behind during SQL Server peak OLTP hours causing eligibility lag exceeding 30-minute SLA. Mitigation: Debezium max.batch.size and poll.interval.ms tuned to limit SQL Server CDC table read impact; MSK consumer lag monitored with Datadog alert. Risk: dbt incremental model watermark drift if SQL Server timestamp columns have precision differences. Mitigation: watermark stored as UTC with explicit CONVERT(DATETIME2, col) in Glue extraction queries. Tradeoff: Snowflake Tri-Secret Secure adds ~10% query latency vs standard encryption; accepted because state Medicaid DUA requires customer-managed encryption keys. Risk: HEDIS measure logic defect causing incorrect CMS submission. Mitigation: clinical quality team validates AMM and FUH outputs against prior-year vendor-produced rates before first CMS submission.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Snowflake chosen over Redshift for dynamic data masking capability without application-layer workarounds. MWAA chosen over self-hosted Airflow to eliminate Kubernetes management overhead. Debezium CDC chosen for eligibility over full daily Glue extract because enrollment changes occur throughout the day and downstream utilization authorization logic requires current eligibility status.

metadata.notes: First-pass strong plan for HIPAA-compliant behavioral health MCO claims ELT pipeline replacing legacy SQL Server batch; rich domain-specific content throughout.

project.goal: Replace legacy HTML template with modern responsive Astro landing page with app install tracking

reasoner_reviews.product: The core problem is that the legacy static HTML page has no UTM passthrough to app store links, so campaign spend cannot be attributed to installs. Fixing this is the primary business value. Responsive design is table stakes for mobile acquisition.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with zero-JS output is the right choice for LCP performance. The app screenshot section must use Astro Image component with proper width/height attributes to eliminate layout shift. UTM passthrough to App Store Connect and Google Play requires reading query parameters client-side and appending them to the store URLs, which requires a minimal Astro client-side island.
reasoner_reviews.security: No authentication or form surfaces mean the attack surface is minimal. CSP should be set permissively enough for GA4 but restrictive against unknown third-party scripts. No consent banner needed for non-EU audience per current scope.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Astro 4 builds are typically under 30 seconds for a small landing page. LCP under 2s requires hero image preloading with fetchpriority=high on the hero screenshot. The UTM passthrough island must be minimal: read URLSearchParams, update href attributes on CTA links only.
