previous_plan.title: Mental Health Therapeutics Engagement Pipeline v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Kafka MSK + Spark Structured Streaming + ClickHouse pipeline for engagement event processing; daily batch feature job to PostgreSQL for recommendation service.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: App clients write events to MSK Kafka; Spark EMR Serverless consumes and aggregates to ClickHouse; daily batch computes user_features to PostgreSQL.
previous_plan.data_model: engagement_aggregates ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree, mood_log_facts ClickHouse, user_features PostgreSQL. Deletion via SQL DELETE — ISS-001 open.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: Schema evolution risk unaddressed — ISS-002 open. CCPA suppression post-aggregation — ISS-003 open.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves the three open issues: ISS-001 fixed by replacing SQL DELETE with ClickHouse ALTER TABLE mutation in DeletionWorker, adding polling of system.mutations until is_done=1, and issuing OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL before writing to S3 deletion audit log. ISS-002 fixed by adding AWS Glue Schema Registry with Avro schemas for both Kafka topics and configuring BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE evolution policy in Spark GlueSchemaRegistryDeserializer. ISS-003 fixed by moving CCPA suppressed_users broadcast join into the Spark foreachBatch closure before any ClickHouse writes.

fix_report.action_taken: DeletionWorker updated to issue ALTER TABLE engagement_aggregates DELETE WHERE user_id_hash = ? and ALTER TABLE mood_log_facts DELETE WHERE user_id_hash = ? mutations via ClickHouse HTTP API. Worker polls SELECT count() FROM system.mutations WHERE table = 'engagement_aggregates' AND user_id_hash_filter = ? AND is_done = 0 with 30-second intervals and 10-minute timeout. After both mutations report is_done=1, issues OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL to force merge. Only then writes completion record to S3 Object Lock deletion audit log. Idempotent: checks status in deletion_requests before reissuing mutation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR deletion requests processed correctly in ClickHouse; deletion audit log in S3 Object Lock provides immutable compliance record within 30-day SLA

fix_report.action_taken: AWS Glue Schema Registry created with registry name 'therapeutics-events'. Avro schemas registered for engagement-events and mood-log-events topics with BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE compatibility setting (new optional fields allowed; field removals and type changes rejected). Spark jobs updated to use GlueSchemaRegistryAvroDeserializer; deserialization failures route to DLQ topic with schema_version metadata for debugging. App clients use GlueSchemaRegistryAvroSerializer; schema registration gated in CI before any topic publish changes.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Zero Spark job failures from schema evolution; BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE policy prevents breaking changes from reaching production

fix_report.action_taken: EngagementAggregator Spark job loads suppressed_users from PostgreSQL as a broadcast DataFrame, refreshed every 60 seconds using foreachBatch streaming trigger. In foreachBatch, micro-batch DataFrame is left-anti-joined with suppressed_users broadcast before any ClickHouse write — opted-out user events are dropped at this point and counted in a SuppressedEventsCount CloudWatch metric.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CCPA-compliant pre-aggregation suppression; suppressed user events never written to ClickHouse analytics tables

target_output.title: Mental Health Digital Therapeutics Engagement Event Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A high-throughput Kafka + Spark Structured Streaming + ClickHouse data pipeline processing 50M+ daily engagement events from a mental health digital therapeutics platform, feeding near-real-time product analytics and personalization feature vectors. GDPR deletion workflows use ClickHouse ALTER TABLE mutations with completion tracking and S3 Object Lock audit logs. CCPA opt-out suppression is enforced in the Spark processing layer before any analytics writes. AWS Glue Schema Registry with Avro schemas and BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE evolution policy protects streaming jobs from upstream event schema changes.
target_output.architecture_overview: App clients serialize engagement events to Avro using the AWS Glue Schema Registry and publish to MSK Kafka engagement-events and mood-log-events topics (16 partitions each). Three Spark Structured Streaming jobs run on EMR Serverless: EngagementAggregator computes 5-minute tumbling window aggregates per user per module and writes to ClickHouse; MoodLogProcessor validates and enriches mood events before writing to ClickHouse mood_log_facts; FeatureVectorJob runs daily as a batch Spark job computing per-user engagement feature vectors to PostgreSQL user_features for the recommendation service. CCPA-suppressed users are filtered via broadcast join before any ClickHouse write. GDPR deletion requests are processed by DeletionWorker using ClickHouse ALTER TABLE mutations with system.mutations polling. S3 Object Lock bucket provides immutable GDPR deletion audit trail.
target_output.technology_stack: Event streaming: Apache Kafka on AWS MSK (3-broker, 16 partitions). Schema registry: AWS Glue Schema Registry with Avro, BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE policy. Stream processing: Spark Structured Streaming on EMR Serverless (PySpark 3.5). Analytics store: ClickHouse (2-node cluster on c6a.4xlarge EC2, local NVMe, ReplacingMergeTree and MergeTree engines). Feature store: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. Deletion audit: S3 Object Lock (COMPLIANCE mode). Observability: Grafana + Prometheus (Spark metrics, Kafka consumer lag), Datadog (ClickHouse query perf, RDS). CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Infrastructure: Terraform.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Engagement Event Ingestion: Avro-serialized events from mobile app clients published to MSK with schema validation at publish time. Near-Real-Time Aggregation: 5-minute tumbling window counts per user per module in ClickHouse for product analytics and real-time dashboard freshness. Mood Log Processing: mood_score, energy_level, anxiety_level per user per session stored in ClickHouse for trend analysis. Daily Feature Computation: per-user module_completion_rate, streak length, 7-day mood trend written to PostgreSQL for recommendation service consumption. GDPR Erasure: mutation-based deletion from ClickHouse with system.mutations completion tracking and S3 Object Lock audit log. CCPA Suppression: pre-aggregation broadcast join filtering opted-out users before any analytics writes. Data Quality Monitoring: dead-letter topic for schema violations; row count and latency alerts.
target_output.system_components: MSK Kafka cluster: engagement-events (16 partitions, 7-day retention, Avro+Glue registry), mood-log-events (8 partitions), engagement-events-dlq (schema failure sink). EMR Serverless Spark application: EngagementAggregator (streaming, 5-min trigger), MoodLogProcessor (streaming), FeatureVectorJob (daily batch, 03:00 UTC). ClickHouse 2-node cluster: engagement_aggregates (ReplacingMergeTree ORDER BY (user_id_hash, module_id, event_date)), mood_log_facts (MergeTree TTL 2 years). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: user_features (recommendation service reads), deletion_requests, suppressed_users. S3 buckets: spark-checkpoints (versioned), dlq-archive (7-day lifecycle), deletion-audit-log (Object Lock COMPLIANCE 7-year retention). AWS Glue Schema Registry: schemas for both Kafka topics. DeletionWorker: ECS Fargate single-task, polls deletion_requests hourly.
target_output.workflows: Event Ingestion: mobile app serializes engagement event to Avro via Glue Schema Registry SDK, publishes to MSK engagement-events. MSK accepts message; schema version embedded in Avro wire format. Near-Real-Time Aggregation: EngagementAggregator Spark job reads from MSK, loads suppressed_users broadcast from PostgreSQL (refreshed every 60 seconds), left-anti-joins batch to filter opted-out users, computes 5-minute tumbling window GROUP BY (user_id_hash, module_id, event_type) counts, writes to ClickHouse engagement_aggregates via JDBC async insert. Schema Failure Handling: records failing Avro deserialization routed to engagement-events-dlq topic; DLQ consumer archives to S3 with schema_version metadata for debugging. GDPR Deletion: deletion_requests row inserted (user_id_hash, status=pending); DeletionWorker picks up pending requests; issues ALTER TABLE engagement_aggregates DELETE WHERE user_id_hash = ? via ClickHouse HTTP API; polls system.mutations is_done every 30 seconds up to 10 minutes; issues OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL; issues same mutation on mood_log_facts; DELETEs PostgreSQL user_features row; updates deletion_requests status=completed; writes S3 Object Lock JSON record (request_id, user_id_hash, tables_affected, records_deleted_count, completed_at). Daily Feature Batch: FeatureVectorJob reads engagement_aggregates and mood_log_facts from ClickHouse for last 30 days, computes per-user vectors, upserts to PostgreSQL user_features via INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE.
target_output.data_model: engagement_events Kafka Avro schema (user_id STRING, event_type ENUM, module_id STRING, session_id STRING, client_timestamp LONG millis, app_version STRING, platform ENUM ios/android). engagement_aggregates ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree (user_id_hash String, module_id String, event_date Date, event_type String, event_count UInt32, last_event_at DateTime, _version UInt64, ORDER BY (user_id_hash, module_id, event_date, event_type)). mood_log_facts ClickHouse MergeTree (user_id_hash String, logged_at DateTime, mood_score Int8, energy_level Int8, anxiety_level Int8, TTL logged_at + INTERVAL 2 YEAR). user_features PostgreSQL (user_id_hash VARCHAR PK, module_completion_rate NUMERIC(5,4), current_streak_days INT, mood_trend_7d NUMERIC(5,2), last_computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). deletion_requests PostgreSQL (id UUID PK, user_id_hash VARCHAR, requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, status VARCHAR, records_deleted_count INT, tables_affected JSONB). suppressed_users PostgreSQL (user_id_hash VARCHAR PK, opt_out_type ENUM gdpr/ccpa, opted_out_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: No external-facing REST API. Internal interfaces: MSK Kafka producer API (app clients via Avro+Glue registry). ClickHouse HTTP API: used by Spark JDBC connector and DeletionWorker (POST /query with SQL). PostgreSQL: standard JDBC for DeletionWorker and FeatureVectorJob. DeletionWorker internal endpoint: POST /deletions (accept deletion request, enqueue in deletion_requests table) — callable by user account service. EMR Serverless: AWS SDK StartJobRun API for FeatureVectorJob daily trigger via EventBridge.
target_output.security_and_compliance: MSK TLS in transit with IAM authentication; Kafka ACLs restrict topic produce/consume to designated IAM roles. ClickHouse and PostgreSQL EBS volumes encrypted with KMS CMKs. user_id hashed with HMAC-SHA256 (per-deployment secret in Secrets Manager) before writing to ClickHouse or PostgreSQL; original user_id never persisted downstream of Kafka. GDPR deletion audit: S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode (7-year retention); deletion records immutable; DeletionWorker cannot overwrite. CCPA suppression: pre-aggregation broadcast join in Spark; SuppressedEventsCount metric published to CloudWatch for compliance reporting. Secrets rotation: Secrets Manager 90-day rotation for ClickHouse password, RDS credentials, HMAC key. CloudTrail: all S3, RDS, MSK API actions logged; retention 2 years.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages MSK cluster, EMR Serverless application, ClickHouse EC2 instances, RDS, S3 buckets (with Object Lock config), Glue Schema Registry, ECS task for DeletionWorker. GitHub Actions CI: ruff, mypy, pytest (PySpark unit tests with pyspark.testing LocalRelationalGroupedDataset, ClickHouse testcontainers, PostgreSQL testcontainers), Docker build, ECR push, EMR job definition update. Staging environment: separate MSK cluster + EMR application consuming synthetic event generator (generates 500K events/day matching production Avro schema). Production EMR Serverless auto-scales worker nodes to handle event volume spikes (mental wellness campaigns). Spark checkpoints in S3 versioned bucket enable streaming job restart from last committed offset. ClickHouse schema migrations: non-destructive ADD COLUMN only in CI gate; DROP COLUMN requires 30-day deprecation with metrics showing zero reads on column.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics scraped from Spark EMR job executor: KafkaConsumerLag per topic-partition (alert if lag >100K for 5 minutes), EngagementAggregatorBatchDuration (alert if >4 minutes), SuppressedEventsCount per 5-minute window. Datadog ClickHouse integration: query_duration_ms p99 (alert if >2s), MergeTreeMutationsPending count (alert if >10 pending mutations for >30 minutes — signals GDPR deletion backlog). Grafana dashboard: real-time Kafka lag, Spark micro-batch latency, ClickHouse insert rate, daily deletion request SLA compliance (requests completed within 30 days). PagerDuty: MoodLogProcessor consumer lag >50K messages; DeletionWorker failure two consecutive polls.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $9,800/month at 50M daily events: MSK 3-broker (kafka.m5.large) $1,200/month, EMR Serverless auto-scaling Spark (peak 40 vCPU hours/day) $3,100/month, ClickHouse EC2 c6a.4xlarge x2 + NVMe storage $2,800/month, RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.large $280/month, S3 storage + requests $420/month, DeletionWorker ECS Fargate $50/month. Under $12,000 budget with 18% headroom. EMR Serverless scales to zero outside peak hours; ClickHouse c6a Spot pricing available for replica node saving ~$700/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): MSK cluster, Glue Schema Registry with Avro schemas, EngagementAggregator Spark job with CCPA broadcast suppression, MoodLogProcessor, DLQ consumer, ClickHouse cluster setup, PostgreSQL user_features table, DeletionWorker with ALTER TABLE mutation + S3 audit log, GitHub Actions CI, Terraform IaC, Grafana/Datadog observability. Phase 2 (8 weeks): FeatureVectorJob daily batch with user_features upsert, recommendation service integration testing, A/B experiment event tracking schema additions (BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE), ClickHouse TTL policy enforcement validation, GDPR deletion SLA dashboard. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Real-time feature serving API from ClickHouse for sub-second personalization latency, cross-platform event schema registry governance process, Kafka topic compaction for user_preferences state topic.
target_output.development_guidelines: Spark jobs must be idempotent: S3 checkpoint prevents double-processing; ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree deduplicates on re-delivery. Avro schema changes must include schema_evolution_test.py validating BACKWARD_COMPATIBLE deserialization before merge. ClickHouse ALTER TABLE mutations must be tested on staging with synthetic data before production. user_id must never appear in logs, metrics labels, or error messages — only user_id_hash. DeletionWorker: all deletion requests tested against ClickHouse testcontainer in CI to verify mutation completion logic.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ClickHouse OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL for GDPR deletions is expensive on large tables (full table merge). Mitigation: schedule FINAL compaction during off-peak hours (02:00-04:00 UTC); use partition-pruning by deleting from specific date partitions to reduce compaction scope. Risk: Glue Schema Registry adds 3-5ms per message for schema lookup on first publish per schema version. Mitigation: Glue caches schema locally in SDK; cold-start penalty only on first event per schema version per JVM instance. Tradeoff: ClickHouse on EC2 vs ClickHouse Cloud — EC2 chosen for cost control; ClickHouse Cloud would simplify operations at ~$1,500/month higher cost. Risk: EMR Serverless cold-start latency (30-60s) can delay micro-batch processing at low traffic windows. Mitigation: keep-warm configuration for EngagementAggregator streaming job (persistent executor pre-initialization).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected AWS Glue Schema Registry over Confluent Schema Registry to stay within AWS service boundary and avoid additional vendor contract. Confirmed ClickHouse on EC2 over ClickHouse Cloud for cost predictability within $12,000/month budget. Chose ALTER TABLE mutations for GDPR deletion over a tombstone+filter approach because regulatory auditors require actual data removal, not logical deletion.

metadata.notes: Round 2 closes ClickHouse GDPR mutation incompatibility (ISS-001), missing Kafka schema registry (ISS-002), and CCPA pre-aggregation suppression (ISS-003); data_pipeline with no frontend specialists.

profile.domain: Speech Prosody Depression Biomarker Research Prototype

project_goal.value: Research prototype for real-time speech prosody analysis as passive biomarker for depression severity, extracting acoustic features from therapy session audio and correlating with HDRS clinician-rated depression scales to validate the approach for future clinical application
project_goal.rationale: Acoustic biomarkers may enable objective, non-invasive depression severity tracking; research prototype validates feature-outcome correlation before clinical study

target_users.value: Research team (computational linguists, clinical researchers) who upload recordings, run analysis pipelines, and explore correlations via Streamlit dashboard
target_users.rationale: Internal research team only; no clinical staff or patients access the prototype directly

project_class.rationale: Exploratory research system with non-production reliability expectations; Streamlit dashboard and GPU pipeline on self-hosted bare-metal

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Streamlit frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL + DuckDB, LLM-assisted feature interpretation, GPU batch processing, Grafana analytics

complexity_level.rationale: GPU-accelerated audio feature extraction pipeline, LLM integration, statistical correlation analysis, multi-modal data (audio, clinical ratings)

risk_level.rationale: Audio recordings of therapy sessions are highly sensitive PHI; IRB protocols and data use agreements govern all handling

data_sensitivity.rationale: Therapy session audio recordings are PHI even when de-identified at the transcript level due to voice re-identification risk

external_exposure.rationale: Research prototype accessible only on institutional VPN; no internet-facing endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT for research team (3-5 researchers); no RBAC complexity; all team members have full access to all prototype functions
access_model.rationale: Small research team; access control is network-level (VPN) supplemented by application-level JWT

feature_scope.value: Recording upload and storage, GPU-accelerated prosody feature extraction (pitch, speech rate, pause duration, jitter, shimmer), correlation analysis with HDRS ratings, LLM-assisted feature interpretation, Streamlit exploration dashboard, model experiment tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Full research workflow from raw audio to publication-ready statistical findings

mvp_scope.value: Recording upload, librosa-based feature extraction pipeline, PostgreSQL feature storage, correlation analysis between acoustic features and HDRS ratings, basic Streamlit dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Core hypothesis validation requires feature extraction and correlation; LLM interpretation is phase 2

security_baseline.value: Audio files stored with AES-256 encryption at rest; access restricted to VPN + JWT; no cloud storage of audio files; IRB data management plan compliance
security_baseline.rationale: PHI audio files must not leave the institutional GPU server; IRB prohibits cloud storage of session recordings

frontend_stack.value: Streamlit (research UI)
frontend_stack.rationale: Streamlit rapid prototyping appropriate for research tool; no production UI requirements

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for audio upload endpoint and pipeline trigger; Python data science ecosystem for librosa and scipy analysis

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (feature store) + DuckDB (local analysis)
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for persistent feature and experiment storage; DuckDB for fast in-memory analytical queries on research datasets

hosting_target.value: Hetzner bare-metal GPU server (self-hosted, on-premise institutional network)
hosting_target.rationale: IRB prohibits PHI audio files on public cloud; institutional GPU server with NVIDIA A100 meets librosa GPU acceleration requirements

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audio recordings retained only during active IRB study period (maximum 3 years); extracted acoustic features retained 5 years for publication replication; HDRS rating data retained 5 years; recordings deleted upon IRB study closure or participant withdrawal
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IRB data management plan and institutional records retention policy

future_scope.value: Real-time prosody monitoring during live therapy sessions, multi-modal fusion with facial action unit features, clinical validation study with larger patient cohort
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 clinical validation pending prototype findings

constraints.value: Audio files must not leave institutional GPU server; no cloud APIs for audio processing; IRB protocol limits dataset to 120 de-identified session recordings from 40 participants; publication of findings requires model explainability
constraints.rationale: IRB and institutional security restrictions; research integrity requirements

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for GPU utilization and pipeline job duration; no external monitoring SaaS
observability_baseline.rationale: Self-hosted monitoring appropriate for bare-metal research server; no budget for external APM

execution_preference.value: Iterative research sprints; prototype-first with no production deployment obligations
execution_preference.rationale: Research prototype lifecycle; shipping findings matters more than operational polish

compliance_context.value: HIPAA for therapy session audio recordings (PHI even de-identified at transcript level due to voice re-identification); IRB approval from institutional review board; research data use agreement with participating clinics; institutional data governance policy restricts data to on-premise server
compliance_context.rationale: Audio recordings of therapy sessions are among the most sensitive PHI categories; HIPAA, IRB, and institutional controls all apply

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o API for LLM-assisted interpretation of prosodic feature clusters; prompts contain statistical summaries and feature correlation coefficients only — no audio content, transcript text, or participant identifiers; outputs are natural language interpretations of correlation patterns for research reports
llm_integration.rationale: LLM accelerates qualitative interpretation of statistical findings; no PHI in prompts maintains IRB compliance

project.goal: Validate speech prosody acoustic features as passive biomarkers for depression severity in therapy sessions
project.target_users: Research team (3-5 computational linguists and clinical researchers)

frontend.approach: Rapid prototyping dashboard for feature exploration and correlation visualization

backend.api: Internal upload endpoint and pipeline trigger

security.transport: HTTPS on VPN; no public internet exposure

devops.environments: single shared research environment

constraints.irb: data use agreement with participating clinics

confirmed_decisions.llm: OpenAI GPT-4o (statistical summaries only, no PHI)

reasoner_reviews.product: Core hypothesis validation requires clean correlation methodology between acoustic features and HDRS ratings. 120 recordings is a small dataset for statistical significance; researchers should plan for bootstrap confidence intervals and effect size reporting alongside p-values for publication credibility.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Streamlit + FastAPI + PostgreSQL on bare-metal GPU is appropriate for a research prototype at this scale. The LLM integration using OpenAI API is fine since only statistical summaries enter prompts. However, the plan as written fails to address audio file encryption at rest on the filesystem level — GPU server OS disk encryption must be specified to satisfy HIPAA controls for PHI storage.
reasoner_reviews.security: The current JWT implementation described uses a static hardcoded secret — this is a critical flaw for a system holding HIPAA-protected audio recordings. JWT secret must be stored in an environment variable loaded from a secrets file outside the repository. Additionally, audit logging for audio file access is required by HIPAA — currently absent from the plan.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: DuckDB for local analysis is a good choice for the small dataset size; in-memory query performance on 120 recordings is excellent. GitHub Actions CI on bare-metal requires a self-hosted runner configuration.

frontend.app_structure: Streamlit multi-page app: pages/upload.py (recording upload + participant metadata entry), pages/features.py (acoustic feature visualization per recording), pages/correlations.py (scatter plots, heatmaps of feature-HDRS correlations), pages/experiments.py (model experiment comparison table), pages/interpretations.py (LLM-generated narrative interpretations).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Upload flow: researcher uploads WAV file + enters participant_id, session_date, hdrs_score; pipeline triggered on upload. Feature explorer: select recording, view pitch contour, speech rate timeline, pause distribution visualizations. Correlation dashboard: select acoustic feature vs HDRS rating, view Pearson r, p-value, bootstrap CI, scatter plot. LLM interpretation: select feature cluster, request GPT-4o interpretation, view and save narrative.
frontend.performance_notes: Streamlit caching (@st.cache_data) on DuckDB queries for feature aggregation; large WAV file uploads via chunked multipart to FastAPI; GPU pipeline status polled via 5-second Streamlit rerun.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app: /upload endpoint receives WAV file + metadata, stores encrypted on local filesystem, enqueues extraction task in a Python multiprocessing queue. GPU extraction worker process runs librosa feature extraction (pitch via librosa.yin, speech rate via syllable nuclei detection, pause detection via VAD, jitter and shimmer via custom STFT analysis). Results written to PostgreSQL acoustic_features.
backend.api_patterns: POST /recordings (multipart: wav_file + participant_id + session_date + hdrs_score). POST /pipelines/run/{recording_id} (trigger extraction). GET /recordings/{recording_id}/features (fetch extracted features). POST /interpretations/generate (body: feature_correlation_summary JSON, calls GPT-4o). GET /experiments (list MLflow experiment runs).
backend.failure_handling: GPU extraction worker writes status=failed to recordings table on exception; Streamlit dashboard shows error with librosa traceback. No retry logic in prototype.

security.auth_design: FastAPI JWT middleware with HS256 algorithm; secret loaded from JWT_SECRET environment variable set in .env file on bare-metal server. Token expiry 24 hours. All Streamlit pages call FastAPI with Bearer token stored in st.session_state.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Python logging to /var/log/prosody-prototype/access.log: recording upload events, feature extraction completions, GPT-4o API calls. Log rotation weekly. No PHI in log entries — only recording_id (UUID) and researcher username.
security.privacy_controls: Audio files stored at /data/recordings/{recording_id}.wav.enc (AES-256-CBC encrypted via Python cryptography library, key in .env). Participant identifiers in PostgreSQL participants table only; acoustic_features table references participant_id FK — no name or clinical identifiers in features table.

data.entities: participants (id UUID PK, study_participant_code VARCHAR UNIQUE — no PII; IRB de-identification applied before entry), session_recordings (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, session_date DATE, hdrs_score INT CHECK(0<=hdrs_score<=52), recording_filepath VARCHAR, encryption_key_id VARCHAR, status ENUM uploaded/extracting/completed/failed, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ), acoustic_features (id UUID PK, recording_id FK, feature_type VARCHAR, feature_value NUMERIC, time_segment_start NUMERIC, time_segment_end NUMERIC, extracted_at TIMESTAMPTZ), prosody_segments (id UUID PK, recording_id FK, segment_start NUMERIC, segment_end NUMERIC, pitch_hz_mean NUMERIC, pitch_hz_std NUMERIC, speech_rate_syl_per_sec NUMERIC, pause_count INT, jitter_pct NUMERIC, shimmer_db NUMERIC), model_experiments (id UUID PK, experiment_name VARCHAR, feature_set JSONB, correlation_results JSONB, statistical_tests JSONB, hdrs_outcome VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ), correlation_results (recording_id FK, feature_type VARCHAR, hdrs_score INT, pearson_r NUMERIC, p_value NUMERIC, bootstrap_ci_lower NUMERIC, bootstrap_ci_upper NUMERIC, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.schema_notes: DuckDB used for ad-hoc analysis queries joining acoustic_features and prosody_segments; DuckDB reads PostgreSQL tables via postgres_scan extension. No separate DuckDB database file — ephemeral in-process queries only.
data.retention_and_deletion: session_recordings and acoustic_features deleted on participant withdrawal request within 48 hours. All data deleted at IRB study closure. audio files (.wav.enc) deleted separately with secure overwrite (shred -u).

devops.deployment_topology: Single Hetzner bare-metal server: NVIDIA A100 GPU, 64GB RAM, 4TB NVMe. Services: FastAPI (uvicorn, port 8000), Streamlit (port 8501), PostgreSQL 16 (port 5432 local only), GPU extraction worker (subprocess). Nginx reverse proxy: HTTPS termination (self-signed cert on institutional VPN), forwards /api/ to FastAPI, /app/ to Streamlit.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions with self-hosted runner on bare-metal: ruff lint, mypy, pytest (unit tests for feature extraction functions, FastAPI routes with pytest-asyncio). No Docker — direct Python venv deployment. Manual deployment: git pull + systemd service restart.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus node_exporter for GPU utilization (nvidia_gpu_utilization_ratio), CPU, memory. Grafana dashboard: GPU utilization during extraction jobs, extraction job duration per recording. No external monitoring SaaS.

target_output.thinking_summary: Research prototype on self-hosted GPU bare-metal for speech prosody analysis; intentional flaws introduced: (1) OpenAI GPT-4o is used for LLM interpretation — but the plan fails to verify that statistical summaries sent to the OpenAI API do not indirectly identify participants through correlation coefficients linked to small study populations; (2) the JWT secret is stored in a .env file on the server filesystem without describing secure permissions or key rotation, creating a PHI access control gap; (3) no mention of HIPAA audit log for recording file access beyond general Python logging, which may not meet the minimum necessary standard for PHI access tracking. These are the intentional flaws for a flawed plan_quality row.
target_output.title: Speech Prosody Depression Biomarker Research Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype for a computational psychiatry team investigating speech prosody as a passive biomarker for depression severity. The system processes de-identified therapy session audio recordings on a HIPAA-restricted Hetzner bare-metal GPU server, extracting acoustic features including pitch variability, speech rate, pause patterns, jitter, and shimmer using librosa and custom STFT analysis. Extracted features are correlated with clinician-administered HDRS depression ratings and explored via a Streamlit dashboard with LLM-assisted interpretation. Note: several HIPAA compliance controls in this plan are insufficiently specified for production and require remediation in subsequent revisions.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI service on port 8000 receives WAV file uploads and metadata via multipart POST, stores encrypted audio files locally on NVMe, and enqueues GPU extraction tasks in a Python multiprocessing queue. The GPU worker process runs librosa feature extraction on NVIDIA A100, computes prosody_segments and acoustic_features, and writes results to PostgreSQL 16. Streamlit dashboard on port 8501 queries PostgreSQL and DuckDB for feature visualization and correlation analysis. LLM interpretation calls the OpenAI GPT-4o API with statistical summaries (no audio or transcript content). Nginx handles HTTPS termination on the institutional VPN. Prometheus and Grafana provide GPU utilization monitoring. IRB data governance restricts all data to this server — no cloud storage of audio files permitted.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Streamlit 1.35. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, uvicorn. Feature extraction: librosa 0.10, scipy, custom STFT jitter/shimmer functions. LLM: OpenAI GPT-4o API (statistical summaries only). Database: PostgreSQL 16 (feature store), DuckDB (ad-hoc analysis via postgres_scan). Encryption: Python cryptography library (AES-256-CBC for audio files). Infrastructure: Hetzner bare-metal GPU server, NVIDIA A100, Nginx, systemd. Monitoring: Prometheus node_exporter, Grafana. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with self-hosted runner.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Audio Ingestion: WAV file upload with participant metadata and HDRS rating via Streamlit upload page. Feature Extraction: GPU-accelerated librosa pipeline producing pitch contours (librosa.yin), speech rate (syllable nuclei algorithm), pause detection (energy-based VAD), jitter (cycle-to-cycle F0 perturbation), and shimmer (amplitude perturbation). Correlation Analysis: Pearson r and bootstrap confidence intervals correlating each acoustic feature with HDRS total score across the 120-recording dataset. Streamlit Dashboard: interactive scatter plots, heatmaps, feature timeline visualizations using Plotly. LLM Interpretation: GPT-4o-generated natural language summaries of correlation patterns for research report drafting. Experiment Tracking: model_experiments table stores feature sets, statistical results, and comparison metadata.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI (uvicorn): recording upload endpoint, pipeline trigger, feature query API, LLM interpretation proxy. GPU Extraction Worker: Python subprocess consuming extraction queue, running librosa on NVIDIA A100. PostgreSQL 16: participants, session_recordings, acoustic_features, prosody_segments, model_experiments, correlation_results tables. DuckDB in-process: ad-hoc joins and aggregations on PostgreSQL data via postgres_scan extension. Encrypted NVMe storage: /data/recordings/ with AES-256-CBC encrypted WAV files. Streamlit: multi-page research dashboard. Nginx: HTTPS reverse proxy (self-signed cert, VPN-only). Prometheus + Grafana: GPU and pipeline monitoring. OpenAI GPT-4o API: LLM interpretation endpoint (FLAW: statistical summaries from a 40-participant study may allow indirect re-identification through unique correlation patterns; IRB review of this data sharing not confirmed in plan).
target_output.workflows: Recording Upload and Extraction: researcher navigates to Streamlit upload page, selects WAV file, enters participant_code, session_date, hdrs_score; Streamlit calls POST /recordings with multipart body; FastAPI validates JWT (FLAW: JWT secret stored in plaintext .env file at /opt/prosody/.env with chmod 644 — accessible to any local process), saves encrypted file to /data/recordings/{uuid}.wav.enc, inserts session_recordings row with status=uploaded, returns recording_id; FastAPI enqueues extraction task; GPU worker picks up task, loads encrypted file, decrypts to RAM (never to disk), runs librosa.yin for pitch, computes speech rate via syllable nuclei, detects pauses via RMS energy threshold, computes jitter and shimmer via STFT, writes prosody_segments and acoustic_features rows, updates status=completed. Correlation Analysis: researcher opens correlations page, selects feature type and participant cohort filter; Streamlit runs DuckDB postgres_scan query joining acoustic_features and session_recordings on recording_id, computes Pearson r, p-value, and 1000-iteration bootstrap CI using scipy.stats; renders Plotly scatter plot with regression line. LLM Interpretation: researcher selects correlation_results for a feature cluster; Streamlit calls POST /interpretations/generate with JSON body containing feature names, Pearson r values, p-values, and bootstrap CIs for the selected cluster (no participant IDs, no audio data); FastAPI constructs prompt and calls OpenAI GPT-4o API (FLAW: no mechanism to verify that combination of specific r values and CIs from a 40-participant study cannot be used to re-identify participants via linkage with published data; IRB sign-off on this API usage not documented in plan); returns narrative interpretation stored in model_experiments.
target_output.data_model: participants (id UUID PK, study_participant_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, enrolled_at DATE, withdrawal_date DATE NULLABLE — IRB de-identification applied; no name, DOB, or clinical identifiers). session_recordings (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, session_date DATE, hdrs_score INT CHECK(0<=hdrs_score<=52), recording_filepath VARCHAR, encryption_key_id VARCHAR, status ENUM uploaded/extracting/completed/failed, file_size_bytes INT, duration_seconds NUMERIC, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, uploaded_by VARCHAR). acoustic_features (id UUID PK, recording_id FK, feature_type VARCHAR, feature_value NUMERIC, time_segment_start NUMERIC, time_segment_end NUMERIC, window_size_ms INT, extracted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). prosody_segments (id UUID PK, recording_id FK, segment_start NUMERIC, segment_end NUMERIC, pitch_hz_mean NUMERIC, pitch_hz_std NUMERIC, speech_rate_syl_per_sec NUMERIC, pause_count INT, pause_total_duration_sec NUMERIC, jitter_pct NUMERIC, shimmer_db NUMERIC, voiced_fraction NUMERIC). model_experiments (id UUID PK, experiment_name VARCHAR, feature_set JSONB, hdrs_outcome VARCHAR, correlation_results JSONB, statistical_tests JSONB, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_by VARCHAR). correlation_results (recording_id FK, feature_type VARCHAR, hdrs_score INT, pearson_r NUMERIC, p_value NUMERIC, bootstrap_ci_lower NUMERIC, bootstrap_ci_upper NUMERIC, effect_size_cohens_d NUMERIC, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /recordings — multipart: wav_file + participant_code + session_date (ISO date) + hdrs_score (int). Returns {recording_id: UUID, status: 'uploaded'}. POST /pipelines/run/{recording_id} — triggers GPU extraction; returns {job_id: UUID}. GET /recordings/{recording_id}/features — returns prosody_segments and acoustic_features for a recording. POST /interpretations/generate — body: {feature_correlations: [{feature_type, pearson_r, p_value, bootstrap_ci_lower, bootstrap_ci_upper}], cohort_description: string}; calls GPT-4o; returns {narrative: string, tokens_used: int}. GET /experiments — returns list of model_experiments with feature_set and correlation_results summaries. All routes require Authorization: Bearer {jwt_token}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Audio file encryption: AES-256-CBC via Python cryptography library; encryption key stored in ENCRYPTION_KEY env var in /opt/prosody/.env. HIPAA control gap: .env file permissions not specified as restrictive; plan does not describe chmod 600 or systemd EnvironmentFile with root-only read access. JWT authentication: HS256 with 24-hour expiry; JWT_SECRET in same .env file. HIPAA audit log gap: Python access.log records recording_id and researcher username for upload events but does not log every read access to acoustic_features or prosody_segments tables, which may not satisfy HIPAA Minimum Necessary access tracking requirements. HIPAA control gap: no description of PostgreSQL row-level access log or pgaudit extension to track PHI read events. LLM API re-identification risk: GPT-4o API called with correlation coefficients from a 40-participant study; no IRB waiver or data processing agreement with OpenAI documented in this plan to cover statistical summaries derived from PHI. Correct controls: VPN-only network exposure, IRB data use agreement with participating clinics, AES-256 audio file encryption, participant de-identification before database entry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Hetzner bare-metal deployment: Python 3.12 venv at /opt/prosody/venv; systemd units: prosody-api.service (FastAPI uvicorn), prosody-streamlit.service (Streamlit), prosody-worker.service (GPU extraction worker). Nginx reverse proxy at /opt/nginx/sites/prosody.conf: location /api/ proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000; location /app/ proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8501; SSL with institutional self-signed cert. GitHub Actions self-hosted runner on bare-metal: ruff, mypy, pytest (unit tests for librosa wrapper functions with 10-second synthetic WAV fixtures, FastAPI routes with TestClient). Deployment: git pull origin main on server, systemctl restart prosody-api prosody-streamlit prosody-worker. No Docker or container orchestration — direct venv deployment appropriate for single-server research prototype. Backup: PostgreSQL pg_dump to /data/backups/ nightly via cron; /data/recordings/ backed up to encrypted external drive weekly by research coordinator.
target_output.observability: Prometheus node_exporter scrapes GPU utilization (nvidia_smi via node_exporter textfile collector), CPU, memory, NVMe I/O. Grafana dashboard: GPU utilization per extraction job (target: A100 >60% during extraction), extraction queue depth, job duration histogram (alert if p50 >90 seconds for a 30-minute recording). Python logging: FastAPI request log with recording_id, researcher_username, endpoint, response_time (no PHI values). GPU worker: logs extraction_start, feature_types_computed, extraction_duration_seconds, status. No external monitoring SaaS — Grafana and Prometheus self-hosted on same bare-metal server.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Infrastructure cost: Hetzner bare-metal GPU server ~$800/month (AX102 with A100). OpenAI GPT-4o API: estimated 50 interpretation calls/month at ~$0.03/call = ~$1.50/month. GitHub Actions: self-hosted runner, no metered cost. PostgreSQL storage: 120 recordings with features = ~5GB data, negligible. Encrypted audio NVMe: 120 recordings x avg 50MB = 6GB, within 4TB capacity. Total: ~$802/month for active study period. System does not need to scale beyond the 40-participant IRB dataset; if future clinical study scales to 500 participants, ClickHouse would replace DuckDB/PostgreSQL for feature analytics.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Hetzner server setup, CUDA + librosa environment, FastAPI upload and pipeline trigger, GPU extraction worker with pitch/speech rate/pause extraction, PostgreSQL schema, basic Streamlit upload and feature visualization pages, JWT auth, Prometheus+Grafana. Phase 2 (6 weeks): jitter and shimmer extraction via STFT, correlation analysis with bootstrap CIs, Pearson r computation, Streamlit correlation dashboard with Plotly, DuckDB postgres_scan integration, model_experiments tracking. Phase 3 (4 weeks): LLM interpretation via GPT-4o (pending IRB sign-off on statistical summary sharing), publication export feature (LaTeX table generation from correlation_results), dataset release package preparation with de-identification audit.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict type annotations; mypy enforced in CI. librosa wrapper functions must be unit-tested with synthetic WAV fixtures (tone generation via scipy.io.wavfile). Never log PHI values — only UUIDs and researcher usernames in log files. Audio files must be decrypted to RAM only (tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile with no disk spill). DuckDB queries must not JOIN on participant name or DOB fields. Model experiment results must include effect size (Cohen's d) alongside p-values to prevent publication of underpowered findings.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OpenAI GPT-4o API usage with correlation statistics from 40-participant study may constitute sharing of PHI-derived data under HIPAA; IRB and institutional privacy office must review before LLM feature activates. Mitigation: gate LLM interpretation behind an accepted_exceptions flag until IRB approval obtained — currently not done in this plan (intentional flaw). Risk: .env file containing JWT_SECRET and ENCRYPTION_KEY stored on server filesystem; if server is compromised, both PHI access key and encryption key are exposed. Mitigation: use systemd EnvironmentFile with chmod 400 root:root ownership — not specified in current plan (intentional flaw). Risk: no pgaudit extension specified for PostgreSQL; HIPAA requires audit trail for all PHI access events, not just application-level upload logs. Mitigation: enable pgaudit with log_level=LOG for SELECT on session_recordings and acoustic_features — absent from current plan (intentional flaw). Tradeoff: direct venv deployment over Docker simplifies GPU access on bare-metal but makes environment reproducibility dependent on manual setup documentation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected OpenAI GPT-4o over self-hosted LLM (Ollama/Llama) for interpretation quality; IRB approval for API usage with statistical summaries is pending and noted as an open compliance item. Chose DuckDB over a separate ClickHouse instance for ad-hoc analysis given the small 120-recording dataset. Selected AES-256-CBC over age-based encryption for simpler Python implementation at prototype stage.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: .env file permission not hardened, PHI access audit logging absent from plan, IRB/OpenAI DPA for statistical summaries not resolved; research_prototype with all 5 active specialists including frontend (Streamlit).
